1→# Service Documentation 2→ 3→Comprehensive guide to all services in the application. Services manage business logic, state, and side effects. 4→ 5→## Core Services 6→ 7→### authService 8→ 9→**Location:** `src/services/authService.ts` 10→ 11→**Purpose:** Manages user authentication state, token storage, and Sentry user tracking. 12→ 13→**Methods:** 14→ 15→#### `initialize()` 16→ 17→Initializes auth state on app startup by loading token from localStorage. 18→ 19→```typescript 20→authService.initialize(); 21→``` 22→ 23→**Behavior:** 24→ 25→- Reads `ACCESS_TOKEN` from localStorage 26→- If token exists, updates global auth state 27→- Sets Sentry user from token for error tracking 28→ 29→**When to call:** App initialization (in root component or App.tsx) 30→ 31→--- 32→ 33→#### `login(token: string)` 34→ 35→Stores auth token and updates global state after successful login. 36→ 37→```typescript 38→authService.login('eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...'); 39→``` 40→ 41→**Behavior:** 42→ 43→- Stores token in localStorage 44→- Updates global `AUTH_TOKEN` state 45→- Sets Sentry user from token 46→ 47→**When to call:** After successful login API response 48→ 49→--- 50→ 51→#### `logout()` 52→ 53→Clears auth state and cached data on logout. 54→ 55→```typescript 56→authService.logout(); 57→``` 58→ 59→**Behavior:** 60→ 61→- Removes token from localStorage 62→- Clears global `AUTH_TOKEN` state 63→- Clears all React Query cache 64→- Clears Sentry user data 65→ 66→**When to call:** User logout, session expiry, or unauthorized errors 67→ 68→--- 69→ 70→**Dependencies:** 71→ 72→- `localStorageService` - Token persistence 73→- `globalStore` - Auth state management 74→- `queryClient` - Cache clearing 75→- `sentryHelpers` - Error tracking 76→ 77→**Usage Example:** 78→ 79→```typescript 80→// On app start 81→useEffect(() => { 82→ authService.initialize(); 83→}, []); 84→ 85→// After login 86→const handleLogin = async (email: string, password: string) => { 87→ const { token } = await loginApi(email, password); 88→ authService.login(token); 89→ navigate('/dashboard'); 90→}; 91→ 92→// On logout 93→const handleLogout = () => { 94→ authService.logout(); 95→ navigate('/login'); 96→}; 97→``` 98→ 99→--- 100→ 101→### modalService 102→ 103→**Location:** `src/services/modalService.ts` 104→ 105→**Purpose:** Manages modal state globally using signals. See [PATTERNS.md](./PATTERNS.md#1-modal-pattern) for detailed pattern documentation. 106→ 107→**Methods:** 108→ 109→#### `open(modalName, props?)` 110→ 111→Opens a modal with optional typed props. 112→ 113→```typescript 114→modalService.open(ModalNameE.TRANSACTION, { transactionId: 'uuid' }); 115→``` 116→ 117→**Parameters:** 118→ 119→- `modalName` - Modal identifier from `ModalNameE` 120→- `props` - Optional props (type-safe based on modal name) 121→ 122→**Returns:** `modalName` 123→ 124→**Behavior:** 125→ 126→- Adds modal to top of stack 127→- Props are type-checked against `ModalPropsT` 128→ 129→--- 130→ 131→#### `close(modalName: ModalNameE)` 132→ 133→Closes a specific modal. 134→ 135→```typescript 136→modalService.close(ModalNameE.TRANSACTION); 137→``` 138→ 139→**Behavior:** 140→ 141→- Removes modal from global state 142→- Modal component detects removal and closes 143→ 144→--- 145→ 146→**Type Safety:** 147→ 148→```typescript 149→// ModalNameE defines available modals 150→export enum ModalNameE { 151→ TRANSACTION = 'transaction', 152→} 153→ 154→// ModalPropsT defines props for each modal 155→export type ModalPropsT = { 156→ [ModalNameE.TRANSACTION]: { transactionId: UuidT }; 157→}; 158→ 159→// TypeScript enforces correct props 160→modalService.open(ModalNameE.TRANSACTION, { transactionId: 'uuid' }); // ✅ 161→modalService.open(ModalNameE.TRANSACTION, { id: 'uuid' }); // ❌ Type error 162→``` 163→ 164→**Dependencies:** `globalStore`, `createGlobalValueSetter` 165→ 166→--- 167→ 168→### sidebarService 169→ 170→**Location:** `src/services/sidebarService.ts` 171→ 172→**Purpose:** Manages sidebar state with stacking and graceful animations. See [PATTERNS.md](./PATTERNS.md#2-sidebar-pattern) for detailed pattern documentation. 173→ 174→**Methods:** 175→ 176→#### `open(sidebarName, props?)` 177→ 178→Opens sidebar and adds to stack. Removes duplicate if already open. 179→ 180→```typescript 181→sidebarService.open(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET, { budgetId: 'uuid' }); 182→``` 183→ 184→**Behavior:** 185→ 186→- If sidebar already in stack, removes old instance 187→- Adds new instance to top of stack 188→- Multiple different sidebars can be open simultaneously 189→ 190→--- 191→ 192→#### `openFirst(sidebarName, props?)` 193→ 194→Opens sidebar and clears entire stack first. 195→ 196→```typescript 197→sidebarService.openFirst(SidebarNameE.CREATE_TRANSACTION); 198→``` 199→ 200→**Behavior:** 201→ 202→- Clears all open sidebars 203→- Opens only the specified sidebar 204→- Use when you want a clean state 205→ 206→--- 207→ 208→#### `close(sidebarName: SidebarNameE)` 209→ 210→Marks sidebar as deleted (triggers close animation). 211→ 212→```typescript 213→sidebarService.close(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET); 214→``` 215→ 216→**Behavior:** 217→ 218→- Sets `deleted: true` on sidebar 219→- Sidebar component detects change and plays close animation 220→- After animation, `finalizeClose()` removes from state 221→ 222→--- 223→ 224→#### `closeAll()` 225→ 226→Marks all open sidebars as deleted. 227→ 228→```typescript 229→sidebarService.closeAll(); 230→``` 231→ 232→**Behavior:** 233→ 234→- Sets `deleted: true` on all sidebars 235→- All sidebars animate closed simultaneously 236→ 237→--- 238→ 239→#### `finalizeClose(sidebarName: SidebarNameE)` 240→ 241→Removes sidebar from state after animation completes. 242→ 243→```typescript 244→sidebarService.finalizeClose(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET); 245→``` 246→ 247→**Behavior:** 248→ 249→- Removes sidebar from global state 250→- **Called automatically by Sidebar component** after animation ends 251→- **Do not call manually** unless you have a specific reason 252→ 253→--- 254→ 255→**Type Safety:** 256→ 257→```typescript 258→// SidebarNameE defines available sidebars 259→export enum SidebarNameE { 260→ EDIT_BUDGET = 'editBudget', 261→ CREATE_TRANSACTION = 'createTransaction', 262→} 263→ 264→// SidebarPropsT defines props for each sidebar 265→export type SidebarPropsT = { 266→ [SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET]: { budgetId: UuidT }; 267→ [SidebarNameE.CREATE_TRANSACTION]: void; 268→}; 269→ 270→// TypeScript enforces correct props 271→sidebarService.open(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET, { budgetId: 'uuid' }); // ✅ 272→sidebarService.open(SidebarNameE.CREATE_TRANSACTION); // ✅ 273→sidebarService.open(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET); // ❌ Missing required props 274→``` 275→ 276→**Dependencies:** `globalStore`, `createGlobalValueSetter` 277→ 278→--- 279→ 280→### notificationService 281→ 282→**Location:** `src/services/notificationService.ts` 283→ 284→**Purpose:** Manages toast notifications with auto-dismiss timers. 285→ 286→**Methods:** 287→ 288→#### `addAlert(content: ReactNode, showTime?: number)` 289→ 290→Adds an info notification. 291→ 292→```typescript 293→notificationService.addAlert('Budget saved successfully!'); 294→notificationService.addAlert(
Custom content
, 3000); 295→``` 296→ 297→**Parameters:** 298→ 299→- `content` - Notification message (string or React element) 300→- `showTime` - Duration in milliseconds (default: 5000ms) 301→ 302→**Returns:** `notificationId` (number) 303→ 304→**Behavior:** 305→ 306→- Adds notification to global state 307→- Auto-increments ID 308→- Sets type to `NotificationTypeE.INFO` 309→ 310→--- 311→ 312→#### `removeAlert(id: number)` 313→ 314→Marks notification for removal (starts hide animation). 315→ 316→```typescript 317→notificationService.removeAlert(notificationId); 318→``` 319→ 320→**Behavior:** 321→ 322→- Sets `hideAt` timestamp 323→- Notification component detects and animates out 324→ 325→--- 326→ 327→#### `clearAlert(id: number)` 328→ 329→Marks notification as deleted and cleans up store. 330→ 331→```typescript 332→notificationService.clearAlert(notificationId); 333→``` 334→ 335→**Behavior:** 336→ 337→- Sets `deleted: true` 338→- Calls `clearStore()` to remove all deleted notifications 339→ 340→--- 341→ 342→#### `clearStore()` 343→ 344→Removes all deleted notifications from state. 345→ 346→```typescript 347→notificationService.clearStore(); 348→``` 349→ 350→**Behavior:** 351→ 352→- If no non-deleted notifications remain, clears entire array 353→- Prevents memory leaks from accumulating notifications 354→ 355→--- 356→ 357→#### `addErrorAlert(message: string)` 358→ 359→Logs error to console. 360→ 361→```typescript 362→notificationService.addErrorAlert('Failed to save budget'); 363→``` 364→ 365→**Note:** Currently only logs to console. Future implementation may show error toast. 366→ 367→--- 368→ 369→**Usage Example:** 370→ 371→```typescript 372→// Success notification 373→const handleSave = async () => { 374→ await saveBudget(); 375→ notificationService.addAlert('Budget saved!'); 376→}; 377→ 378→// Custom duration 379→const handleDelete = async () => { 380→ await deleteBudget(); 381→ notificationService.addAlert('Budget deleted', 3000); 382→}; 383→ 384→// Manual control 385→const notificationId = notificationService.addAlert('Processing...'); 386→// ... later 387→notificationService.removeAlert(notificationId); 388→``` 389→ 390→**Dependencies:** `globalStore`, `createGlobalValueSetter` 391→ 392→--- 393→ 394→### scrollLockService 395→ 396→**Location:** `src/services/scrollLockService.ts` 397→ 398→**Purpose:** Prevents body scroll when modals or sidebars are open. Uses reference counting to support multiple locks. 399→ 400→**Methods:** 401→ 402→#### `scrollLockManager.add(source: string)` 403→ 404→Adds a scroll lock from a specific source. 405→ 406→```typescript 407→scrollLockManager.add('my-sidebar'); 408→``` 409→ 410→**Behavior:** 411→ 412→- Adds source to internal Set 413→- If first lock, adds `modal-open` class to `` 414→- `modal-open` class applies `overflow: hidden` via CSS 415→ 416→--- 417→ 418→#### `scrollLockManager.remove(source: string)` 419→ 420→Removes a scroll lock from a specific source. 421→ 422→```typescript 423→scrollLockManager.remove('my-sidebar'); 424→``` 425→ 426→**Behavior:** 427→ 428→- Removes source from internal Set 429→- If last lock removed, removes `modal-open` class from `` 430→- Body scroll is restored 431→ 432→--- 433→ 434→**How It Works:** 435→ 436→```typescript 437→// Multiple sources can lock scroll 438→scrollLockManager.add('modal-1'); // Body locked 439→scrollLockManager.add('modal-2'); // Still locked 440→scrollLockManager.remove('modal-1'); // Still locked (modal-2 active) 441→scrollLockManager.remove('modal-2'); // Body unlocked 442→``` 443→ 444→**Usage Example:** 445→ 446→```typescript 447→// In Sidebar component 448→useEffect(() => { 449→ if (isOpen) { 450→ scrollLockManager.add('my-sidebar'); 451→ 452→ return () => { 453→ scrollLockManager.remove('my-sidebar'); 454→ }; 455→ } 456→}, [isOpen]); 457→``` 458→ 459→**Note:** Sidebar component automatically manages scroll lock. You rarely need to call this directly. 460→ 461→**Dependencies:** None (pure DOM manipulation) 462→ 463→--- 464→ 465→## AI Assistant Services 466→ 467→### aiAssistantService 468→ 469→**Location:** `src/services/aiAssistant/aiAssistantService.ts` 470→ 471→**Purpose:** Communicates with AI chat backend API. 472→ 473→**Methods:** 474→ 475→#### `sendMessage(message: string, conversationHistory?: AIMessageDTO[])` 476→ 477→Sends a message to AI and returns response. 478→ 479→```typescript 480→const response = await aiAssistantService.sendMessage( 481→ 'How much did I spend on groceries?', 482→ conversationHistory, 483→); 484→``` 485→ 486→**Parameters:** 487→ 488→- `message` - User's message 489→- `conversationHistory` - Previous messages (optional, default: `[]`) 490→ 491→**Returns:** `Promise` 492→ 493→```typescript 494→type AIResponseT = { 495→ message: string; // AI response 496→ conversationId?: string; 497→}; 498→``` 499→ 500→**Error Handling:** 501→ 502→- Timeout (120s): Throws `'AI request timed out. Please try again.'` 503→- Server error (500): Throws `'AI service error. Please try again later.'` 504→- Other errors: Re-throws original error 505→ 506→**Usage Example:** 507→ 508→```typescript 509→try { 510→ const response = await aiAssistantService.sendMessage( 511→ userInput, 512→ previousMessages, 513→ ); 514→ 515→ addMessageToChat(response.message); 516→} catch (error) { 517→ console.error('AI error:', error); 518→ notificationService.addErrorAlert(error.message); 519→} 520→``` 521→ 522→--- 523→ 524→#### `healthCheck()` 525→ 526→Checks AI service health and configuration. 527→ 528→```typescript 529→const health = await aiAssistantService.healthCheck(); 530→``` 531→ 532→**Returns:** 533→ 534→```typescript 535→type HealthResponse = { 536→ status: 'ok' | 'error'; 537→ ollamaConnected: boolean; 538→ model: string; 539→ availableModels?: string[]; 540→ error?: string; 541→}; 542→``` 543→ 544→**Usage Example:** 545→ 546→```typescript 547→const checkAI = async () => { 548→ const health = await aiAssistantService.healthCheck(); 549→ 550→ if (health.status === 'error') { 551→ console.warn('AI unavailable:', health.error); 552→ } else { 553→ console.log('AI model:', health.model); 554→ } 555→}; 556→``` 557→ 558→**Dependencies:** `axiosClient`, `ApiGatewayE` 559→ 560→--- 561→ 562→### aiConversationStorageService 563→ 564→**Location:** `src/services/aiAssistant/aiConversationStorageService.ts` 565→ 566→**Purpose:** Persists AI conversation history to localStorage. 567→ 568→**Methods:** 569→ 570→#### `save(messages: AIMessageT[])` 571→ 572→Saves conversation to localStorage. 573→ 574→```typescript 575→aiConversationStorageService.save(conversationMessages); 576→``` 577→ 578→**Behavior:** 579→ 580→- Serializes messages (converts Date to ISO string) 581→- Stores in localStorage under `AI_CONVERSATION_STORAGE` 582→- Catches and logs errors silently 583→ 584→--- 585→ 586→#### `load()` 587→ 588→Loads conversation from localStorage. 589→ 590→```typescript 591→const messages = aiConversationStorageService.load(); 592→``` 593→ 594→**Returns:** `AIMessageT[]` (empty array if none found) 595→ 596→**Behavior:** 597→ 598→- Deserializes messages (converts ISO string to Date) 599→- Returns empty array on error or missing data 600→ 601→--- 602→ 603→#### `clear()` 604→ 605→Clears conversation from localStorage. 606→ 607→```typescript 608→aiConversationStorageService.clear(); 609→``` 610→ 611→**Behavior:** 612→ 613→- Removes conversation from localStorage 614→- Catches and logs errors silently 615→ 616→--- 617→ 618→**Message Format:** 619→ 620→```typescript 621→type AIMessageT = { 622→ id: string; 623→ role: AIRoleE; // 'user' | 'assistant' 624→ content: string; 625→ timestamp: Date; 626→}; 627→``` 628→ 629→**Usage Example:** 630→ 631→```typescript 632→// Save after each message 633→const handleSendMessage = async (text: string) => { 634→ const newMessage: AIMessageT = { 635→ id: generateId(), 636→ role: AIRoleE.USER, 637→ content: text, 638→ timestamp: new Date(), 639→ }; 640→ 641→ const updatedMessages = [...messages, newMessage]; 642→ aiConversationStorageService.save(updatedMessages); 643→}; 644→ 645→// Load on mount 646→useEffect(() => { 647→ const history = aiConversationStorageService.load(); 648→ setMessages(history); 649→}, []); 650→ 651→// Clear on logout or reset 652→const handleClearChat = () => { 653→ aiConversationStorageService.clear(); 654→ setMessages([]); 655→}; 656→``` 657→ 658→**Dependencies:** `localStorageService` 659→ 660→--- 661→ 662→## Storage Services 663→ 664→### localStorageService 665→ 666→**Location:** `src/services/localStorage/localStorageService.ts` 667→ 668→**Purpose:** Type-safe wrapper for browser localStorage with JSON serialization. 669→ 670→**Methods:** 671→ 672→#### `set(name: T, value: LocalStorageT[T])` 673→ 674→Stores value in localStorage. 675→ 676→```typescript 677→localStorageService.set(LocalStorageNameE.ACCESS_TOKEN, 'token-value'); 678→``` 679→ 680→**Behavior:** 681→ 682→- Serializes value to JSON 683→- Stores in localStorage 684→- Ignores `undefined` and `null` values 685→ 686→--- 687→ 688→#### `get(name: T)` 689→ 690→Retrieves value from localStorage. 691→ 692→```typescript 693→const token = localStorageService.get(LocalStorageNameE.ACCESS_TOKEN); 694→``` 695→ 696→**Returns:** `LocalStorageT[T] | null` 697→ 698→**Behavior:** 699→ 700→- Parses JSON from localStorage 701→- Returns `null` if not found 702→- On parse error, removes corrupted item and returns `null` 703→ 704→--- 705→ 706→#### `remove(name: T)` 707→ 708→Removes value from localStorage. 709→ 710→```typescript 711→localStorageService.remove(LocalStorageNameE.ACCESS_TOKEN); 712→``` 713→ 714→--- 715→ 716→**Type Safety:** 717→ 718→```typescript 719→// LocalStorageNameE defines keys 720→export enum LocalStorageNameE { 721→ ACCESS_TOKEN = 'accessToken', 722→ AI_CONVERSATION_STORAGE = 'aiConversationStorage', 723→} 724→ 725→// LocalStorageT maps keys to value types 726→export type LocalStorageT = { 727→ [LocalStorageNameE.ACCESS_TOKEN]: string; 728→ [LocalStorageNameE.AI_CONVERSATION_STORAGE]: AIMessageStorageT[]; 729→}; 730→ 731→// TypeScript ensures correct types 732→const token: string | null = localStorageService.get( 733→ LocalStorageNameE.ACCESS_TOKEN, 734→); 735→``` 736→ 737→**Usage Example:** 738→ 739→```typescript 740→// Store user preference 741→localStorageService.set(LocalStorageNameE.THEME, 'dark'); 742→ 743→// Retrieve on app start 744→const theme = localStorageService.get(LocalStorageNameE.THEME) || 'light'; 745→ 746→// Clear on logout 747→localStorageService.remove(LocalStorageNameE.USER_PREFERENCES); 748→``` 749→ 750→**Dependencies:** `jsonHelpers` (custom JSON parse/stringify with error handling) 751→ 752→--- 753→ 754→### cookieService 755→ 756→**Location:** `src/services/cookieService.ts` 757→ 758→**Purpose:** Type-safe wrapper for browser cookies using `universal-cookie` library. 759→ 760→**Methods:** 761→ 762→#### `set(name: T, value: CookieT[T], days?: number)` 763→ 764→Stores value in cookie with expiration. 765→ 766→```typescript 767→cookieService.set(CookieNameE.USER_ID, 'user-123', 30); 768→``` 769→ 770→**Parameters:** 771→ 772→- `name` - Cookie name from `CookieNameE` 773→- `value` - Cookie value (type-safe) 774→- `days` - Expiration in days (default: 180) 775→ 776→**Behavior:** 777→ 778→- Sets cookie with expiration date 779→- Path is always `/` (available site-wide) 780→ 781→--- 782→ 783→#### `get(name: T)` 784→ 785→Retrieves cookie value. 786→ 787→```typescript 788→const userId = cookieService.get(CookieNameE.USER_ID); 789→``` 790→ 791→**Returns:** `CookieT[T] | undefined` 792→ 793→--- 794→ 795→#### `remove(name: T)` 796→ 797→Removes cookie. 798→ 799→```typescript 800→cookieService.remove(CookieNameE.USER_ID); 801→``` 802→ 803→--- 804→ 805→**Type Safety:** 806→ 807→```typescript 808→// CookieNameE defines cookie names 809→export enum CookieNameE { 810→ USER_ID = 'userId', 811→ PREFERENCES = 'preferences', 812→} 813→ 814→// CookieT maps names to value types 815→export type CookieT = { 816→ [CookieNameE.USER_ID]: string; 817→ [CookieNameE.PREFERENCES]: PreferencesT; 818→}; 819→``` 820→ 821→**Usage Example:** 822→ 823→```typescript 824→// Remember user preference 825→cookieService.set(CookieNameE.LANGUAGE, 'en', 365); 826→ 827→// Read on page load 828→const language = cookieService.get(CookieNameE.LANGUAGE) || 'en'; 829→ 830→// Clear on logout 831→cookieService.remove(CookieNameE.SESSION_ID); 832→``` 833→ 834→**Dependencies:** `universal-cookie` 835→ 836→--- 837→ 838→## Service Patterns 839→ 840→### Pattern 1: Global State Services 841→ 842→Services that manage global state using signals. 843→ 844→**Services:** `authService`, `modalService`, `sidebarService`, `notificationService` 845→ 846→**Pattern:** 847→ 848→```typescript 849→import { createGlobalValueSetter } from 'Shared/store/createGlobalValueSetter'; 850→import { GlobalStoreFieldE } from 'Shared/types/GlobalStoreFieldE'; 851→ 852→const updateField = createGlobalValueSetter(GlobalStoreFieldE.MY_FIELD); 853→ 854→export const myService = { 855→ add: item => { 856→ updateField(current => [...current, item]); 857→ }, 858→ remove: id => { 859→ updateField(current => current.filter(i => i.id !== id)); 860→ }, 861→}; 862→``` 863→ 864→**Why:** 865→ 866→- Centralized state management 867→- Type-safe updates 868→- Reactive - components re-render automatically 869→- Service logic separated from UI 870→ 871→--- 872→ 873→### Pattern 2: Storage Services 874→ 875→Services that wrap browser storage APIs with type safety. 876→ 877→**Services:** `localStorageService`, `cookieService` 878→ 879→**Pattern:** 880→ 881→```typescript 882→export const storageService = { 883→ set(name: T, value: TypeMap[T]) { 884→ // Type-safe set 885→ }, 886→ get(name: T): TypeMap[T] | null { 887→ // Type-safe get with return type inference 888→ }, 889→ remove(name: T) { 890→ // Type-safe remove 891→ }, 892→}; 893→``` 894→ 895→**Benefits:** 896→ 897→- TypeScript ensures correct types for each key 898→- Centralized storage logic 899→- Easy to add new storage keys 900→- Consistent error handling 901→ 902→--- 903→ 904→### Pattern 3: API Services 905→ 906→Services that wrap API calls with error handling. 907→ 908→**Services:** `aiAssistantService` 909→ 910→**Pattern:** 911→ 912→```typescript 913→export const apiService = { 914→ async operation(params): Promise { 915→ try { 916→ const response = await axiosClient.post(endpoint, params); 917→ return response.data; 918→ } catch (error) { 919→ if (error instanceof AxiosError) { 920→ // Handle specific errors 921→ if (error.code === 'ECONNABORTED') { 922→ throw new Error('User-friendly message'); 923→ } 924→ } 925→ throw error; // Re-throw unknown errors 926→ } 927→ }, 928→}; 929→``` 930→ 931→**Benefits:** 932→ 933→- Consistent error handling 934→- User-friendly error messages 935→- Timeout handling 936→- Type-safe responses 937→ 938→--- 939→ 940→## Error Handling 941→ 942→### General Principles 943→ 944→1. **Catch at service level** - Services should catch and transform errors 945→2. **User-friendly messages** - Convert technical errors to readable messages 946→3. **Log for debugging** - Console errors in development 947→4. **Graceful degradation** - Return sensible defaults when possible 948→ 949→### Examples 950→ 951→**Storage Services:** 952→ 953→```typescript 954→try { 955→ return JSON.parse(data); 956→} catch { 957→ // Remove corrupted data and return null 958→ localStorage.removeItem(key); 959→ return null; 960→} 961→``` 962→ 963→**API Services:** 964→ 965→```typescript 966→catch (error: unknown) { 967→ if (error instanceof AxiosError) { 968→ if (error.code === 'ECONNABORTED') { 969→ throw new Error('Request timed out'); 970→ } 971→ if (error.response?.status === 500) { 972→ throw new Error('Server error'); 973→ } 974→ } 975→ throw error; // Unknown errors re-thrown 976→} 977→``` 978→ 979→**Notification:** 980→ 981→```typescript 982→try { 983→ await riskyOperation(); 984→ notificationService.addAlert('Success!'); 985→} catch (error) { 986→ notificationService.addErrorAlert(error.message); 987→} 988→``` 989→ 990→--- 991→ 992→## Testing Services 993→ 994→Services should have tests in `__tests__` directories: 995→ 996→``` 997→src/services/ 998→ ├── authService.ts 999→ ├── authService.test.ts 1000→ ├── modalService.ts 1001→ └── modalService.test.ts 1002→``` 1003→ 1004→**Test Guidelines:** 1005→ 1006→- Test each public method 1007→- Test error cases 1008→- Mock dependencies (storage, API calls) 1009→- Test state changes for state-management services 1010→ 1011→**Example:** 1012→ 1013→```typescript 1014→describe('authService', () => { 1015→ it('should initialize with token from localStorage', () => { 1016→ localStorageService.set(LocalStorageNameE.ACCESS_TOKEN, 'token-123'); 1017→ 1018→ authService.initialize(); 1019→ 1020→ expect(globalStore[GlobalStoreFieldE.AUTH_TOKEN].value).toBe('token-123'); 1021→ }); 1022→ 1023→ it('should clear cache on logout', () => { 1024→ authService.logout(); 1025→ 1026→ expect(queryClient.clear).toHaveBeenCalled(); 1027→ }); 1028→}); 1029→``` 1030→ 1031→--- 1032→ 1033→## Service Dependencies 1034→ 1035→``` 1036→authService 1037→ ├── localStorageService 1038→ ├── globalStore 1039→ ├── queryClient 1040→ └── sentryHelpers 1041→ 1042→modalService 1043→ └── globalStore 1044→ 1045→sidebarService 1046→ └── globalStore 1047→ 1048→notificationService 1049→ └── globalStore 1050→ 1051→scrollLockService 1052→ └── (none - pure DOM) 1053→ 1054→aiAssistantService 1055→ └── axiosClient 1056→ 1057→aiConversationStorageService 1058→ └── localStorageService 1059→ 1060→localStorageService 1061→ └── jsonHelpers 1062→ 1063→cookieService 1064→ └── universal-cookie (external) 1065→``` 1066→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. 1→# Code Patterns 2→ 3→**Purpose:** Practical guide on how to implement features using established patterns. For understanding why these patterns exist, see [ARCHITECTURE.md](./ARCHITECTURE.md). 4→ 5→--- 6→ 7→## 1. Modal Pattern 8→ 9→### How It Works 10→ 11→Modals are managed through `modalService` which uses global state to control modal lifecycle. Each modal has a name (enum) and optional typed props. 12→ 13→**Service Methods:** 14→ 15→- `open(modalName, props?)` - Opens a modal with optional props 16→- `close(modalName)` - Closes a specific modal 17→ 18→**Implementation:** 19→ 20→- Modals stack on top of each other 21→- Props are type-safe based on modal name 22→- State stored in `GlobalStoreFieldE.MODALS` 23→ 24→### When to Use 25→ 26→- Confirmation dialogs 27→- Small forms or quick actions 28→- Alerts and notifications requiring user acknowledgment 29→ 30→### Usage Example 31→ 32→```typescript 33→import { modalService } from 'Services/modalService'; 34→import { ModalNameE } from 'Components/modal/ModalNameE'; 35→ 36→// Open modal with props 37→modalService.open(ModalNameE.TRANSACTION, { transactionId: 'uuid' }); 38→ 39→// Close modal 40→modalService.close(ModalNameE.TRANSACTION); 41→``` 42→ 43→### Example Files 44→ 45→- Service: `src/services/modalService.ts` 46→- Modal names: `src/components/modal/ModalNameE.ts` 47→- Modal props: `src/components/modal/ModalPropsT.ts` 48→- Modal component: `src/components/modal/Modal.tsx` 49→ 50→--- 51→ 52→## 2. Sidebar Pattern 53→ 54→### How It Works 55→ 56→Sidebars are managed through `sidebarService` with support for stacking and graceful animations. Each sidebar has a name (enum) and typed props. 57→ 58→**Service Methods:** 59→ 60→- `open(sidebarName, props?)` - Opens sidebar, adds to stack (removes duplicate if exists) 61→- `openFirst(sidebarName, props?)` - Opens sidebar, clears entire stack first 62→- `close(sidebarName)` - Marks sidebar as deleted (triggers close animation) 63→- `closeAll()` - Marks all sidebars as deleted 64→- `finalizeClose(sidebarName)` - Removes sidebar from state after animation completes 65→ 66→**Implementation:** 67→ 68→- Sidebars support stacking (multiple open at once) 69→- Graceful closing with animations via `deleted` flag 70→- Props are type-safe based on sidebar name 71→- State stored in `GlobalStoreFieldE.SIDEBAR` 72→ 73→### When to Use 74→ 75→- Forms (create, edit) 76→- Detail views 77→- Multi-step workflows 78→- Delete confirmations 79→- Any panel that slides in from the side 80→ 81→### Usage Example 82→ 83→```typescript 84→import { sidebarService } from 'Services/sidebarService'; 85→import { SidebarNameE } from 'Components/sidebar/types/SidebarNameE'; 86→ 87→// Open sidebar and clear stack 88→sidebarService.openFirst(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET_GROUP, { 89→ budgetGroup: budgetGroup, 90→}); 91→ 92→// Open sidebar and add to stack 93→sidebarService.open(SidebarNameE.CREATE_BUDGET_ITEM); 94→ 95→// Close specific sidebar (triggers animation) 96→sidebarService.close(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET_GROUP); 97→ 98→// Close all sidebars 99→sidebarService.closeAll(); 100→ 101→// Finalize close after animation (called by Sidebar component) 102→sidebarService.finalizeClose(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET_GROUP); 103→``` 104→ 105→### Example Files 106→ 107→- Service: `src/services/sidebarService.ts` 108→- Sidebar names: `src/components/sidebar/types/SidebarNameE.ts` 109→- Sidebar props: `src/components/sidebar/types/SidebarPropsT.ts` 110→- Sidebar component: `src/components/sidebar/Sidebar.tsx` 111→- Usage example: `src/pages/budget/group/BudgetGroup.tsx:72` 112→ 113→--- 114→ 115→## 3. State Management Pattern 116→ 117→### How It Works 118→ 119→Global state uses `@preact/signals-react` for reactive state management. State is defined in `globalStore` and accessed/updated through utilities. 120→ 121→**Core Components:** 122→ 123→- `globalStore` - Central store with signals for each field 124→- `useGlobalValue(field)` - Hook to read state in components 125→- `createGlobalValueSetter(field)` - Creates updater function for services 126→ 127→**Implementation:** 128→ 129→- Each field is a `signal` that can be subscribed to 130→- React components subscribe using `useGlobalValue` 131→- Services update state using setter functions 132→- Changes automatically trigger re-renders in subscribed components 133→ 134→### When to Use 135→ 136→- Auth token 137→- Notifications 138→- Modals and sidebars state 139→- IDs lists (e.g., deleted items for optimistic updates) 140→- Any state shared across multiple components 141→ 142→### Usage Example 143→ 144→```typescript 145→// In a service 146→import { createGlobalValueSetter } from 'Shared/store/createGlobalValueSetter'; 147→import { GlobalStoreFieldE } from 'Shared/types/GlobalStoreFieldE'; 148→ 149→const updateNotifications = createGlobalValueSetter( 150→ GlobalStoreFieldE.NOTIFICATIONS, 151→); 152→ 153→export const notificationService = { 154→ add: notification => { 155→ updateNotifications(current => [...current, notification]); 156→ }, 157→}; 158→ 159→// In a component 160→import { useGlobalValue } from 'Shared/store/useGlobalValue'; 161→import { GlobalStoreFieldE } from 'Shared/types/GlobalStoreFieldE'; 162→ 163→const MyComponent = () => { 164→ const notifications = useGlobalValue(GlobalStoreFieldE.NOTIFICATIONS); 165→ 166→ return
{notifications.length} notifications
; 167→}; 168→``` 169→ 170→### Example Files 171→ 172→- Store: `src/shared/store/globalStore.ts` 173→- Hook: `src/shared/store/useGlobalValue.ts` 174→- Setter: `src/shared/store/createGlobalValueSetter.ts` 175→- Field names: `src/shared/types/GlobalStoreFieldE.ts` 176→- Usage in service: `src/services/sidebarService.ts:6` 177→ 178→--- 179→ 180→## 4. Data Fetching Pattern 181→ 182→### How It Works 183→ 184→Data fetching uses `@tanstack/react-query` with repository hooks that wrap API calls. Each repository hook handles a specific operation. 185→ 186→**Naming Convention:** 187→ 188→- `useLoadX` - Fetch single item or list 189→- `useCreateX` - Create new item 190→- `useEditX` - Update existing item 191→- `useDeleteX` - Delete item 192→ 193→**Implementation:** 194→ 195→- All API calls go through `requestClient` (Axios wrapper) 196→- Hooks use `useQuery` (read) or `useMutation` (write) 197→- Query keys based on `ApiGatewayE` enum 198→- Automatic cache invalidation after mutations 199→- Infinite queries for paginated lists 200→ 201→### When to Use 202→ 203→- All API interactions 204→- Server state management 205→- Caching and background updates 206→- Optimistic updates 207→ 208→### Query Hook Example (Read) 209→ 210→```typescript 211→import { useInfiniteQuery } from '@tanstack/react-query'; 212→import { requestClient } from 'Shared/requestClient/requestClient'; 213→import { ApiGatewayE } from 'Shared/types/api/ApiGatewayE'; 214→ 215→export const useTransactionList = filters => { 216→ return useInfiniteQuery({ 217→ queryKey: [ApiGatewayE.BANK_TRANSACTIONS, filters], 218→ queryFn: async ({ pageParam = 1 }) => { 219→ return await requestClient.get(ApiGatewayE.BANK_TRANSACTIONS, undefined, { 220→ page: pageParam, 221→ ...filters, 222→ }); 223→ }, 224→ getNextPageParam: lastPage => { 225→ return lastPage.page < lastPage.totalPages ? lastPage.page + 1 : undefined; 226→ }, 227→ }); 228→}; 229→ 230→// In component - destructure what you need 231→const MyComponent = () => { 232→ const { data, isLoading, fetchNextPage, hasNextPage } = 233→ useTransactionList(filters); 234→ 235→ const transactions = data?.pages.flatMap(p => p.results) ?? []; 236→ 237→ return ; 238→}; 239→``` 240→ 241→### Mutation Hook Example (Write) 242→ 243→```typescript 244→import { useMutation } from '@tanstack/react-query'; 245→import { queryClient } from 'Shared/requestClient/queryClient'; 246→import { requestClient } from 'Shared/requestClient/requestClient'; 247→import { ApiGatewayE } from 'Shared/types/api/ApiGatewayE'; 248→ 249→export const useCreateTransaction = () => { 250→ const mutation = useMutation({ 251→ mutationFn: async data => { 252→ return await requestClient.post( 253→ ApiGatewayE.BANK_TRANSACTIONS, 254→ undefined, 255→ data, 256→ ); 257→ }, 258→ }); 259→ 260→ return async transactionBody => { 261→ await mutation.mutateAsync(transactionBody); 262→ 263→ // Invalidate cache to refetch 264→ await queryClient.invalidateQueries({ 265→ queryKey: [ApiGatewayE.BANK_TRANSACTIONS], 266→ }); 267→ }; 268→}; 269→ 270→// In component 271→const MyComponent = () => { 272→ const createTransaction = useCreateTransaction(); 273→ 274→ const handleCreate = async data => { 275→ try { 276→ await createTransaction(data); 277→ notificationService.addAlert('Transaction created!'); 278→ } catch (error) { 279→ notificationService.addErrorAlert(error.message); 280→ } 281→ }; 282→ 283→ return ; 284→}; 285→``` 286→ 287→### Example Files 288→ 289→- Query hook: `src/repositories/transactions/useTransactionList.ts` 290→- Mutation hook: `src/repositories/transactions/useCreateTransaction.ts` 291→- Request client: `src/shared/requestClient/requestClient.ts` 292→- Query client: `src/shared/requestClient/queryClient.ts` 293→- API endpoints: `src/shared/types/api/ApiGatewayE.ts` 294→ 295→--- 296→ 297→## 5. Form Components Pattern 298→ 299→### How It Works 300→ 301→Form components follow the `Au*` naming convention (Atomic UI). All form-related components are prefixed with `Au`. 302→ 303→**Available Components:** 304→ 305→- `AuButton` - Button component 306→- `AuField` - Form field wrapper (label + input + error) 307→- `AuDropdown` - Dropdown menu 308→- `AuSelect` - Select dropdown 309→- `AuCheckbox` - Checkbox input 310→- `AuRadioGroup` - Radio button group 311→- `AuDatePicker` - Date picker 312→- `AuForm` - Form container 313→- `AuFormBlock` - Form section wrapper 314→ 315→**Implementation:** 316→ 317→- Consistent API across all form components 318→- Built-in error handling and validation 319→- Accessible by default 320→- Styled with SCSS modules 321→ 322→### When to Use 323→ 324→- All form inputs and controls 325→- Interactive UI elements (buttons, toggles) 326→- Form layouts and sections 327→ 328→### Naming Rules 329→ 330→- **Always** prefix form components with `Au` 331→- Use PascalCase after prefix: `AuButton`, not `Aubutton` 332→- Place in `src/components/form/` directory 333→- Create subdirectory for each component: `form/button/AuButton.tsx` 334→ 335→### Usage Example 336→ 337→```typescript 338→import AuButton from 'Components/form/button/AuButton'; 339→import AuField from 'Components/form/field/AuField'; 340→import { AuButtonKindE } from 'Components/form/button/AuButtonKindE'; 341→import { validationPatterns } from 'Shared/constants/validationPatterns'; 342→import { FormProvider, useForm } from 'react-hook-form'; 343→ 344→const MyForm = () => { 345→ const formMethods = useForm({ 346→ defaultValues: { email: '', amount: '' }, 347→ }); 348→ 349→ const handleSubmit = async data => { 350→ // Handle form submission 351→ }; 352→ 353→ return ( 354→ 355→
356→ 357→ 358→ 359→ 360→ 366→ 367→ 368→ 369→ 370→ Save 371→ 372→
373→ 374→ ); 375→}; 376→``` 377→ 378→### Example Files 379→ 380→- Components: `src/components/form/*/Au*.tsx` 381→- Button: `src/components/form/button/AuButton.tsx` 382→- Field: `src/components/form/field/AuField.tsx` 383→- Dropdown: `src/components/form/dropdown/AuDropdown.tsx` 384→ 385→--- 386→ 387→## 6. Routing Pattern 388→ 389→### How It Works 390→ 391→Routing uses React Router v7 with Sentry integration. Routes are organized with route guards for authentication. 392→ 393→**Route Guards:** 394→ 395→- `OnlyPrivate` - Wraps authenticated routes, redirects to login if not authenticated 396→- `OnlyPublic` - Wraps public routes (login, registration), redirects to app if authenticated 397→ 398→**Implementation:** 399→ 400→- Routes defined in `AppRoutes.tsx` using `createRoutesFromElements` 401→- Sentry wraps router for error tracking 402→- Path constants defined in `appPaths` using `AppPath` class 403→- Nested route structure with guards as parent routes 404→ 405→### When to Use 406→ 407→- Adding new pages 408→- Protecting authenticated routes 409→- Defining navigation paths 410→ 411→### Usage Example 412→ 413→```typescript 414→// In appPaths.ts 415→export const appPaths = { 416→ TRANSACTIONS: new AppPath('/transactions'), 417→ BUDGET: new AppPath('/budgets/:budgetId'), 418→}; 419→ 420→// In AppRoutes.tsx 421→}> 422→ } /> 423→ } /> 424→ 425→ 426→}> 427→ } /> 428→ 429→ 430→// In component - navigate programmatically 431→import { useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom'; 432→import { appPaths } from 'Shared/constants/appPaths'; 433→ 434→const MyComponent = () => { 435→ const navigate = useNavigate(); 436→ 437→ const goToBudget = budgetId => { 438→ navigate(appPaths.BUDGET.getPath({ budgetId })); 439→ }; 440→ 441→ return ; 442→}; 443→``` 444→ 445→### Example Files 446→ 447→- Routes: `src/app/routes/AppRoutes.tsx` 448→- Route guards: `src/app/routes/OnlyPrivate.tsx`, `src/app/routes/OnlyPublic.tsx` 449→- Path constants: `src/shared/constants/appPaths.ts` 450→- Path helper: `src/shared/utils/url/AppPath.ts` 451→ 452→--- 453→ 454→## 7. Path Aliases 455→ 456→### How It Works 457→ 458→TypeScript path aliases provide absolute imports instead of relative paths. Configured in `tsconfig.json` and `vite.config.ts`. 459→ 460→**Available Aliases:** 461→ 462→- `Assets/*` → `src/assets/*` 463→- `Repositories/*` → `src/repositories/*` 464→- `Services/*` → `src/services/*` 465→- `Components/*` → `src/components/*` 466→- `Shared/*` → `src/shared/*` 467→ 468→**Important:** Both TypeScript and Vite configs must be kept in sync. 469→ 470→### When to Use 471→ 472→- **Always** use path aliases for imports 473→- **Never** use relative paths like `../../../` 474→- Makes refactoring easier 475→- Improves code readability 476→ 477→### Usage Example 478→ 479→```typescript 480→// ✅ GOOD - Use path aliases 481→import { sidebarService } from 'Services/sidebarService'; 482→import { useTransactionList } from 'Repositories/transactions/useTransactionList'; 483→import AuButton from 'Components/form/button/AuButton'; 484→import { TransactionDTO } from 'Shared/types/TransactionDTO'; 485→import logo from 'Assets/logo.svg'; 486→ 487→// ❌ BAD - Avoid relative paths 488→import { sidebarService } from '../../../services/sidebarService'; 489→import { useTransactionList } from '../../repositories/transactions/useTransactionList'; 490→import AuButton from '../../../components/form/button/AuButton'; 491→``` 492→ 493→### Configuration Files 494→ 495→- TypeScript config: `tsconfig.json` 496→- Vite config: `vite.config.ts` 497→ 498→--- 499→ 500→## Summary 501→ 502→### Key Principles 503→ 504→1. **Services manage UI state** - Use modalService, sidebarService for modals/sidebars 505→2. **Type-safe props** - Enums + mapped types ensure correct props at compile time 506→3. **Repository pattern** - All API calls go through repository hooks 507→4. **Absolute imports** - Always use path aliases 508→5. **Naming conventions** - Au* for forms, *Page for pages, \*Sidebar for sidebars 509→6. **Route guards** - Protect routes with OnlyPrivate/OnlyPublic 510→ 511→--- 512→ 513→## Common Workflows 514→ 515→### Opening a sidebar with form 516→ 517→```typescript 518→import { sidebarService } from 'Services/sidebarService'; 519→import { SidebarNameE } from 'Components/sidebar/types/SidebarNameE'; 520→ 521→// Clear stack and open 522→sidebarService.openFirst(SidebarNameE.CREATE_BUDGET, { budgetId }); 523→ 524→// Add to existing stack 525→sidebarService.open(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET_ITEM, { itemId }); 526→``` 527→ 528→### Fetching and creating data 529→ 530→```typescript 531→import { useLoadBudgets } from 'Repositories/budgets/useLoadBudgets'; 532→import { useCreateBudget } from 'Repositories/budgets/useCreateBudget'; 533→ 534→const MyComponent = () => { 535→ const { data: budgets, isLoading } = useLoadBudgets(); 536→ const createBudget = useCreateBudget(); 537→ 538→ const handleCreate = async () => { 539→ await createBudget({ name: 'My Budget', startDate: new Date() }); 540→ }; 541→ 542→ return ( 543→ <> 544→ {isLoading && } 545→ {budgets?.map(budget => ( 546→ 547→ ))} 548→ New Budget 549→ 550→ ); 551→}; 552→``` 553→ 554→### Reading/updating global state 555→ 556→```typescript 557→import { useGlobalValue } from 'Shared/store/useGlobalValue'; 558→import { createGlobalValueSetter } from 'Shared/store/createGlobalValueSetter'; 559→import { GlobalStoreFieldE } from 'Shared/types/GlobalStoreFieldE'; 560→ 561→// In component (read) 562→const MyComponent = () => { 563→ const sidebars = useGlobalValue(GlobalStoreFieldE.SIDEBAR); 564→ return
{sidebars.length} sidebars open
; 565→}; 566→ 567→// In service (update) 568→const updateSidebar = createGlobalValueSetter(GlobalStoreFieldE.SIDEBAR); 569→ 570→export const sidebarService = { 571→ open: sidebar => { 572→ updateSidebar(current => [...current, sidebar]); 573→ }, 574→}; 575→``` 576→ 577→### Navigating to route 578→ 579→```typescript 580→import { useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom'; 581→import { appPaths } from 'Shared/constants/appPaths'; 582→ 583→const MyComponent = () => { 584→ const navigate = useNavigate(); 585→ 586→ const goToTransactions = () => { 587→ navigate(appPaths.TRANSACTIONS.getPath()); 588→ }; 589→ 590→ const goToBudget = budgetId => { 591→ navigate(appPaths.BUDGET.getPath({ budgetId })); 592→ }; 593→ 594→ return ( 595→ <> 596→ 597→ 598→ 599→ ); 600→}; 601→``` 602→ 603→### Form with validation 604→ 605→```typescript 606→import { FormProvider, useForm } from 'react-hook-form'; 607→import AuField from 'Components/form/field/AuField'; 608→import AuButton from 'Components/form/button/AuButton'; 609→import { validationPatterns } from 'Shared/constants/validationPatterns'; 610→import { replacePatterns } from 'Shared/constants/replacePatterns'; 611→ 612→const MyForm = () => { 613→ const formMethods = useForm(); 614→ 615→ const handleSubmit = async data => { 616→ try { 617→ await saveData(data); 618→ notificationService.addAlert('Saved!'); 619→ } catch (error) { 620→ notificationService.addErrorAlert(error.message); 621→ } 622→ }; 623→ 624→ return ( 625→ 626→

const response = await axiosClient.get('/api/users'); ``` **Error handling:** - 400 (ValidationError) - handled by validation layer - 401 (AuthorizationError) - handled by authInterceptor - All others - caught by errorInterceptor, displays ErrorModal ### Error Interceptor Utility **Location:** `src/shared/requestClient/errorInterceptor.ts` **Purpose:** Catches API errors not handled by ValidationError or AuthorizationError, extracts error message, and displays ErrorModal. **Function: handleErrorModal** ```typescript handleErrorModal(error: AxiosError): void ``` **What it does:** 1. # Component Documentation Comprehensive guide to all React components in the application. ## Form Components (`src/components/form/`) ### AuButton **Location:** `src/components/form/button/AuButton.tsx` **Purpose:** Flexible button component with multiple variants and sizes. **Props:** ```typescript type PropsT = { areaPressed?: boolean; className?: string; children: React.ReactNode; form?: string; // Form ID for submit buttons kind?: AuButtonKindE; // Button variant onClick?: () => void; size?: AuButtonSizeE; // Button size testId?: string; type?: 'submit' | 'reset' | 'button'; disabled?: boolean; }; ``` **Key Features:** - Six button variants via `AuButtonKindE`: PRIMARY, SECONDARY, LINK, ICON, CHIPS, TRANSPARENT - Three sizes via `AuButtonSizeE`: SMALL, MEDIUM, LARGE - Prevents default on click (calls `e.preventDefault()`) - Accessible with aria-pressed support - Default type is 'button' (prevents accidental form submission) **Usage Example:** ```typescript import AuButton from 'Components/form/button/AuButton'; import { AuButtonKindE } from 'Components/form/button/AuButtonKindE'; import { AuButtonSizeE } from 'Components/form/button/AuButtonSizeE'; handleSubmit()} > Save Changes ``` **Dependencies:** `classnames` --- ### AuField **Location:** `src/components/form/field/AuField.tsx` **Purpose:** Form field wrapper that integrates with react-hook-form, providing label, input, validation, and error display. **Props:** ```typescript type MyFieldProps = { children: React.ReactElement; // Input element to wrap className?: string; clearValue?: AuFieldClearValueT; // Value for clear button defaultFocus?: boolean; // Auto-focus on mount htmlFor?: string; maxLength?: number; minLength?: number; name: string; // Required - field name label?: string; type?: string; placeholder?: string; pattern?: ValidationPatternT; // Regex validation replace?: ReplacePatternT; // Replace pattern on input required?: boolean; withoutValidation?: boolean; // Skip validation }; ``` **Key Features:** - Integrates with react-hook-form via `useController` - Built-in validation: required, minLength, maxLength, pattern - Optional clear button when value differs from `clearValue` - Real-time pattern replacement on input - Blocks invalid input based on pattern - Auto-focus support - Error display below input - Warns in dev mode if child props are overridden **Usage Example:** ```typescript import AuField from 'Components/form/field/AuField'; import { useFormContext } from 'react-hook-form'; // Inside a FormProvider ``` **Dependencies:** `react-hook-form`, `classnames`, `AuButton`, `ClearSvg` --- ### AuDropdown **Location:** `src/components/form/dropdown/AuDropdown.tsx` **Purpose:** Dropdown menu component for displaying action menus. **Usage:** Typically used for action menus in table rows or cards. **Dependencies:** Position-based dropdown with click-outside detection --- ### AuSelect **Location:** `src/components/form/select/AuSelect.tsx` **Purpose:** Select dropdown for choosing from options. **Props:** Accepts options as `SelectOptionT[]` type. **Usage:** Form select with react-hook-form integration. --- ### AuCheckbox **Location:** `src/components/form/checkbox/AuCheckbox.tsx` **Purpose:** Checkbox input component. **Usage:** Integrated with react-hook-form for boolean values. --- ### AuRadioGroup **Location:** `src/components/form/radioGroup/AuRadioGroup.tsx` **Purpose:** Radio button group component. **Usage:** Multiple choice selection with react-hook-form. --- ### AuDatePicker **Location:** `src/components/form/datePicker/AuDatePicker.tsx` **Purpose:** Date picker component. **Usage:** Date input with calendar UI. --- ### AuForm **Location:** `src/components/form/AuForm.tsx` **Purpose:** Form container that provides react-hook-form context. **Usage:** Wraps form fields with FormProvider. --- ### AuFormBlock **Location:** `src/components/form/formBlock/AuFormBlock.tsx` **Purpose:** Form section wrapper for grouping related fields. **Usage:** Visual grouping of form fields. --- ## Layout Components (`src/components/layouts/`) ### MainLayout **Location:** `src/components/layouts/main/MainLayout.tsx` **Purpose:** Main application layout with navigation menu, content area, footer, and AI assistant. **Props:** ```typescript type PropsT = { className?: string; children?: ReactNode; // Page content }; ``` **Key Features:** - Wraps content in `BaseLayout` - Includes `Menu` component in nav - Includes footer with logo - Includes `AIAssistant` component - Applies custom className to main content area **Structure:** ``` BaseLayout ├──
Menu ├──
{children} ├──
Logo └── AIAssistant ``` **Usage Example:** ```typescript import MainLayout from 'Components/layouts/main/MainLayout'; const MyPage = () => (

Page Content

 ); ``` **Dependencies:** `BaseLayout`, `Menu`, `AIAssistant` --- ### BaseLayout **Location:** `src/components/layouts/base/BaseLayout.tsx` **Purpose:** Base layout wrapper that provides fundamental structure. **Usage:** Usually not used directly - consumed by `MainLayout`. --- ### Menu **Location:** `src/components/layouts/main/menu/Menu.tsx` **Purpose:** Navigation menu component. **Key Features:** - Contains `Navigation` links - Includes `LogoutButton` --- ### Navigation **Location:** `src/components/layouts/main/menu/navigation/Navigation.tsx` **Purpose:** Navigation links for main pages. **Features:** Links to Transactions, Budgets, Bank Accounts, Categories, Profile --- ### LogoutButton **Location:** `src/components/layouts/main/menu/logoutButton/LogoutButton.tsx` **Purpose:** Logout button in menu. **Features:** Calls auth service to log out user --- ## Modal System (`src/components/modal/`) ### Modal **Location:** `src/components/modal/Modal.tsx` **Purpose:** Modal dialog component that uses HTML `` element and global state management. **Props:** ```typescript type PropsT = { children: ReactNode; // Modal content name: ModalNameE; // Modal identifier title: string; // Modal title type?: ModalTypeE; // Modal variant (REGULAR) }; ``` **Key Features:** - Uses native `` element with `showModal()` - Subscribes to global modals state via `useGlobalValue` - Automatically closes on unmount - Closes on outside click (via `useOutsideClickListener`) - Manages open/close based on presence in global state - Uses `modalService` for state management **Usage Example:** ```typescript import Modal from 'Components/modal/Modal'; import { ModalNameE } from 'Components/modal/ModalNameE'; // Modal component (rendered in app root) // Open modal from anywhere import { modalService } from 'Services/modalService'; modalService.open(ModalNameE.TRANSACTION); ``` **Dependencies:** `modalService`, `useGlobalValue`, `useOutsideClickListener` **Related Files:** - `ModalNameE.ts` - Enum of modal names - `ModalPropsT.ts` - Type-safe props for each modal - `ModalTypeE.ts` - Modal variants --- ## Sidebar System (`src/components/sidebar/`) ### Sidebar **Location:** `src/components/sidebar/Sidebar.tsx` **Purpose:** Sliding sidebar panel with animations, stacking support, and scroll lock management. **Props:** ```typescript type PropsT = { children: ReactNode; // Sidebar content className?: string; headerActions?: ReactNode; // Additional header buttons name: SidebarNameE; // Sidebar identifier onBack?: () => void; // Back button handler onClose?: () => void; // Close callback title?: string; // Sidebar title size?: SidebarSizeE; // REGULAR or other sizes }; ``` **Key Features:** - Renders via React Portal to `#sidebar_portal` - Subscribes to global sidebar state - Supports stacking (multiple sidebars open) - Graceful closing animations via `deleted` flag - Automatically closes on unmount - Closes on backdrop click - Scroll lock for REGULAR size (prevents body scroll) - Back button support - Header actions area - Calls `sidebarService.finalizeClose()` after animation ends **Usage Example:** ```typescript import Sidebar from 'Components/sidebar/Sidebar'; import { SidebarNameE } from 'Components/sidebar/types/SidebarNameE'; import { SidebarSizeE } from 'Components/sidebar/types/SidebarSizeE'; // Sidebar component const EditBudgetSidebar = () => { const sidebars = useGlobalValue(GlobalStoreFieldE.SIDEBAR); const props = sidebars.find(s => s.name === SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET)?.props; if (!props) return null; return ( console.log('Closed')} > ); }; // Open from anywhere import { sidebarService } from 'Services/sidebarService'; sidebarService.openFirst(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET, { budgetId: 'uuid' }); ``` **Dependencies:** `sidebarService`, `scrollLockService`, `useGlobalValue`, `createPortal` **Related Files:** - `SidebarNameE.ts` - Enum of sidebar names - `SidebarPropsT.ts` - Type-safe props for each sidebar - `SidebarSizeE.ts` - Sidebar size variants - `SidebarLayout.tsx` - Layout wrapper for sidebar content --- ### SidebarLayout **Location:** `src/components/sidebar/SidebarLayout.tsx` **Purpose:** Layout wrapper for sidebar content with consistent structure. **Usage:** Wraps sidebar forms and content for consistent padding and structure. --- ## UI Components ### PageHeader **Location:** `src/components/pageHeader/PageHeader.tsx` **Purpose:** Page header component with title and optional actions. **Props:** ```typescript type PropsT = { children?: React.ReactNode; // Action buttons title: string; // Page title }; ``` **Key Features:** - Displays page title as `

` - Optional actions area on the right - Consistent styling across pages **Usage Example:** ```typescript import PageHeader from 'Components/pageHeader/PageHeader'; import AuButton from 'Components/form/button/AuButton'; New Transaction ``` **Dependencies:** None (pure presentation component) --- ### Spinner **Location:** `src/components/spinner/Spinner.tsx` **Purpose:** Loading spinner component. **Usage:** Display loading state during async operations. --- ### EmptyState **Location:** `src/components/emptyState/EmptyState.tsx` **Purpose:** Empty state placeholder component. **Usage:** Display when lists or data are empty. --- ### CategoryBadge **Location:** `src/components/categoryBage/CategoryBadge.tsx` **Purpose:** Badge component for displaying category with color. **Usage:** Show category in transaction lists and details. --- ### EditableLabel **Location:** `src/components/editableLabel/EditableLabel.tsx` **Purpose:** Inline editable label component. **Usage:** Edit text in place (e.g., budget group names). --- ### ActionDropdown **Location:** `src/components/actionDropdown/ActionDropdown.tsx` **Purpose:** Dropdown menu for row actions. **Usage:** Edit/Delete actions in table rows. --- ## AI Assistant Components (`src/components/aiAssistant/`) ### AIAssistant **Location:** `src/components/aiAssistant/AIAssistant.tsx` **Purpose:** Main AI assistant container with chat bubble and window. **Key Features:** - Toggles between bubble and chat window - Uses `useToggle` hook for state - Positioned fixed on page (via CSS) **Structure:** ```typescript
{isOpen && }
``` **Usage:** Automatically included in `MainLayout`. **Dependencies:** `ChatBubble`, `ChatWindow`, `useToggle` --- ### ChatBubble **Location:** `src/components/aiAssistant/chatBubble/ChatBubble.tsx` **Purpose:** Floating chat bubble button to open AI assistant. **Props:** - `onClick`: Opens chat window - `isOpen`: Current state **Usage:** Rendered in `AIAssistant` component. --- ### ChatWindow **Location:** `src/components/aiAssistant/chatWindow/ChatWindow.tsx` **Purpose:** Chat window for AI assistant conversations. **Props:** - `onClose`: Closes chat window **Key Features:** - Displays conversation history - Input for new messages - Integrates with `aiAssistantService` --- ### MessageContent **Location:** `src/components/aiAssistant/chatWindow/MessageContent.tsx` **Purpose:** Renders individual chat messages. **Usage:** Renders user and assistant messages with formatting. --- ## SVG Icon Components (`src/components/svg/`) **Location:** `src/components/svg/` **Purpose:** SVG icon components for consistent iconography. **Icons:** - `BackSvg` - Back arrow - `CalendarSvg` - Calendar icon - `ClearSvg` - Clear/X icon - `CloseSvg` - Close icon - `CopySvg` - Copy icon - `EditSvg` - Edit/Pen icon - `FileImportSvg` - File import icon - `FilterSvg` - Filter icon - `GrabjectSvg` - Grab handle for drag-and-drop - `HamburgerSvg` - Hamburger menu icon - `LogoutSvg` - Logout icon - `PencilSvg` - Pencil edit icon - `PlusSvg` - Plus icon - `ReloadSVG` - Reload icon - `TrashSvg` - Delete/Trash icon - `TriangleSVG` - Triangle icon - `Svg` - Base SVG wrapper component **Usage Example:** ```typescript import PencilSvg from 'Components/svg/PencilSvg'; import AuButton from 'Components/form/button/AuButton'; import { AuButtonKindE } from 'Components/form/button/AuButtonKindE'; ``` **Key Features:** - Consistent sizing - Themeable via CSS - Accessible with proper aria labels - Can be used in buttons and inline --- ## Component Naming Conventions ### Form Components - **Always** prefix with `Au` - PascalCase: `AuButton`, `AuField`, `AuSelect` - Located in `src/components/form/` ### Page Components - Suffix with `Page` - Example: `TransactionsPage`, `BudgetPage` - Located in `src/pages/` ### Sidebar Components - Suffix with `Sidebar` - Example: `EditBudgetSidebar`, `TransactionFormSidebar` - Located in page directories: `src/pages/budget/sidebars/` ### Layout Components - Suffix with `Layout` - Example: `MainLayout`, `BaseLayout` - Located in `src/components/layouts/` ### SVG Components - Suffix with `Svg` or `SVG` - Example: `PencilSvg`, `CloseSvg` - Located in `src/components/svg/` --- ## Component Patterns ### 1. Form Integration Pattern Components that integrate with react-hook-form: ```typescript import { useFormContext, useController } from 'react-hook-form'; const MyFormComponent = ({ name }: { name: string }) => { const { control } = useFormContext(); const { field, fieldState: { error } } = useController({ name, control, rules: { required: 'Required' } }); return (
{error && {error.message}}
); }; ``` ### 2. Global State Subscription Pattern Components that read from global state: ```typescript import { useGlobalValue } from 'Shared/store/useGlobalValue'; import { GlobalStoreFieldE } from 'Shared/types/GlobalStoreFieldE'; const MyComponent = () => { const sidebars = useGlobalValue(GlobalStoreFieldE.SIDEBAR); return
{sidebars.length} open
; }; ``` ### 3. Service-Managed UI Pattern Components managed by services (modals, sidebars): ```typescript // Component definition const MySidebar = () => { const sidebars = useGlobalValue(GlobalStoreFieldE.SIDEBAR); const props = sidebars.find(s => s.name === SidebarNameE.MY_SIDEBAR)?.props; if (!props) return null; return ( Content ); }; // Usage - open from anywhere sidebarService.openFirst(SidebarNameE.MY_SIDEBAR, { id: 'uuid' }); ``` ### 4. Portal Pattern Components that render outside their parent DOM hierarchy: ```typescript import { createPortal } from 'react-dom'; const container = document.getElementById('portal_root'); const MyPortalComponent = () => { return createPortal(
Portal Content
, container!, 'unique-key' ); }; ``` --- ## Testing Components Components should have tests in `__tests__` directories adjacent to the component: ``` src/components/form/button/ ├── AuButton.tsx ├── AuButtonKindE.ts ├── AuButtonSizeE.ts ├── auButton.scss └── __tests__/ └── AuButton.test.tsx ``` **Test Guidelines:** - Test user interactions - Test conditional rendering - Test accessibility - Don't test simple prop passing - Use `@testing-library/react` for component tests --- ## Accessibility Guidelines All components should follow these accessibility practices: 1. **Semantic HTML**: Use appropriate HTML elements (` ```

1→# Service Documentation 2→ 3→Comprehensive guide to all services in the application. Services manage business logic, state, and side effects. 4→ 5→## Core Services 6→ 7→### authService 8→ 9→**Location:** `src/services/authService.ts` 10→ 11→**Purpose:** Manages user authentication state, token storage, and Sentry user tracking. 12→ 13→**Methods:** 14→ 15→#### `initialize()` 16→ 17→Initializes auth state on app startup by loading token from localStorage. 18→ 19→```typescript 20→authService.initialize(); 21→``` 22→ 23→**Behavior:** 24→ 25→- Reads `ACCESS_TOKEN` from localStorage 26→- If token exists, updates global auth state 27→- Sets Sentry user from token for error tracking 28→ 29→**When to call:** App initialization (in root component or App.tsx) 30→ 31→--- 32→ 33→#### `login(token: string)` 34→ 35→Stores auth token and updates global state after successful login. 36→ 37→```typescript 38→authService.login('eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...'); 39→``` 40→ 41→**Behavior:** 42→ 43→- Stores token in localStorage 44→- Updates global `AUTH_TOKEN` state 45→- Sets Sentry user from token 46→ 47→**When to call:** After successful login API response 48→ 49→--- 50→ 51→#### `logout()` 52→ 53→Clears auth state and cached data on logout. 54→ 55→```typescript 56→authService.logout(); 57→``` 58→ 59→**Behavior:** 60→ 61→- Removes token from localStorage 62→- Clears global `AUTH_TOKEN` state 63→- Clears all React Query cache 64→- Clears Sentry user data 65→ 66→**When to call:** User logout, session expiry, or unauthorized errors 67→ 68→--- 69→ 70→**Dependencies:** 71→ 72→- `localStorageService` - Token persistence 73→- `globalStore` - Auth state management 74→- `queryClient` - Cache clearing 75→- `sentryHelpers` - Error tracking 76→ 77→**Usage Example:** 78→ 79→```typescript 80→// On app start 81→useEffect(() => { 82→ authService.initialize(); 83→}, []); 84→ 85→// After login 86→const handleLogin = async (email: string, password: string) => { 87→ const { token } = await loginApi(email, password); 88→ authService.login(token); 89→ navigate('/dashboard'); 90→}; 91→ 92→// On logout 93→const handleLogout = () => { 94→ authService.logout(); 95→ navigate('/login'); 96→}; 97→``` 98→ 99→--- 100→ 101→### modalService 102→ 103→**Location:** `src/services/modalService.ts` 104→ 105→**Purpose:** Manages modal state globally using signals. See [PATTERNS.md](./PATTERNS.md#1-modal-pattern) for detailed pattern documentation. 106→ 107→**Methods:** 108→ 109→#### `open(modalName, props?)` 110→ 111→Opens a modal with optional typed props. 112→ 113→```typescript 114→modalService.open(ModalNameE.TRANSACTION, { transactionId: 'uuid' }); 115→``` 116→ 117→**Parameters:** 118→ 119→- `modalName` - Modal identifier from `ModalNameE` 120→- `props` - Optional props (type-safe based on modal name) 121→ 122→**Returns:** `modalName` 123→ 124→**Behavior:** 125→ 126→- Adds modal to top of stack 127→- Props are type-checked against `ModalPropsT` 128→ 129→--- 130→ 131→#### `close(modalName: ModalNameE)` 132→ 133→Closes a specific modal. 134→ 135→```typescript 136→modalService.close(ModalNameE.TRANSACTION); 137→``` 138→ 139→**Behavior:** 140→ 141→- Removes modal from global state 142→- Modal component detects removal and closes 143→ 144→--- 145→ 146→**Type Safety:** 147→ 148→```typescript 149→// ModalNameE defines available modals 150→export enum ModalNameE { 151→ TRANSACTION = 'transaction', 152→} 153→ 154→// ModalPropsT defines props for each modal 155→export type ModalPropsT = { 156→ [ModalNameE.TRANSACTION]: { transactionId: UuidT }; 157→}; 158→ 159→// TypeScript enforces correct props 160→modalService.open(ModalNameE.TRANSACTION, { transactionId: 'uuid' }); // ✅ 161→modalService.open(ModalNameE.TRANSACTION, { id: 'uuid' }); // ❌ Type error 162→``` 163→ 164→**Dependencies:** `globalStore`, `createGlobalValueSetter` 165→ 166→--- 167→ 168→### sidebarService 169→ 170→**Location:** `src/services/sidebarService.ts` 171→ 172→**Purpose:** Manages sidebar state with stacking and graceful animations. See [PATTERNS.md](./PATTERNS.md#2-sidebar-pattern) for detailed pattern documentation. 173→ 174→**Methods:** 175→ 176→#### `open(sidebarName, props?)` 177→ 178→Opens sidebar and adds to stack. Removes duplicate if already open. 179→ 180→```typescript 181→sidebarService.open(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET, { budgetId: 'uuid' }); 182→``` 183→ 184→**Behavior:** 185→ 186→- If sidebar already in stack, removes old instance 187→- Adds new instance to top of stack 188→- Multiple different sidebars can be open simultaneously 189→ 190→--- 191→ 192→#### `openFirst(sidebarName, props?)` 193→ 194→Opens sidebar and clears entire stack first. 195→ 196→```typescript 197→sidebarService.openFirst(SidebarNameE.CREATE_TRANSACTION); 198→``` 199→ 200→**Behavior:** 201→ 202→- Clears all open sidebars 203→- Opens only the specified sidebar 204→- Use when you want a clean state 205→ 206→--- 207→ 208→#### `close(sidebarName: SidebarNameE)` 209→ 210→Marks sidebar as deleted (triggers close animation). 211→ 212→```typescript 213→sidebarService.close(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET); 214→``` 215→ 216→**Behavior:** 217→ 218→- Sets `deleted: true` on sidebar 219→- Sidebar component detects change and plays close animation 220→- After animation, `finalizeClose()` removes from state 221→ 222→--- 223→ 224→#### `closeAll()` 225→ 226→Marks all open sidebars as deleted. 227→ 228→```typescript 229→sidebarService.closeAll(); 230→``` 231→ 232→**Behavior:** 233→ 234→- Sets `deleted: true` on all sidebars 235→- All sidebars animate closed simultaneously 236→ 237→--- 238→ 239→#### `finalizeClose(sidebarName: SidebarNameE)` 240→ 241→Removes sidebar from state after animation completes. 242→ 243→```typescript 244→sidebarService.finalizeClose(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET); 245→``` 246→ 247→**Behavior:** 248→ 249→- Removes sidebar from global state 250→- **Called automatically by Sidebar component** after animation ends 251→- **Do not call manually** unless you have a specific reason 252→ 253→--- 254→ 255→**Type Safety:** 256→ 257→```typescript 258→// SidebarNameE defines available sidebars 259→export enum SidebarNameE { 260→ EDIT_BUDGET = 'editBudget', 261→ CREATE_TRANSACTION = 'createTransaction', 262→} 263→ 264→// SidebarPropsT defines props for each sidebar 265→export type SidebarPropsT = { 266→ [SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET]: { budgetId: UuidT }; 267→ [SidebarNameE.CREATE_TRANSACTION]: void; 268→}; 269→ 270→// TypeScript enforces correct props 271→sidebarService.open(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET, { budgetId: 'uuid' }); // ✅ 272→sidebarService.open(SidebarNameE.CREATE_TRANSACTION); // ✅ 273→sidebarService.open(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET); // ❌ Missing required props 274→``` 275→ 276→**Dependencies:** `globalStore`, `createGlobalValueSetter` 277→ 278→--- 279→ 280→### notificationService 281→ 282→**Location:** `src/services/notificationService.ts` 283→ 284→**Purpose:** Manages toast notifications with auto-dismiss timers. 285→ 286→**Methods:** 287→ 288→#### `addAlert(content: ReactNode, showTime?: number)` 289→ 290→Adds an info notification. 291→ 292→```typescript 293→notificationService.addAlert('Budget saved successfully!'); 294→notificationService.addAlert(
Custom content
, 3000); 295→``` 296→ 297→**Parameters:** 298→ 299→- `content` - Notification message (string or React element) 300→- `showTime` - Duration in milliseconds (default: 5000ms) 301→ 302→**Returns:** `notificationId` (number) 303→ 304→**Behavior:** 305→ 306→- Adds notification to global state 307→- Auto-increments ID 308→- Sets type to `NotificationTypeE.INFO` 309→ 310→--- 311→ 312→#### `removeAlert(id: number)` 313→ 314→Marks notification for removal (starts hide animation). 315→ 316→```typescript 317→notificationService.removeAlert(notificationId); 318→``` 319→ 320→**Behavior:** 321→ 322→- Sets `hideAt` timestamp 323→- Notification component detects and animates out 324→ 325→--- 326→ 327→#### `clearAlert(id: number)` 328→ 329→Marks notification as deleted and cleans up store. 330→ 331→```typescript 332→notificationService.clearAlert(notificationId); 333→``` 334→ 335→**Behavior:** 336→ 337→- Sets `deleted: true` 338→- Calls `clearStore()` to remove all deleted notifications 339→ 340→--- 341→ 342→#### `clearStore()` 343→ 344→Removes all deleted notifications from state. 345→ 346→```typescript 347→notificationService.clearStore(); 348→``` 349→ 350→**Behavior:** 351→ 352→- If no non-deleted notifications remain, clears entire array 353→- Prevents memory leaks from accumulating notifications 354→ 355→--- 356→ 357→#### `addErrorAlert(message: string)` 358→ 359→Logs error to console. 360→ 361→```typescript 362→notificationService.addErrorAlert('Failed to save budget'); 363→``` 364→ 365→**Note:** Currently only logs to console. Future implementation may show error toast. 366→ 367→--- 368→ 369→**Usage Example:** 370→ 371→```typescript 372→// Success notification 373→const handleSave = async () => { 374→ await saveBudget(); 375→ notificationService.addAlert('Budget saved!'); 376→}; 377→ 378→// Custom duration 379→const handleDelete = async () => { 380→ await deleteBudget(); 381→ notificationService.addAlert('Budget deleted', 3000); 382→}; 383→ 384→// Manual control 385→const notificationId = notificationService.addAlert('Processing...'); 386→// ... later 387→notificationService.removeAlert(notificationId); 388→``` 389→ 390→**Dependencies:** `globalStore`, `createGlobalValueSetter` 391→ 392→--- 393→ 394→### scrollLockService 395→ 396→**Location:** `src/services/scrollLockService.ts` 397→ 398→**Purpose:** Prevents body scroll when modals or sidebars are open. Uses reference counting to support multiple locks. 399→ 400→**Methods:** 401→ 402→#### `scrollLockManager.add(source: string)` 403→ 404→Adds a scroll lock from a specific source. 405→ 406→```typescript 407→scrollLockManager.add('my-sidebar'); 408→``` 409→ 410→**Behavior:** 411→ 412→- Adds source to internal Set 413→- If first lock, adds `modal-open` class to `` 414→- `modal-open` class applies `overflow: hidden` via CSS 415→ 416→--- 417→ 418→#### `scrollLockManager.remove(source: string)` 419→ 420→Removes a scroll lock from a specific source. 421→ 422→```typescript 423→scrollLockManager.remove('my-sidebar'); 424→``` 425→ 426→**Behavior:** 427→ 428→- Removes source from internal Set 429→- If last lock removed, removes `modal-open` class from `` 430→- Body scroll is restored 431→ 432→--- 433→ 434→**How It Works:** 435→ 436→```typescript 437→// Multiple sources can lock scroll 438→scrollLockManager.add('modal-1'); // Body locked 439→scrollLockManager.add('modal-2'); // Still locked 440→scrollLockManager.remove('modal-1'); // Still locked (modal-2 active) 441→scrollLockManager.remove('modal-2'); // Body unlocked 442→``` 443→ 444→**Usage Example:** 445→ 446→```typescript 447→// In Sidebar component 448→useEffect(() => { 449→ if (isOpen) { 450→ scrollLockManager.add('my-sidebar'); 451→ 452→ return () => { 453→ scrollLockManager.remove('my-sidebar'); 454→ }; 455→ } 456→}, [isOpen]); 457→``` 458→ 459→**Note:** Sidebar component automatically manages scroll lock. You rarely need to call this directly. 460→ 461→**Dependencies:** None (pure DOM manipulation) 462→ 463→--- 464→ 465→## AI Assistant Services 466→ 467→### aiAssistantService 468→ 469→**Location:** `src/services/aiAssistant/aiAssistantService.ts` 470→ 471→**Purpose:** Communicates with AI chat backend API. 472→ 473→**Methods:** 474→ 475→#### `sendMessage(message: string, conversationHistory?: AIMessageDTO[])` 476→ 477→Sends a message to AI and returns response. 478→ 479→```typescript 480→const response = await aiAssistantService.sendMessage( 481→ 'How much did I spend on groceries?', 482→ conversationHistory, 483→); 484→``` 485→ 486→**Parameters:** 487→ 488→- `message` - User's message 489→- `conversationHistory` - Previous messages (optional, default: `[]`) 490→ 491→**Returns:** `Promise` 492→ 493→```typescript 494→type AIResponseT = { 495→ message: string; // AI response 496→ conversationId?: string; 497→}; 498→``` 499→ 500→**Error Handling:** 501→ 502→- Timeout (120s): Throws `'AI request timed out. Please try again.'` 503→- Server error (500): Throws `'AI service error. Please try again later.'` 504→- Other errors: Re-throws original error 505→ 506→**Usage Example:** 507→ 508→```typescript 509→try { 510→ const response = await aiAssistantService.sendMessage( 511→ userInput, 512→ previousMessages, 513→ ); 514→ 515→ addMessageToChat(response.message); 516→} catch (error) { 517→ console.error('AI error:', error); 518→ notificationService.addErrorAlert(error.message); 519→} 520→``` 521→ 522→--- 523→ 524→#### `healthCheck()` 525→ 526→Checks AI service health and configuration. 527→ 528→```typescript 529→const health = await aiAssistantService.healthCheck(); 530→``` 531→ 532→**Returns:** 533→ 534→```typescript 535→type HealthResponse = { 536→ status: 'ok' | 'error'; 537→ ollamaConnected: boolean; 538→ model: string; 539→ availableModels?: string[]; 540→ error?: string; 541→}; 542→``` 543→ 544→**Usage Example:** 545→ 546→```typescript 547→const checkAI = async () => { 548→ const health = await aiAssistantService.healthCheck(); 549→ 550→ if (health.status === 'error') { 551→ console.warn('AI unavailable:', health.error);
