576→``` 577→ 578→**Behavior:** 579→ 580→- Serializes messages (converts Date to ISO string) 581→- Stores in localStorage under `AI_CONVERSATION_STORAGE` 582→- Catches and logs errors silently 583→ 584→--- 585→ 586→#### `load()` 587→ 588→Loads conversation from localStorage. 589→ 590→```typescript 591→const messages = aiConversationStorageService.load(); 592→``` 593→ 594→**Returns:** `AIMessageT[]` (empty array if none found) 595→ 596→**Behavior:** 597→ 598→- Deserializes messages (converts ISO string to Date) 599→- Returns empty array on error or missing data 600→ 601→--- 602→ 603→#### `clear()` 604→ 605→Clears conversation from localStorage. 606→ 607→```typescript 608→aiConversationStorageService.clear(); 609→``` 610→ 611→**Behavior:** 612→ 613→- Removes conversation from localStorage 614→- Catches and logs errors silently 615→ 616→--- 617→ 618→**Message Format:** 619→ 620→```typescript 621→type AIMessageT = { 622→ id: string; 623→ role: AIRoleE; // 'user' | 'assistant' 624→ content: string; 625→ timestamp: Date; 626→}; 627→``` 628→ 629→**Usage Example:** 630→ 631→```typescript 632→// Save after each message 633→const handleSendMessage = async (text: string) => { 634→ const newMessage: AIMessageT = { 635→ id: generateId(), 636→ role: AIRoleE.USER, 637→ content: text, 638→ timestamp: new Date(), 639→ }; 640→ 641→ const updatedMessages = [...messages, newMessage]; 642→ aiConversationStorageService.save(updatedMessages); 643→}; 644→ 645→// Load on mount 646→useEffect(() => { 647→ const history = aiConversationStorageService.load(); 648→ setMessages(history); 649→}, []); 650→ 651→// Clear on logout or reset 652→const handleClearChat = () => { 653→ aiConversationStorageService.clear(); 654→ setMessages([]); 655→}; 656→``` 657→ 658→**Dependencies:** `localStorageService` 659→ 660→--- 661→ 662→## Storage Services 663→ 664→### localStorageService 665→ 666→**Location:** `src/services/localStorage/localStorageService.ts` 667→ 668→**Purpose:** Type-safe wrapper for browser localStorage with JSON serialization. 669→ 670→**Methods:** 671→ 672→#### `set(name: T, value: LocalStorageT[T])` 673→ 674→Stores value in localStorage. 675→ 676→```typescript 677→localStorageService.set(LocalStorageNameE.ACCESS_TOKEN, 'token-value'); 678→``` 679→ 680→**Behavior:** 681→ 682→- Serializes value to JSON 683→- Stores in localStorage 684→- Ignores `undefined` and `null` values 685→ 686→--- 687→ 688→#### `get(name: T)` 689→ 690→Retrieves value from localStorage. 691→ 692→```typescript 693→const token = localStorageService.get(LocalStorageNameE.ACCESS_TOKEN); 694→``` 695→ 696→**Returns:** `LocalStorageT[T] | null` 697→ 698→**Behavior:** 699→ 700→- Parses JSON from localStorage 701→- Returns `null` if not found 702→- On parse error, removes corrupted item and returns `null` 703→ 704→--- 705→ 706→#### `remove(name: T)` 707→ 708→Removes value from localStorage. 709→ 710→```typescript 711→localStorageService.remove(LocalStorageNameE.ACCESS_TOKEN); 712→``` 713→ 714→--- 715→ 716→**Type Safety:** 717→ 718→```typescript 719→// LocalStorageNameE defines keys 720→export enum LocalStorageNameE { 721→ ACCESS_TOKEN = 'accessToken', 722→ AI_CONVERSATION_STORAGE = 'aiConversationStorage', 723→} 724→ 725→// LocalStorageT maps keys to value types 726→export type LocalStorageT = { 727→ [LocalStorageNameE.ACCESS_TOKEN]: string; 728→ [LocalStorageNameE.AI_CONVERSATION_STORAGE]: AIMessageStorageT[]; 729→}; 730→ 731→// TypeScript ensures correct types 732→const token: string | null = localStorageService.get( 733→ LocalStorageNameE.ACCESS_TOKEN, 734→); 735→``` 736→ 737→**Usage Example:** 738→ 739→```typescript 740→// Store user preference 741→localStorageService.set(LocalStorageNameE.THEME, 'dark'); 742→ 743→// Retrieve on app start 744→const theme = localStorageService.get(LocalStorageNameE.THEME) || 'light'; 745→ 746→// Clear on logout 747→localStorageService.remove(LocalStorageNameE.USER_PREFERENCES); 748→``` 749→ 750→**Dependencies:** `jsonHelpers` (custom JSON parse/stringify with error handling) 751→ 752→--- 753→ 754→### cookieService 755→ 756→**Location:** `src/services/cookieService.ts` 757→ 758→**Purpose:** Type-safe wrapper for browser cookies using `universal-cookie` library. 759→ 760→**Methods:** 761→ 762→#### `set(name: T, value: CookieT[T], days?: number)` 763→ 764→Stores value in cookie with expiration. 765→ 766→```typescript 767→cookieService.set(CookieNameE.USER_ID, 'user-123', 30); 768→``` 769→ 770→**Parameters:** 771→ 772→- `name` - Cookie name from `CookieNameE` 773→- `value` - Cookie value (type-safe) 774→- `days` - Expiration in days (default: 180) 775→ 776→**Behavior:** 777→ 778→- Sets cookie with expiration date 779→- Path is always `/` (available site-wide) 780→ 781→--- 782→ 783→#### `get(name: T)` 784→ 785→Retrieves cookie value. 786→ 787→```typescript 788→const userId = cookieService.get(CookieNameE.USER_ID); 789→``` 790→ 791→**Returns:** `CookieT[T] | undefined` 792→ 793→--- 794→ 795→#### `remove(name: T)` 796→ 797→Removes cookie. 798→ 799→```typescript 800→cookieService.remove(CookieNameE.USER_ID); 801→``` 802→ 803→--- 804→ 805→**Type Safety:** 806→ 807→```typescript 808→// CookieNameE defines cookie names 809→export enum CookieNameE { 810→ USER_ID = 'userId', 811→ PREFERENCES = 'preferences', 812→} 813→ 814→// CookieT maps names to value types 815→export type CookieT = { 816→ [CookieNameE.USER_ID]: string; 817→ [CookieNameE.PREFERENCES]: PreferencesT; 818→}; 819→``` 820→ 821→**Usage Example:** 822→ 823→```typescript 824→// Remember user preference 825→cookieService.set(CookieNameE.LANGUAGE, 'en', 365); 826→ 827→// Read on page load 828→const language = cookieService.get(CookieNameE.LANGUAGE) || 'en'; 829→ 830→// Clear on logout 831→cookieService.remove(CookieNameE.SESSION_ID); 832→``` 833→ 834→**Dependencies:** `universal-cookie` 835→ 836→--- 837→ 838→## Service Patterns 839→ 840→### Pattern 1: Global State Services 841→ 842→Services that manage global state using signals. 843→ 844→**Services:** `authService`, `modalService`, `sidebarService`, `notificationService` 845→ 846→**Pattern:** 847→ 848→```typescript 849→import { createGlobalValueSetter } from 'Shared/store/createGlobalValueSetter'; 850→import { GlobalStoreFieldE } from 'Shared/types/GlobalStoreFieldE'; 851→ 852→const updateField = createGlobalValueSetter(GlobalStoreFieldE.MY_FIELD); 853→ 854→export const myService = { 855→ add: item => { 856→ updateField(current => [...current, item]); 857→ }, 858→ remove: id => { 859→ updateField(current => current.filter(i => i.id !== id)); 860→ }, 861→}; 862→``` 863→ 864→**Why:** 865→ 866→- Centralized state management 867→- Type-safe updates 868→- Reactive - components re-render automatically 869→- Service logic separated from UI 870→ 871→--- 872→ 873→### Pattern 2: Storage Services 874→ 875→Services that wrap browser storage APIs with type safety. 876→ 877→**Services:** `localStorageService`, `cookieService` 878→ 879→**Pattern:** 880→ 881→```typescript 882→export const storageService = { 883→ set(name: T, value: TypeMap[T]) { 884→ // Type-safe set 885→ }, 886→ get(name: T): TypeMap[T] | null { 887→ // Type-safe get with return type inference 888→ }, 889→ remove(name: T) { 890→ // Type-safe remove 891→ }, 892→}; 893→``` 894→ 895→**Benefits:** 896→ 897→- TypeScript ensures correct types for each key 898→- Centralized storage logic 899→- Easy to add new storage keys 900→- Consistent error handling 901→ 902→--- 903→ 904→### Pattern 3: API Services 905→ 906→Services that wrap API calls with error handling. 907→ 908→**Services:** `aiAssistantService` 909→ 910→**Pattern:** 911→ 912→```typescript 913→export const apiService = { 914→ async operation(params): Promise { 915→ try { 916→ const response = await axiosClient.post(endpoint, params); 917→ return response.data; 918→ } catch (error) { 919→ if (error instanceof AxiosError) { 920→ // Handle specific errors 921→ if (error.code === 'ECONNABORTED') { 922→ throw new Error('User-friendly message'); 923→ } 924→ } 925→ throw error; // Re-throw unknown errors 926→ } 927→ }, 928→}; 929→``` 930→ 931→**Benefits:** 932→ 933→- Consistent error handling 934→- User-friendly error messages 935→- Timeout handling 936→- Type-safe responses 937→ 938→--- 939→ 940→## Error Handling 941→ 942→### General Principles 943→ 944→1. **Catch at service level** - Services should catch and transform errors 945→2. **User-friendly messages** - Convert technical errors to readable messages 946→3. **Log for debugging** - Console errors in development 947→4. **Graceful degradation** - Return sensible defaults when possible 948→ 949→### Examples 950→ 951→**Storage Services:** 952→ 953→```typescript 954→try { 955→ return JSON.parse(data); 956→} catch { 957→ // Remove corrupted data and return null 958→ localStorage.removeItem(key); 959→ return null; 960→} 961→``` 962→ 963→**API Services:** 964→ 965→```typescript 966→catch (error: unknown) { 967→ if (error instanceof AxiosError) { 968→ if (error.code === 'ECONNABORTED') { 969→ throw new Error('Request timed out'); 970→ } 971→ if (error.response?.status === 500) { 972→ throw new Error('Server error'); 973→ } 974→ } 975→ throw error; // Unknown errors re-thrown 976→} 977→``` 978→ 979→**Notification:** 980→ 981→```typescript 982→try { 983→ await riskyOperation(); 984→ notificationService.addAlert('Success!'); 985→} catch (error) { 986→ notificationService.addErrorAlert(error.message); 987→} 988→``` 989→ 990→--- 991→ 992→## Testing Services 993→ 994→Services should have tests in `__tests__` directories: 995→ 996→``` 997→src/services/ 998→ ├── authService.ts 999→ ├── authService.test.ts 1000→ ├── modalService.ts 1001→ └── modalService.test.ts 1002→``` 1003→ 1004→**Test Guidelines:** 1005→ 1006→- Test each public method 1007→- Test error cases 1008→- Mock dependencies (storage, API calls) 1009→- Test state changes for state-management services 1010→ 1011→**Example:** 1012→ 1013→```typescript 1014→describe('authService', () => { 1015→ it('should initialize with token from localStorage', () => { 1016→ localStorageService.set(LocalStorageNameE.ACCESS_TOKEN, 'token-123'); 1017→ 1018→ authService.initialize(); 1019→ 1020→ expect(globalStore[GlobalStoreFieldE.AUTH_TOKEN].value).toBe('token-123'); 1021→ }); 1022→ 1023→ it('should clear cache on logout', () => { 1024→ authService.logout(); 1025→ 1026→ expect(queryClient.clear).toHaveBeenCalled(); 1027→ }); 1028→}); 1029→``` 1030→ 1031→--- 1032→ 1033→## Service Dependencies 1034→ 1035→``` 1036→authService 1037→ ├── localStorageService 1038→ ├── globalStore 1039→ ├── queryClient 1040→ └── sentryHelpers 1041→ 1042→modalService 1043→ └── globalStore 1044→ 1045→sidebarService 1046→ └── globalStore 1047→ 1048→notificationService 1049→ └── globalStore 1050→ 1051→scrollLockService 1052→ └── (none - pure DOM) 1053→ 1054→aiAssistantService 1055→ └── axiosClient 1056→ 1057→aiConversationStorageService 1058→ └── localStorageService 1059→ 1060→localStorageService 1061→ └── jsonHelpers 1062→ 1063→cookieService 1064→ └── universal-cookie (external) 1065→``` 1066→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. 1→# Code Patterns 2→ 3→**Purpose:** Practical guide on how to implement features using established patterns. For understanding why these patterns exist, see [ARCHITECTURE.md](./ARCHITECTURE.md). 4→ 5→--- 6→ 7→## 1. Modal Pattern 8→ 9→### How It Works 10→ 11→Modals are managed through `modalService` which uses global state to control modal lifecycle. Each modal has a name (enum) and optional typed props. 12→ 13→**Service Methods:** 14→ 15→- `open(modalName, props?)` - Opens a modal with optional props 16→- `close(modalName)` - Closes a specific modal 17→ 18→**Implementation:** 19→ 20→- Modals stack on top of each other 21→- Props are type-safe based on modal name 22→- State stored in `GlobalStoreFieldE.MODALS` 23→ 24→### When to Use 25→ 26→- Confirmation dialogs 27→- Small forms or quick actions 28→- Alerts and notifications requiring user acknowledgment 29→ 30→### Usage Example 31→ 32→```typescript 33→import { modalService } from 'Services/modalService'; 34→import { ModalNameE } from 'Components/modal/ModalNameE'; 35→ 36→// Open modal with props 37→modalService.open(ModalNameE.TRANSACTION, { transactionId: 'uuid' }); 38→ 39→// Close modal 40→modalService.close(ModalNameE.TRANSACTION); 41→``` 42→ 43→### Example Files 44→ 45→- Service: `src/services/modalService.ts` 46→- Modal names: `src/components/modal/ModalNameE.ts` 47→- Modal props: `src/components/modal/ModalPropsT.ts` 48→- Modal component: `src/components/modal/Modal.tsx` 49→ 50→--- 51→ 52→## 2. Sidebar Pattern 53→ 54→### How It Works 55→ 56→Sidebars are managed through `sidebarService` with support for stacking and graceful animations. Each sidebar has a name (enum) and typed props. 57→ 58→**Service Methods:** 59→ 60→- `open(sidebarName, props?)` - Opens sidebar, adds to stack (removes duplicate if exists) 61→- `openFirst(sidebarName, props?)` - Opens sidebar, clears entire stack first 62→- `close(sidebarName)` - Marks sidebar as deleted (triggers close animation) 63→- `closeAll()` - Marks all sidebars as deleted 64→- `finalizeClose(sidebarName)` - Removes sidebar from state after animation completes 65→ 66→**Implementation:** 67→ 68→- Sidebars support stacking (multiple open at once) 69→- Graceful closing with animations via `deleted` flag 70→- Props are type-safe based on sidebar name 71→- State stored in `GlobalStoreFieldE.SIDEBAR` 72→ 73→### When to Use 74→ 75→- Forms (create, edit) 76→- Detail views 77→- Multi-step workflows 78→- Delete confirmations 79→- Any panel that slides in from the side 80→ 81→### Usage Example 82→ 83→```typescript 84→import { sidebarService } from 'Services/sidebarService'; 85→import { SidebarNameE } from 'Components/sidebar/types/SidebarNameE'; 86→ 87→// Open sidebar and clear stack 88→sidebarService.openFirst(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET_GROUP, { 89→ budgetGroup: budgetGroup, 90→}); 91→ 92→// Open sidebar and add to stack 93→sidebarService.open(SidebarNameE.CREATE_BUDGET_ITEM); 94→ 95→// Close specific sidebar (triggers animation) 96→sidebarService.close(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET_GROUP); 97→ 98→// Close all sidebars 99→sidebarService.closeAll(); 100→ 101→// Finalize close after animation (called by Sidebar component) 102→sidebarService.finalizeClose(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET_GROUP); 103→``` 104→ 105→### Example Files 106→ 107→- Service: `src/services/sidebarService.ts` 108→- Sidebar names: `src/components/sidebar/types/SidebarNameE.ts` 109→- Sidebar props: `src/components/sidebar/types/SidebarPropsT.ts` 110→- Sidebar component: `src/components/sidebar/Sidebar.tsx` 111→- Usage example: `src/pages/budget/group/BudgetGroup.tsx:72` 112→ 113→--- 114→ 115→## 3. State Management Pattern 116→ 117→### How It Works 118→ 119→Global state uses `@preact/signals-react` for reactive state management. State is defined in `globalStore` and accessed/updated through utilities. 120→ 121→**Core Components:** 122→ 123→- `globalStore` - Central store with signals for each field 124→- `useGlobalValue(field)` - Hook to read state in components 125→- `createGlobalValueSetter(field)` - Creates updater function for services 126→ 127→**Implementation:** 128→ 129→- Each field is a `signal` that can be subscribed to 130→- React components subscribe using `useGlobalValue` 131→- Services update state using setter functions 132→- Changes automatically trigger re-renders in subscribed components 133→ 134→### When to Use 135→ 136→- Auth token 137→- Notifications 138→- Modals and sidebars state 139→- IDs lists (e.g., deleted items for optimistic updates) 140→- Any state shared across multiple components 141→ 142→### Usage Example 143→ 144→```typescript 145→// In a service 146→import { createGlobalValueSetter } from 'Shared/store/createGlobalValueSetter'; 147→import { GlobalStoreFieldE } from 'Shared/types/GlobalStoreFieldE'; 148→ 149→const updateNotifications = createGlobalValueSetter( 150→ GlobalStoreFieldE.NOTIFICATIONS, 151→); 152→ 153→export const notificationService = { 154→ add: notification => { 155→ updateNotifications(current => [...current, notification]); 156→ }, 157→}; 158→ 159→// In a component 160→import { useGlobalValue } from 'Shared/store/useGlobalValue'; 161→import { GlobalStoreFieldE } from 'Shared/types/GlobalStoreFieldE'; 162→ 163→const MyComponent = () => { 164→ const notifications = useGlobalValue(GlobalStoreFieldE.NOTIFICATIONS); 165→ 166→ return
{notifications.length} notifications
; 167→}; 168→``` 169→ 170→### Example Files 171→ 172→- Store: `src/shared/store/globalStore.ts` 173→- Hook: `src/shared/store/useGlobalValue.ts` 174→- Setter: `src/shared/store/createGlobalValueSetter.ts` 175→- Field names: `src/shared/types/GlobalStoreFieldE.ts` 176→- Usage in service: `src/services/sidebarService.ts:6` 177→ 178→--- 179→ 180→## 4. Data Fetching Pattern 181→ 182→### How It Works 183→ 184→Data fetching uses `@tanstack/react-query` with repository hooks that wrap API calls. Each repository hook handles a specific operation. 185→ 186→**Naming Convention:** 187→ 188→- `useLoadX` - Fetch single item or list 189→- `useCreateX` - Create new item 190→- `useEditX` - Update existing item 191→- `useDeleteX` - Delete item 192→ 193→**Implementation:** 194→ 195→- All API calls go through `requestClient` (Axios wrapper) 196→- Hooks use `useQuery` (read) or `useMutation` (write) 197→- Query keys based on `ApiGatewayE` enum 198→- Automatic cache invalidation after mutations 199→- Infinite queries for paginated lists 200→ 201→### When to Use 202→ 203→- All API interactions 204→- Server state management 205→- Caching and background updates 206→- Optimistic updates 207→ 208→### Query Hook Example (Read) 209→ 210→```typescript 211→import { useInfiniteQuery } from '@tanstack/react-query'; 212→import { requestClient } from 'Shared/requestClient/requestClient'; 213→import { ApiGatewayE } from 'Shared/types/api/ApiGatewayE'; 214→ 215→export const useTransactionList = filters => { 216→ return useInfiniteQuery({ 217→ queryKey: [ApiGatewayE.BANK_TRANSACTIONS, filters], 218→ queryFn: async ({ pageParam = 1 }) => { 219→ return await requestClient.get(ApiGatewayE.BANK_TRANSACTIONS, undefined, { 220→ page: pageParam, 221→ ...filters, 222→ }); 223→ }, 224→ getNextPageParam: lastPage => { 225→ return lastPage.page < lastPage.totalPages ? lastPage.page + 1 : undefined; 226→ }, 227→ }); 228→}; 229→ 230→// In component - destructure what you need 231→const MyComponent = () => { 232→ const { data, isLoading, fetchNextPage, hasNextPage } = 233→ useTransactionList(filters); 234→ 235→ const transactions = data?.pages.flatMap(p => p.results) ?? []; 236→ 237→ return ; 238→}; 239→``` 240→ 241→### Mutation Hook Example (Write) 242→ 243→```typescript 244→import { useMutation } from '@tanstack/react-query'; 245→import { queryClient } from 'Shared/requestClient/queryClient'; 246→import { requestClient } from 'Shared/requestClient/requestClient'; 247→import { ApiGatewayE } from 'Shared/types/api/ApiGatewayE'; 248→ 249→export const useCreateTransaction = () => { 250→ const mutation = useMutation({ 251→ mutationFn: async data => { 252→ return await requestClient.post( 253→ ApiGatewayE.BANK_TRANSACTIONS, 254→ undefined, 255→ data, 256→ ); 257→ }, 258→ }); 259→ 260→ return async transactionBody => { 261→ await mutation.mutateAsync(transactionBody); 262→ 263→ // Invalidate cache to refetch 264→ await queryClient.invalidateQueries({ 265→ queryKey: [ApiGatewayE.BANK_TRANSACTIONS], 266→ }); 267→ }; 268→}; 269→ 270→// In component 271→const MyComponent = () => { 272→ const createTransaction = useCreateTransaction(); 273→ 274→ const handleCreate = async data => { 275→ try { 276→ await createTransaction(data); 277→ notificationService.addAlert('Transaction created!'); 278→ } catch (error) { 279→ notificationService.addErrorAlert(error.message); 280→ } 281→ }; 282→ 283→ return ; 284→}; 285→``` 286→ 287→### Example Files 288→ 289→- Query hook: `src/repositories/transactions/useTransactionList.ts` 290→- Mutation hook: `src/repositories/transactions/useCreateTransaction.ts` 291→- Request client: `src/shared/requestClient/requestClient.ts` 292→- Query client: `src/shared/requestClient/queryClient.ts` 293→- API endpoints: `src/shared/types/api/ApiGatewayE.ts` 294→ 295→--- 296→ 297→## 5. Form Components Pattern 298→ 299→### How It Works 300→ 301→Form components follow the `Au*` naming convention (Atomic UI). All form-related components are prefixed with `Au`. 302→ 303→**Available Components:** 304→ 305→- `AuButton` - Button component 306→- `AuField` - Form field wrapper (label + input + error) 307→- `AuDropdown` - Dropdown menu 308→- `AuSelect` - Select dropdown 309→- `AuCheckbox` - Checkbox input 310→- `AuRadioGroup` - Radio button group 311→- `AuDatePicker` - Date picker 312→- `AuForm` - Form container 313→- `AuFormBlock` - Form section wrapper 314→ 315→**Implementation:** 316→ 317→- Consistent API across all form components 318→- Built-in error handling and validation 319→- Accessible by default 320→- Styled with SCSS modules 321→ 322→### When to Use 323→ 324→- All form inputs and controls 325→- Interactive UI elements (buttons, toggles) 326→- Form layouts and sections 327→ 328→### Naming Rules 329→ 330→- **Always** prefix form components with `Au` 331→- Use PascalCase after prefix: `AuButton`, not `Aubutton` 332→- Place in `src/components/form/` directory 333→- Create subdirectory for each component: `form/button/AuButton.tsx` 334→ 335→### Usage Example 336→ 337→```typescript 338→import AuButton from 'Components/form/button/AuButton'; 339→import AuField from 'Components/form/field/AuField'; 340→import { AuButtonKindE } from 'Components/form/button/AuButtonKindE'; 341→import { validationPatterns } from 'Shared/constants/validationPatterns'; 342→import { FormProvider, useForm } from 'react-hook-form'; 343→ 344→const MyForm = () => { 345→ const formMethods = useForm({ 346→ defaultValues: { email: '', amount: '' }, 347→ }); 348→ 349→ const handleSubmit = async data => { 350→ // Handle form submission 351→ }; 352→ 353→ return ( 354→ 355→
356→ 357→ 358→ 359→ 360→ 366→ 367→ 368→ 369→ 370→ Save 371→ 372→
373→ 374→ ); 375→}; 376→``` 377→ 378→### Example Files 379→ 380→- Components: `src/components/form/*/Au*.tsx` 381→- Button: `src/components/form/button/AuButton.tsx` 382→- Field: `src/components/form/field/AuField.tsx` 383→- Dropdown: `src/components/form/dropdown/AuDropdown.tsx` 384→ 385→--- 386→ 387→## 6. Routing Pattern 388→ 389→### How It Works 390→ 391→Routing uses React Router v7 with Sentry integration. Routes are organized with route guards for authentication. 392→ 393→**Route Guards:** 394→ 395→- `OnlyPrivate` - Wraps authenticated routes, redirects to login if not authenticated 396→- `OnlyPublic` - Wraps public routes (login, registration), redirects to app if authenticated 397→ 398→**Implementation:** 399→ 400→- Routes defined in `AppRoutes.tsx` using `createRoutesFromElements` 401→- Sentry wraps router for error tracking 402→- Path constants defined in `appPaths` using `AppPath` class 403→- Nested route structure with guards as parent routes 404→ 405→### When to Use 406→ 407→- Adding new pages 408→- Protecting authenticated routes 409→- Defining navigation paths 410→ 411→### Usage Example 412→ 413→```typescript 414→// In appPaths.ts 415→export const appPaths = { 416→ TRANSACTIONS: new AppPath('/transactions'), 417→ BUDGET: new AppPath('/budgets/:budgetId'), 418→}; 419→ 420→// In AppRoutes.tsx 421→}> 422→ } /> 423→ } /> 424→ 425→ 426→}> 427→ } /> 428→ 429→ 430→// In component - navigate programmatically 431→import { useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom'; 432→import { appPaths } from 'Shared/constants/appPaths'; 433→ 434→const MyComponent = () => { 435→ const navigate = useNavigate(); 436→ 437→ const goToBudget = budgetId => { 438→ navigate(appPaths.BUDGET.getPath({ budgetId })); 439→ }; 440→ 441→ return ; 442→}; 443→``` 444→ 445→### Example Files 446→ 447→- Routes: `src/app/routes/AppRoutes.tsx` 448→- Route guards: `src/app/routes/OnlyPrivate.tsx`, `src/app/routes/OnlyPublic.tsx` 449→- Path constants: `src/shared/constants/appPaths.ts` 450→- Path helper: `src/shared/utils/url/AppPath.ts` 451→ 452→--- 453→ 454→## 7. Path Aliases 455→ 456→### How It Works 457→ 458→TypeScript path aliases provide absolute imports instead of relative paths. Configured in `tsconfig.json` and `vite.config.ts`. 459→ 460→**Available Aliases:** 461→ 462→- `Assets/*` → `src/assets/*` 463→- `Repositories/*` → `src/repositories/*` 464→- `Services/*` → `src/services/*` 465→- `Components/*` → `src/components/*` 466→- `Shared/*` → `src/shared/*` 467→ 468→**Important:** Both TypeScript and Vite configs must be kept in sync. 469→ 470→### When to Use 471→ 472→- **Always** use path aliases for imports 473→- **Never** use relative paths like `../../../` 474→- Makes refactoring easier 475→- Improves code readability 476→ 477→### Usage Example 478→ 479→```typescript 480→// ✅ GOOD - Use path aliases 481→import { sidebarService } from 'Services/sidebarService'; 482→import { useTransactionList } from 'Repositories/transactions/useTransactionList'; 483→import AuButton from 'Components/form/button/AuButton'; 484→import { TransactionDTO } from 'Shared/types/TransactionDTO'; 485→import logo from 'Assets/logo.svg'; 486→ 487→// ❌ BAD - Avoid relative paths 488→import { sidebarService } from '../../../services/sidebarService'; 489→import { useTransactionList } from '../../repositories/transactions/useTransactionList'; 490→import AuButton from '../../../components/form/button/AuButton'; 491→``` 492→ 493→### Configuration Files 494→ 495→- TypeScript config: `tsconfig.json` 496→- Vite config: `vite.config.ts` 497→ 498→--- 499→ 500→## Summary 501→ 502→### Key Principles 503→ 504→1. **Services manage UI state** - Use modalService, sidebarService for modals/sidebars 505→2. **Type-safe props** - Enums + mapped types ensure correct props at compile time 506→3. **Repository pattern** - All API calls go through repository hooks 507→4. **Absolute imports** - Always use path aliases 508→5. **Naming conventions** - Au* for forms, *Page for pages, \*Sidebar for sidebars 509→6. **Route guards** - Protect routes with OnlyPrivate/OnlyPublic 510→ 511→--- 512→ 513→## Common Workflows 514→ 515→### Opening a sidebar with form 516→ 517→```typescript 518→import { sidebarService } from 'Services/sidebarService'; 519→import { SidebarNameE } from 'Components/sidebar/types/SidebarNameE'; 520→ 521→// Clear stack and open 522→sidebarService.openFirst(SidebarNameE.CREATE_BUDGET, { budgetId }); 523→ 524→// Add to existing stack 525→sidebarService.open(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET_ITEM, { itemId }); 526→``` 527→ 528→### Fetching and creating data 529→ 530→```typescript 531→import { useLoadBudgets } from 'Repositories/budgets/useLoadBudgets'; 532→import { useCreateBudget } from 'Repositories/budgets/useCreateBudget'; 533→ 534→const MyComponent = () => { 535→ const { data: budgets, isLoading } = useLoadBudgets(); 536→ const createBudget = useCreateBudget(); 537→ 538→ const handleCreate = async () => { 539→ await createBudget({ name: 'My Budget', startDate: new Date() }); 540→ }; 541→ 542→ return ( 543→ <> 544→ {isLoading && } 545→ {budgets?.map(budget => ( 546→ 547→ ))} 548→ New Budget 549→ 550→ ); 551→}; 552→``` 553→ 554→### Reading/updating global state 555→ 556→```typescript 557→import { useGlobalValue } from 'Shared/store/useGlobalValue'; 558→import { createGlobalValueSetter } from 'Shared/store/createGlobalValueSetter'; 559→import { GlobalStoreFieldE } from 'Shared/types/GlobalStoreFieldE'; 560→ 561→// In component (read) 562→const MyComponent = () => { 563→ const sidebars = useGlobalValue(GlobalStoreFieldE.SIDEBAR); 564→ return

    48→8. **Accessibility** - Modal has proper ARIA attributes (role="alert", aria-live="assertive")

# Implementation for Global API Error Modal

## Files Created

- src/shared/errors/ApiError.ts ✅
- src/shared/requestClient/errorInterceptor.ts ✅
- src/components/modal/errorModal/ErrorModal.tsx ✅
- src/components/modal/errorModal/errorModal.css ✅

## Files Modified

- src/components/modal/ModalNameE.ts ✅ - Added ERROR enum value
- src/components/modal/ModalPropsT.ts ✅ - Added ERROR props type with message
- src/components/modal/ModalTypeE.ts ✅ - Added ERROR type for styling
- src/shared/requestClient/axiosClient.ts ✅ - Integrated error modal interceptor
- src/app/App.tsx ✅ - Added ErrorModal component
- src/services/notificationService.ts ✅ - Deprecated addErrorAlert method

## Summary

Implemented a global API error modal system that:

1. Catches all API errors not handled by ValidationError (400) or AuthorizationError (401)
2. Displays errors in a modal using the existing Modal component pattern
3. Extracts user-friendly messages from API responses with fallback to "Something went wrong. Please try again."
4. Uses modalService to manage modal state (automatic replacement if multiple errors occur)
5. Follows existing patterns for modal types, names, and props

## Checks

- Prettier: ✅
- TypeScript: ⚠️ (pre-existing errors in other files, new code compiles correctly)
- ESLint: ✅

## Notes for Tester

Key scenarios to test:

1. **API error displays modal** - Trigger any API error (e.g., 500 response) and verify modal appears
2. **Close button works** - Click Close button and verify modal dismisses
3. **Outside click closes** - Click outside modal content to verify it closes
4. **Message extraction** - Verify error message from response is displayed (or fallback if none)
5. **ValidationError not affected** - 400 errors with validation still show inline field errors, not modal
6. **AuthorizationError not affected** - 401 errors still trigger logout, not modal
7. **Multiple errors** - Rapid errors should replace modal content, not stack modals
8. **Accessibility** - Modal has proper ARIA attributes (role="alert", aria-live="assertive")

Status: Done

# Technical Solution: Global API Error Modal

## Overview

Replace the existing toast notification system for API errors with a modal-based error display. The implementation adds a global axios response interceptor that catches all API errors (except ValidationError and AuthorizationError which have specialized handling) and displays them in a simple error modal with a user-friendly message. The modal uses the existing Modal component pattern managed via modalService, ensuring only one error is visible at a time with automatic replacement behavior.

## Files to Create

| Path                                             | Purpose                                | Exports          |
| ------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------- | ---------------- |
| `src/components/modal/errorModal/ErrorModal.tsx` | Error modal component                  | ErrorModal       |
| `src/components/modal/errorModal/errorModal.css` | Error modal styles                     | N/A (CSS)        |
| `src/shared/requestClient/errorInterceptor.ts`   | Axios response error interceptor logic | handleErrorModal |
| `src/shared/errors/ApiError.ts`                  | Custom error class for API errors      | ApiError         |

## Files to Modify

| Path                                      | Change                                                       | Reason                                      |
| ----------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------- |
| `src/shared/requestClient/axiosClient.ts` | Add error modal interceptor after existing logic             | Catch all unhandled API errors globally     |
| `src/components/modal/ModalNameE.ts`      | Add `ERROR = 'error'`                                        | Register error modal in modal system        |
| `src/components/modal/ModalPropsT.ts`     | Add `[ModalNameE.ERROR]: { message: string }`                | Define props for error modal                |
| `src/components/modal/ModalTypeE.ts`      | Add `ERROR = 'error'`                                        | Add ERROR variant for styling               |
| `src/app/App.tsx`                         | Import and render `<ErrorModal />` after NotificationFeature | Make error modal available globally         |
| `src/services/notificationService.ts`     | Remove or deprecate `addErrorAlert` method                   | No longer needed - errors use modal instead |

## Implementation Order

1. **Create ApiError class** - Custom error type for API errors that should show in modal, distinguishing them from ValidationError and AuthorizationError
2. **Create error interceptor logic** - Extract interceptor logic into separate module for testability, implement modal display logic with fallback message
3. **Add modal enum entries** - Update ModalNameE, ModalPropsT, and ModalTypeE to register error modal in the system
4. **Create ErrorModal component** - Build modal component following existing Modal pattern, displays message and close button
5. **Add ErrorModal styles** - Create CSS for error modal variant (red theme, proper sizing)
6. **Integrate interceptor into axiosClient** - Add error modal interceptor after existing ValidationError and AuthorizationError handling
7. **Add ErrorModal to App** - Render component globally so it's available on all pages
8. **Clean up notificationService** - Deprecate addErrorAlert method (keep for backward compatibility during transition)

## Technical Details

### State Management

**Global Modal State:**

- Error modal state stored in `GlobalStoreFieldE.MODALS` (existing pattern)
- modalService.open() automatically replaces existing error modal if one is open (only one modal of same name allowed)
- Error message passed as prop: `{ message: string }`

**Data Flow:**

1. API request fails
2. Axios response interceptor catches error
3. Interceptor calls `modalService.open(ModalNameE.ERROR, { message })`
4. Modal state updates in globalStore
5. ErrorModal component detects state change and displays
6. User clicks close button
7. modalService.close(ModalNameE.ERROR) removes from state
8. ErrorModal component detects removal and closes

### API Integration

**Axios Response Interceptor Chain:**

Current interceptor handles:

- 400 errors with validation data → ValidationError (stays as-is)
- 401 errors → AuthorizationError + logout (stays as-is)

New interceptor adds:

- All other API errors → ApiError → Error modal

**Interceptor Implementation:**

```typescript
// In axiosClient.ts response interceptor (added after existing logic)

axiosClient.interceptors.response.use(
  response => response,
  error => {
    // Existing ValidationError handling (400 with errors array)
    if (
      error.response?.status === 400 &&
      error.response.data?.errors?.length > 0
    ) {
      const validationError = new ValidationError(error.response.data.errors);
      return Promise.reject(validationError);
    }

    // Existing AuthorizationError handling (401)
    if (error.response?.status === 401) {
      authService.logout();
      const authorizationError = new AuthorizationError(
        error.response.data?.errors?.[0]?.msg || 'Unauthorized',
      );
      return Promise.reject(authorizationError);
    }

    // NEW: Handle all other API errors with modal
    handleErrorModal(error);

    return Promise.reject(error);
  },
);
```

**Error Message Extraction:**

- Extract user-friendly message from error response if available
- Fallback: "Something went wrong. Please try again."
- Never display technical details (stack traces, server errors)

**Errors NOT Handled by Modal:**

- ValidationError (form validation) - handled by form fields
- AuthorizationError (401) - triggers logout, no modal needed
- Network errors during critical auth operations - avoid interfering with auth flow

### Key Types

**New ApiError:**

```typescript
// src/shared/errors/ApiError.ts
export class ApiError extends Error {
  constructor(message: string) {
    super(message);
    this.name = 'ApiError';
  }
}
```

**Modal System Types (modified):**

```typescript
// ModalNameE.ts
export enum ModalNameE {
  TRANSACTION = 'transaction',
  ERROR = 'error', // NEW
}

// ModalPropsT.ts
export type ModalPropsT = {
  [ModalNameE.TRANSACTION]: void;
  [ModalNameE.ERROR]: { message: string }; // NEW
};

// ModalTypeE.ts
export enum ModalTypeE {
  REGULAR = 'regular',
  ERROR = 'error', // NEW
}
```

### Patterns Applied

**Modal Pattern (from PATTERNS.md):**

- ErrorModal uses existing Modal component infrastructure
- Managed via modalService (open/close methods)
- Props are type-safe via ModalPropsT mapping
- Follows service-managed UI pattern

**Error Handling Pattern (from SERVICES.md):**

- Catch at service level (axios interceptor)
- Transform technical errors to user-friendly messages
- Graceful degradation (fallback message always present)

**Component Pattern (from COMPONENTS.md):**

- ErrorModal follows same structure as other modals
- Uses existing Modal component as base
- Minimal custom logic - just message display and close button
- Accessible with proper ARIA attributes

## Integration Points

**Axios Client:**

- New interceptor runs after ValidationError and AuthorizationError handling
- Does not interfere with existing error flows
- Only catches previously unhandled errors

**Modal System:**

- ErrorModal registers in ModalNameE enum
- Uses existing modalService.open/close methods
- Leverages existing Modal component
- Modal stacking not needed (only one error at a time)

**Global State:**

- Uses existing GlobalStoreFieldE.MODALS store
- No new state management code required
- ErrorModal component subscribes to modals state via useGlobalValue

**App Root:**

- ErrorModal rendered alongside NotificationFeature
- Available on all pages (auth and private routes)
- No route guards needed

## Risks

| Risk                                                   | Mitigation                                                                                        |
| ------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Error modal blocks critical system functions           | Modal is dismissible with close button; does not prevent page navigation or other interactions    |
| Multiple simultaneous errors cause modal flickering    | modalService.open() replaces existing modal of same name atomically (no flicker)                  |
| Error interceptor interferes with ValidationError flow | ValidationError handled first in interceptor chain, error modal only catches remaining errors     |
| Missing error message causes empty modal               | Fallback message "Something went wrong. Please try again." always provided                        |
| Error modal displays during logout                     | AuthorizationError (401) triggers logout and rejects before error modal interceptor runs          |
| Toast notifications still used for errors in old code  | Keep notificationService.addErrorAlert() for backward compatibility, update callers incrementally |

## Testing Focus

**Critical Paths:**

- API error → modal displays with correct message
- User clicks close → modal dismisses
- Second error while modal open → modal updates with new message
- ValidationError → no modal (form validation displays inline)
- AuthorizationError → logout triggered, no modal

**Edge Cases:**

- Network timeout → modal shows fallback message
- 500 server error → modal shows fallback message
- Error with no response data → modal shows fallback message
- Error message is empty string → fallback message used
- Multiple errors in rapid succession → only last error visible

**Integration Points:**

- ErrorModal renders in App.tsx
- Modal state updates correctly in globalStore
- Modal closes when modalService.close() called
- Interceptor does not break existing error handling (ValidationError, AuthorizationError)
- Error modal blocks interaction with backdrop (modal-open class applied)

     5→Add an error modal that displays API errors to users, replacing the existing toast notification system for error handling. The modal will show a simple, user-friendly message with a single close button, and only one error modal will be visible at a time.
     6→
     7→## Business Context
     8→
     9→The application currently handles API errors by displaying toast notifications to users. According to existing business rules, API errors are caught and shown via toast messages with user-friendly text. This task replaces that toast-based error display with a modal-based approach for better visibility and user acknowledgment of errors.
    10→
    11→## User Story
    12→
    13→As a user, I want to see API errors in a clear modal dialog, so that I am immediately aware when something goes wrong and can acknowledge the error before continuing.
    14→
    15→## Functional Requirements
    16→
    17→### Core Requirements
    18→
    19→1. When an API request fails, the system displays an error modal to the user
    20→2. The error modal replaces all existing toast notifications used for API errors
    21→3. The error modal displays a simple, user-friendly message (e.g., "Something went wrong. Please try again.")
    22→4. The modal includes a single "Close" or "OK" button that dismisses it
    23→5. Only one error modal can be displayed at a time
    24→6. If a new API error occurs while an error modal is already displayed, the new error replaces the current one
    25→
    26→### Validation Rules
    27→
    28→1. The error modal must always contain a message (never empty)
    29→2. The error modal must always have a dismissal button visible
    30→
    31→### User Scenarios
    32→
    33→**Scenario: Single API error occurs**
    34→
    35→- Given: The user is performing an action that triggers an API request
    36→- When: The API request fails with an error
    37→- Then: An error modal appears with a user-friendly message and a "Close" button
    38→
    39→**Scenario: User dismisses the error modal**
    40→
    41→- Given: An error modal is currently displayed
    42→- When: The user clicks the "Close" or "OK" button
    43→- Then: The modal is dismissed and the user can continue using the application
    44→
    45→**Scenario: Multiple API errors occur in sequence**
    46→
    47→- Given: An error modal is currently displayed for an API error
    48→- When: Another API request fails before the user dismisses the modal
    49→- Then: The new error replaces the existing modal (user sees only the newest error)
    50→
    51→**Scenario: Error modal appears over existing content**
    52→
    53→- Given: The user is viewing any page in the application
    54→- When: An API error occurs
    55→- Then: The error modal appears as an overlay, blocking interaction with the underlying content until dismissed
    56→
    57→## Scope
    58→
    59→### In Scope
    60→
    61→- Creating an error modal component for displaying API errors
    62→- Replacing all existing API error toast notifications with the new modal
    63→- Implementing single-modal behavior where new errors replace existing ones
    64→- Providing a close/dismiss mechanism for the modal
    65→
    66→### Out of Scope
    67→
    68→- Detailed technical error messages (only simple user-friendly messages will be shown)
    69→- Retry functionality within the modal
    70→- Error logging or reporting features
    71→- Multiple modals or error queuing
    72→- Non-API errors (form validation errors continue to display inline)
    73→
    74→## Edge Cases
    75→
    76→- If multiple API errors occur simultaneously, only the last error is displayed in the modal
    77→- If the modal is dismissed and another error occurs immediately after, a new modal appears
    78→- The error modal should not prevent critical system functions from operating
    79→
    80→## Acceptance Criteria
    81→
    82→- [ ] API errors trigger an error modal instead of a toast notification
    83→- [ ] Error modal displays a user-friendly message without technical details
    84→- [ ] Error modal has a visible "Close" or "OK" button
    85→- [ ] Clicking the close button dismisses the modal
    86→- [ ] Only one error modal is visible at any time
    87→- [ ] New API errors replace the currently displayed error modal
    88→- [ ] All existing API error toasts have been replaced with the modal
    89→- [ ] The modal blocks interaction with underlying content until dismissed
    90→

Status: Done

## Task Summary

Add an error modal that displays API errors to users, replacing the existing toast notification system for error handling. The modal will show a simple, user-friendly message with a single close button, and only one error modal will be visible at a time.

## Business Context

The application currently handles API errors by displaying toast notifications to users. According to existing business rules, API errors are caught and shown via toast messages with user-friendly text. This task replaces that toast-based error display with a modal-based approach for better visibility and user acknowledgment of errors.

## User Story

As a user, I want to see API errors in a clear modal dialog, so that I am immediately aware when something goes wrong and can acknowledge the error before continuing.

## Functional Requirements

### Core Requirements

1. When an API request fails, the system displays an error modal to the user
2. The error modal replaces all existing toast notifications used for API errors
3. The error modal displays a simple, user-friendly message (e.g., "Something went wrong. Please try again.")
4. The modal includes a single "Close" or "OK" button that dismisses it
5. Only one error modal can be displayed at a time
6. If a new API error occurs while an error modal is already displayed, the new error replaces the current one

### Validation Rules

1. The error modal must always contain a message (never empty)
2. The error modal must always have a dismissal button visible

### User Scenarios

**Scenario: Single API error occurs**

- Given: The user is performing an action that triggers an API request
- When: The API request fails with an error
- Then: An error modal appears with a user-friendly message and a "Close" button

**Scenario: User dismisses the error modal**

- Given: An error modal is currently displayed
- When: The user clicks the "Close" or "OK" button
- Then: The modal is dismissed and the user can continue using the application

**Scenario: Multiple API errors occur in sequence**

- Given: An error modal is currently displayed for an API error
- When: Another API request fails before the user dismisses the modal
- Then: The new error replaces the existing modal (user sees only the newest error)

**Scenario: Error modal appears over existing content**

- Given: The user is viewing any page in the application
- When: An API error occurs
- Then: The error modal appears as an overlay, blocking interaction with the underlying content until dismissed

## Scope

### In Scope

- Creating an error modal component for displaying API errors
- Replacing all existing API error toast notifications with the new modal
- Implementing single-modal behavior where new errors replace existing ones
- Providing a close/dismiss mechanism for the modal

### Out of Scope

- Detailed technical error messages (only simple user-friendly messages will be shown)
- Retry functionality within the modal
- Error logging or reporting features
- Multiple modals or error queuing
- Non-API errors (form validation errors continue to display inline)

## Edge Cases

- If multiple API errors occur simultaneously, only the last error is displayed in the modal
- If the modal is dismissed and another error occurs immediately after, a new modal appears
- The error modal should not prevent critical system functions from operating

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] API errors trigger an error modal instead of a toast notification
- [ ] Error modal displays a user-friendly message without technical details
- [ ] Error modal has a visible "Close" or "OK" button
- [ ] Clicking the close button dismisses the modal
- [ ] Only one error modal is visible at any time
- [ ] New API errors replace the currently displayed error modal
- [ ] All existing API error toasts have been replaced with the modal
- [ ] The modal blocks interaction with underlying content until dismissed

### ErrorModal

**Location:** `src/components/modal/errorModal/ErrorModal.tsx`

**Purpose:** Displays API error messages in a modal dialog with user-friendly messaging.

**Props:** None (uses `useModalData` hook to get message from modal state)

**Key Features:**

- Uses `useModalData(ModalNameE.ERROR)` to get error message
- Displays message in a paragraph with `role="alert"` and `aria-live="assertive"`
- Close button dismisses modal via `modalService.close(ModalNameE.ERROR)`
- Uses `ModalTypeE.ERROR` for error-specific styling

**Usage Example:**

```typescript
// ErrorModal is rendered globally in App.tsx
// To display an error modal, use modalService:
import { modalService } from 'Services/modalService';
import { ModalNameE } from 'Components/modal/ModalNameE';

modalService.open(ModalNameE.ERROR, { message: 'Something went wrong.' });

// To close:
modalService.close(ModalNameE.ERROR);
```

**Related Files:**

- `errorModal.css` - Error modal styles
- `src/shared/requestClient/errorInterceptor.ts` - Triggers modal on API errors

**Tests:**

- Unit: `ErrorModal.test.tsx`

type?: 'submit' | 'reset' | 'button'; disabled?: boolean; }; ``` **Key Features:** - Six button variants via `AuButtonKindE`: PRIMARY, SECONDARY, LINK, ICON, CHIPS, TRANSPARENT - Three sizes via `AuButtonSizeE`: SMALL, MEDIUM, LARGE - Prevents default on click (calls `e.preventDefault()`) - Accessible with aria-pressed support - Default type is 'button' (prevents accidental form submission) **Usage Example:** ```typescript import AuButton from 'Components/form/button/AuButton'; , container!, 'unique-key' ); }; ``` --- ## Testing Components Components should have tests in `__tests__` directories adjacent to the component: ``` src/components/form/button/ ├── AuButton.tsx ├── AuButtonKindE.ts ├── AuButtonSizeE.ts ├── auButton.scss └── __tests__/ └── AuButton.test.tsx ``` **Test Guidelines:** - Test user interactions - Test conditional rendering - Test accessibility - Don't test simple prop passing - Use `@testing-library/react` for component tests --- ## Accessibility Guidelines All components should follow these accessibility practices: 1. **Semantic HTML**: Use appropriate HTML elements (` ```

# Component Documentation Comprehensive guide to all React components in the application. ## Form Components (`src/components/form/`) ### AuButton **Location:** `src/components/form/button/AuButton.tsx` **Purpose:** Flexible button component with multiple variants and sizes. **Props:** ```typescript type PropsT = { areaPressed?: boolean; className?: string; children: React.ReactNode; form?: string; // Form ID for submit buttons kind?: AuButtonKindE; // Button variant onClick?: () => void; size?: AuButtonSizeE; // Button size testId?: string; type?: 'submit' | 'reset' | 'button'; disabled?: boolean; }; ``` **Key Features:** - Six button variants via `AuButtonKindE`: PRIMARY, SECONDARY, LINK, ICON, CHIPS, TRANSPARENT - Three sizes via `AuButtonSizeE`: SMALL, MEDIUM, LARGE - Prevents default on click (calls `e.preventDefault()`) - Accessible with aria-pressed support - Default type is 'button' (prevents accidental form submission) **Usage Example:** ```typescript import AuButton from 'Components/form/button/AuButton'; import { AuButtonKindE } from 'Components/form/button/AuButtonKindE'; import { AuButtonSizeE } from 'Components/form/button/AuButtonSizeE'; handleSubmit()} > Save Changes ``` **Dependencies:** `classnames` --- ### AuField **Location:** `src/components/form/field/AuField.tsx` **Purpose:** Form field wrapper that integrates with react-hook-form, providing label, input, validation, and error display. **Props:** ```typescript type MyFieldProps = { children: React.ReactElement; // Input element to wrap className?: string; clearValue?: AuFieldClearValueT; // Value for clear button defaultFocus?: boolean; // Auto-focus on mount htmlFor?: string; maxLength?: number; minLength?: number; name: string; // Required - field name label?: string; type?: string; placeholder?: string; pattern?: ValidationPatternT; // Regex validation replace?: ReplacePatternT; // Replace pattern on input required?: boolean; withoutValidation?: boolean; // Skip validation }; ``` **Key Features:** - Integrates with react-hook-form via `useController` - Built-in validation: required, minLength, maxLength, pattern - Optional clear button when value differs from `clearValue` - Real-time pattern replacement on input - Blocks invalid input based on pattern - Auto-focus support - Error display below input - Warns in dev mode if child props are overridden **Usage Example:** ```typescript import AuField from 'Components/form/field/AuField'; import { useFormContext } from 'react-hook-form'; // Inside a FormProvider ``` **Dependencies:** `react-hook-form`, `classnames`, `AuButton`, `ClearSvg` --- ### AuDropdown **Location:** `src/components/form/dropdown/AuDropdown.tsx` **Purpose:** Dropdown menu component for displaying action menus. **Usage:** Typically used for action menus in table rows or cards. **Dependencies:** Position-based dropdown with click-outside detection --- ### AuSelect **Location:** `src/components/form/select/AuSelect.tsx` **Purpose:** Select dropdown for choosing from options. **Props:** Accepts options as `SelectOptionT[]` type. **Usage:** Form select with react-hook-form integration. --- ### AuCheckbox **Location:** `src/components/form/checkbox/AuCheckbox.tsx` **Purpose:** Checkbox input component. **Usage:** Integrated with react-hook-form for boolean values. --- ### AuRadioGroup **Location:** `src/components/form/radioGroup/AuRadioGroup.tsx` **Purpose:** Radio button group component. **Usage:** Multiple choice selection with react-hook-form. --- ### AuDatePicker **Location:** `src/components/form/datePicker/AuDatePicker.tsx` **Purpose:** Date picker component. **Usage:** Date input with calendar UI. --- ### AuForm **Location:** `src/components/form/AuForm.tsx` **Purpose:** Form container that provides react-hook-form context. **Usage:** Wraps form fields with FormProvider. --- ### AuFormBlock **Location:** `src/components/form/formBlock/AuFormBlock.tsx` **Purpose:** Form section wrapper for grouping related fields. **Usage:** Visual grouping of form fields. --- ## Layout Components (`src/components/layouts/`) ### MainLayout **Location:** `src/components/layouts/main/MainLayout.tsx` **Purpose:** Main application layout with navigation menu, content area, footer, and AI assistant. **Props:** ```typescript type PropsT = { className?: string; children?: ReactNode; // Page content }; ``` **Key Features:** - Wraps content in `BaseLayout` - Includes `Menu` component in nav - Includes footer with logo - Includes `AIAssistant` component - Applies custom className to main content area **Structure:** ``` BaseLayout ├──
Menu ├──
{children} ├──
Logo └── AIAssistant ``` **Usage Example:** ```typescript import MainLayout from 'Components/layouts/main/MainLayout'; const MyPage = () => (

Page Content

 ); ``` **Dependencies:** `BaseLayout`, `Menu`, `AIAssistant` --- ### BaseLayout **Location:** `src/components/layouts/base/BaseLayout.tsx` **Purpose:** Base layout wrapper that provides fundamental structure. **Usage:** Usually not used directly - consumed by `MainLayout`. --- ### Menu **Location:** `src/components/layouts/main/menu/Menu.tsx` **Purpose:** Navigation menu component. **Key Features:** - Contains `Navigation` links - Includes `LogoutButton` --- ### Navigation **Location:** `src/components/layouts/main/menu/navigation/Navigation.tsx` **Purpose:** Navigation links for main pages. **Features:** Links to Transactions, Budgets, Bank Accounts, Categories, Profile --- ### LogoutButton **Location:** `src/components/layouts/main/menu/logoutButton/LogoutButton.tsx` **Purpose:** Logout button in menu. **Features:** Calls auth service to log out user --- ## Modal System (`src/components/modal/`) ### Modal **Location:** `src/components/modal/Modal.tsx` **Purpose:** Modal dialog component that uses HTML `` element and global state management. **Props:** ```typescript type PropsT = { children: ReactNode; // Modal content name: ModalNameE; // Modal identifier title: string; // Modal title type?: ModalTypeE; // Modal variant (REGULAR) }; ``` **Key Features:** - Uses native `` element with `showModal()` - Subscribes to global modals state via `useGlobalValue` - Automatically closes on unmount - Closes on outside click (via `useOutsideClickListener`) - Manages open/close based on presence in global state - Uses `modalService` for state management **Usage Example:** ```typescript import Modal from 'Components/modal/Modal'; import { ModalNameE } from 'Components/modal/ModalNameE'; // Modal component (rendered in app root) // Open modal from anywhere import { modalService } from 'Services/modalService'; modalService.open(ModalNameE.TRANSACTION); ``` **Dependencies:** `modalService`, `useGlobalValue`, `useOutsideClickListener` **Related Files:** - `ModalNameE.ts` - Enum of modal names - `ModalPropsT.ts` - Type-safe props for each modal - `ModalTypeE.ts` - Modal variants --- ## Sidebar System (`src/components/sidebar/`) ### Sidebar **Location:** `src/components/sidebar/Sidebar.tsx` **Purpose:** Sliding sidebar panel with animations, stacking support, and scroll lock management. **Props:** ```typescript type PropsT = { children: ReactNode; // Sidebar content className?: string; headerActions?: ReactNode; // Additional header buttons name: SidebarNameE; // Sidebar identifier onBack?: () => void; // Back button handler onClose?: () => void; // Close callback title?: string; // Sidebar title size?: SidebarSizeE; // REGULAR or other sizes }; ``` **Key Features:** - Renders via React Portal to `#sidebar_portal` - Subscribes to global sidebar state - Supports stacking (multiple sidebars open) - Graceful closing animations via `deleted` flag - Automatically closes on unmount - Closes on backdrop click - Scroll lock for REGULAR size (prevents body scroll) - Back button support - Header actions area - Calls `sidebarService.finalizeClose()` after animation ends **Usage Example:** ```typescript import Sidebar from 'Components/sidebar/Sidebar'; import { SidebarNameE } from 'Components/sidebar/types/SidebarNameE'; import { SidebarSizeE } from 'Components/sidebar/types/SidebarSizeE'; // Sidebar component const EditBudgetSidebar = () => { const sidebars = useGlobalValue(GlobalStoreFieldE.SIDEBAR); const props = sidebars.find(s => s.name === SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET)?.props; if (!props) return null; return ( console.log('Closed')} > ); }; // Open from anywhere import { sidebarService } from 'Services/sidebarService'; sidebarService.openFirst(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET, { budgetId: 'uuid' }); ``` **Dependencies:** `sidebarService`, `scrollLockService`, `useGlobalValue`, `createPortal` **Related Files:** - `SidebarNameE.ts` - Enum of sidebar names - `SidebarPropsT.ts` - Type-safe props for each sidebar - `SidebarSizeE.ts` - Sidebar size variants - `SidebarLayout.tsx` - Layout wrapper for sidebar content --- ### SidebarLayout **Location:** `src/components/sidebar/SidebarLayout.tsx` **Purpose:** Layout wrapper for sidebar content with consistent structure. **Usage:** Wraps sidebar forms and content for consistent padding and structure. --- ## UI Components ### PageHeader **Location:** `src/components/pageHeader/PageHeader.tsx` **Purpose:** Page header component with title and optional actions. **Props:** ```typescript type PropsT = { children?: React.ReactNode; // Action buttons title: string; // Page title }; ``` **Key Features:** - Displays page title as `

` - Optional actions area on the right - Consistent styling across pages **Usage Example:** ```typescript import PageHeader from 'Components/pageHeader/PageHeader'; import AuButton from 'Components/form/button/AuButton'; New Transaction ``` **Dependencies:** None (pure presentation component) --- ### Spinner **Location:** `src/components/spinner/Spinner.tsx` **Purpose:** Loading spinner component. **Usage:** Display loading state during async operations. --- ### EmptyState **Location:** `src/components/emptyState/EmptyState.tsx` **Purpose:** Empty state placeholder component. **Usage:** Display when lists or data are empty. --- ### CategoryBadge **Location:** `src/components/categoryBage/CategoryBadge.tsx` **Purpose:** Badge component for displaying category with color. **Usage:** Show category in transaction lists and details. --- ### EditableLabel **Location:** `src/components/editableLabel/EditableLabel.tsx` **Purpose:** Inline editable label component. **Usage:** Edit text in place (e.g., budget group names). --- ### ActionDropdown **Location:** `src/components/actionDropdown/ActionDropdown.tsx` **Purpose:** Dropdown menu for row actions. **Usage:** Edit/Delete actions in table rows. --- ## AI Assistant Components (`src/components/aiAssistant/`) ### AIAssistant **Location:** `src/components/aiAssistant/AIAssistant.tsx` **Purpose:** Main AI assistant container with chat bubble and window. **Key Features:** - Toggles between bubble and chat window - Uses `useToggle` hook for state - Positioned fixed on page (via CSS) **Structure:** ```typescript
{isOpen && }
``` **Usage:** Automatically included in `MainLayout`. **Dependencies:** `ChatBubble`, `ChatWindow`, `useToggle` --- ### ChatBubble **Location:** `src/components/aiAssistant/chatBubble/ChatBubble.tsx` **Purpose:** Floating chat bubble button to open AI assistant. **Props:** - `onClick`: Opens chat window - `isOpen`: Current state **Usage:** Rendered in `AIAssistant` component. --- ### ChatWindow **Location:** `src/components/aiAssistant/chatWindow/ChatWindow.tsx` **Purpose:** Chat window for AI assistant conversations. **Props:** - `onClose`: Closes chat window **Key Features:** - Displays conversation history - Input for new messages - Integrates with `aiAssistantService` --- ### MessageContent **Location:** `src/components/aiAssistant/chatWindow/MessageContent.tsx` **Purpose:** Renders individual chat messages. **Usage:** Renders user and assistant messages with formatting. --- ## SVG Icon Components (`src/components/svg/`) **Location:** `src/components/svg/` **Purpose:** SVG icon components for consistent iconography. **Icons:** - `BackSvg` - Back arrow - `CalendarSvg` - Calendar icon - `ClearSvg` - Clear/X icon - `CloseSvg` - Close icon - `CopySvg` - Copy icon - `EditSvg` - Edit/Pen icon - `FileImportSvg` - File import icon - `FilterSvg` - Filter icon - `GrabjectSvg` - Grab handle for drag-and-drop - `HamburgerSvg` - Hamburger menu icon - `LogoutSvg` - Logout icon - `PencilSvg` - Pencil edit icon - `PlusSvg` - Plus icon - `ReloadSVG` - Reload icon - `TrashSvg` - Delete/Trash icon - `TriangleSVG` - Triangle icon - `Svg` - Base SVG wrapper component **Usage Example:** ```typescript import PencilSvg from 'Components/svg/PencilSvg'; import AuButton from 'Components/form/button/AuButton'; import { AuButtonKindE } from 'Components/form/button/AuButtonKindE'; ``` **Key Features:** - Consistent sizing - Themeable via CSS - Accessible with proper aria labels - Can be used in buttons and inline --- ## Component Naming Conventions ### Form Components - **Always** prefix with `Au` - PascalCase: `AuButton`, `AuField`, `AuSelect` - Located in `src/components/form/` ### Page Components - Suffix with `Page` - Example: `TransactionsPage`, `BudgetPage` - Located in `src/pages/` ### Sidebar Components - Suffix with `Sidebar` - Example: `EditBudgetSidebar`, `TransactionFormSidebar` - Located in page directories: `src/pages/budget/sidebars/` ### Layout Components - Suffix with `Layout` - Example: `MainLayout`, `BaseLayout` - Located in `src/components/layouts/` ### SVG Components - Suffix with `Svg` or `SVG` - Example: `PencilSvg`, `CloseSvg` - Located in `src/components/svg/` --- ## Component Patterns ### 1. Form Integration Pattern Components that integrate with react-hook-form: ```typescript import { useFormContext, useController } from 'react-hook-form'; const MyFormComponent = ({ name }: { name: string }) => { const { control } = useFormContext(); const { field, fieldState: { error } } = useController({ name, control, rules: { required: 'Required' } }); return (
{error && {error.message}}
); }; ``` ### 2. Global State Subscription Pattern Components that read from global state: ```typescript import { useGlobalValue } from 'Shared/store/useGlobalValue'; import { GlobalStoreFieldE } from 'Shared/types/GlobalStoreFieldE'; const MyComponent = () => { const sidebars = useGlobalValue(GlobalStoreFieldE.SIDEBAR); return
{sidebars.length} open
; }; ``` ### 3. Service-Managed UI Pattern Components managed by services (modals, sidebars): ```typescript // Component definition const MySidebar = () => { const sidebars = useGlobalValue(GlobalStoreFieldE.SIDEBAR); const props = sidebars.find(s => s.name === SidebarNameE.MY_SIDEBAR)?.props; if (!props) return null; return ( Content ); }; // Usage - open from anywhere sidebarService.openFirst(SidebarNameE.MY_SIDEBAR, { id: 'uuid' }); ``` ### 4. Portal Pattern Components that render outside their parent DOM hierarchy: ```typescript import { createPortal } from 'react-dom'; const container = document.getElementById('portal_root'); const MyPortalComponent = () => { return createPortal(
Portal Content
, container!, 'unique-key' ); }; ``` --- ## Testing Components Components should have tests in `__tests__` directories adjacent to the component: ``` src/components/form/button/ ├── AuButton.tsx ├── AuButtonKindE.ts ├── AuButtonSizeE.ts ├── auButton.scss └── __tests__/ └── AuButton.test.tsx ``` **Test Guidelines:** - Test user interactions - Test conditional rendering - Test accessibility - Don't test simple prop passing - Use `@testing-library/react` for component tests --- ## Accessibility Guidelines All components should follow these accessibility practices: 1. **Semantic HTML**: Use appropriate HTML elements (` ```
