Services manage business logic, state, and side effects. 4→ 5→## Core Services 6→ 7→### authService 8→ 9→**Location:** `src/services/authService.ts` 10→ 11→**Purpose:** Manages user authentication state, token storage, and Sentry user tracking. 12→ 13→**Methods:** 14→ 15→#### `initialize()` 16→ 17→Initializes auth state on app startup by loading token from localStorage. 18→ 19→```typescript 20→authService.initialize(); 21→``` 22→ 23→**Behavior:** 24→ 25→- Reads `ACCESS_TOKEN` from localStorage 26→- If token exists, updates global auth state 27→- Sets Sentry user from token for error tracking 28→ 29→**When to call:** App initialization (in root component or App.tsx) 30→ 31→--- 32→ 33→#### `login(token: string)` 34→ 35→Stores auth token and updates global state after successful login. 36→ 37→```typescript 38→authService.login('eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...'); 39→``` 40→ 41→**Behavior:** 42→ 43→- Stores token in localStorage 44→- Updates global `AUTH_TOKEN` state 45→- Sets Sentry user from token 46→ 47→**When to call:** After successful login API response 48→ 49→--- 50→ 51→#### `logout()` 52→ 53→Clears auth state and cached data on logout. 54→ 55→```typescript 56→authService.logout(); 57→``` 58→ 59→**Behavior:** 60→ 61→- Removes token from localStorage 62→- Clears global `AUTH_TOKEN` state 63→- Clears all React Query cache 64→- Clears Sentry user data 65→ 66→**When to call:** User logout, session expiry, or unauthorized errors 67→ 68→--- 69→ 70→**Dependencies:** 71→ 72→- `localStorageService` - Token persistence 73→- `globalStore` - Auth state management 74→- `queryClient` - Cache clearing 75→- `sentryHelpers` - Error tracking 76→ 77→**Usage Example:** 78→ 79→```typescript 80→// On app start 81→useEffect(() => { 82→ authService.initialize(); 83→}, []); 84→ 85→// After login 86→const handleLogin = async (email: string, password: string) => { 87→ const { token } = await loginApi(email, password); 88→ authService.login(token); 89→ navigate('/dashboard'); 90→}; 91→ 92→// On logout 93→const handleLogout = () => { 94→ authService.logout(); 95→ navigate('/login'); 96→}; 97→``` 98→ 99→--- 100→ 101→### modalService 102→ 103→**Location:** `src/services/modalService.ts` 104→ 105→**Purpose:** Manages modal state globally using signals. See [PATTERNS.md](./PATTERNS.md#1-modal-pattern) for detailed pattern documentation. 106→ 107→**Methods:** 108→ 109→#### `open(modalName, props?)` 110→ 111→Opens a modal with optional typed props. 112→ 113→```typescript 114→modalService.open(ModalNameE.TRANSACTION, { transactionId: 'uuid' }); 115→``` 116→ 117→**Parameters:** 118→ 119→- `modalName` - Modal identifier from `ModalNameE` 120→- `props` - Optional props (type-safe based on modal name) 121→ 122→**Returns:** `modalName` 123→ 124→**Behavior:** 125→ 126→- Adds modal to top of stack 127→- Props are type-checked against `ModalPropsT` 128→ 129→--- 130→ 131→#### `close(modalName: ModalNameE)` 132→ 133→Closes a specific modal. 134→ 135→```typescript 136→modalService.close(ModalNameE.TRANSACTION); 137→``` 138→ 139→**Behavior:** 140→ 141→- Removes modal from global state 142→- Modal component detects removal and closes 143→ 144→--- 145→ 146→**Type Safety:** 147→ 148→```typescript 149→// ModalNameE defines available modals 150→export enum ModalNameE { 151→ TRANSACTION = 'transaction', 152→} 153→ 154→// ModalPropsT defines props for each modal 155→export type ModalPropsT = { 156→ [ModalNameE.TRANSACTION]: { transactionId: UuidT }; 157→}; 158→ 159→// TypeScript enforces correct props 160→modalService.open(ModalNameE.TRANSACTION, { transactionId: 'uuid' }); // ✅ 161→modalService.open(ModalNameE.TRANSACTION, { id: 'uuid' }); // ❌ Type error 162→``` 163→ 164→**Dependencies:** `globalStore`, `createGlobalValueSetter` 165→ 166→--- 167→ 168→### sidebarService 169→ 170→**Location:** `src/services/sidebarService.ts` 171→ 172→**Purpose:** Manages sidebar state with stacking and graceful animations. See [PATTERNS.md](./PATTERNS.md#2-sidebar-pattern) for detailed pattern documentation. 173→ 174→**Methods:** 175→ 176→#### `open(sidebarName, props?)` 177→ 178→Opens sidebar and adds to stack. Removes duplicate if already open. 179→ 180→```typescript 181→sidebarService.open(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET, { budgetId: 'uuid' }); 182→``` 183→ 184→**Behavior:** 185→ 186→- If sidebar already in stack, removes old instance 187→- Adds new instance to top of stack 188→- Multiple different sidebars can be open simultaneously 189→ 190→--- 191→ 192→#### `openFirst(sidebarName, props?)` 193→ 194→Opens sidebar and clears entire stack first. 195→ 196→```typescript 197→sidebarService.openFirst(SidebarNameE.CREATE_TRANSACTION); 198→``` 199→ 200→**Behavior:** 201→ 202→- Clears all open sidebars 203→- Opens only the specified sidebar 204→- Use when you want a clean state 205→ 206→--- 207→ 208→#### `close(sidebarName: SidebarNameE)` 209→ 210→Marks sidebar as deleted (triggers close animation). 211→ 212→```typescript 213→sidebarService.close(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET); 214→``` 215→ 216→**Behavior:** 217→ 218→- Sets `deleted: true` on sidebar 219→- Sidebar component detects change and plays close animation 220→- After animation, `finalizeClose()` removes from state 221→ 222→--- 223→ 224→#### `closeAll()` 225→ 226→Marks all open sidebars as deleted. 227→ 228→```typescript 229→sidebarService.closeAll(); 230→``` 231→ 232→**Behavior:** 233→ 234→- Sets `deleted: true` on all sidebars 235→- All sidebars animate closed simultaneously 236→ 237→--- 238→ 239→#### `finalizeClose(sidebarName: SidebarNameE)` 240→ 241→Removes sidebar from state after animation completes. 242→ 243→```typescript 244→sidebarService.finalizeClose(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET); 245→``` 246→ 247→**Behavior:** 248→ 249→- Removes sidebar from global state 250→- **Called automatically by Sidebar component** after animation ends 251→- **Do not call manually** unless you have a specific reason 252→ 253→--- 254→ 255→**Type Safety:** 256→ 257→```typescript 258→// SidebarNameE defines available sidebars 259→export enum SidebarNameE { 260→ EDIT_BUDGET = 'editBudget', 261→ CREATE_TRANSACTION = 'createTransaction', 262→} 263→ 264→// SidebarPropsT defines props for each sidebar 265→export type SidebarPropsT = { 266→ [SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET]: { budgetId: UuidT }; 267→ [SidebarNameE.CREATE_TRANSACTION]: void; 268→}; 269→ 270→// TypeScript enforces correct props 271→sidebarService.open(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET, { budgetId: 'uuid' }); // ✅ 272→sidebarService.open(SidebarNameE.CREATE_TRANSACTION); // ✅ 273→sidebarService.open(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET); // ❌ Missing required props 274→``` 275→ 276→**Dependencies:** `globalStore`, `createGlobalValueSetter` 277→ 278→--- 279→ 280→### notificationService 281→ 282→**Location:** `src/services/notificationService.ts` 283→ 284→**Purpose:** Manages toast notifications with auto-dismiss timers. 285→ 286→**Methods:** 287→ 288→#### `addAlert(content: ReactNode, showTime?: number)` 289→ 290→Adds an info notification. 291→ 292→```typescript 293→notificationService.addAlert('Budget saved successfully!'); 294→notificationService.addAlert(
Custom content
, 3000); 295→``` 296→ 297→**Parameters:** 298→ 299→- `content` - Notification message (string or React element) 300→- `showTime` - Duration in milliseconds (default: 5000ms) 301→ 302→**Returns:** `notificationId` (number) 303→ 304→**Behavior:** 305→ 306→- Adds notification to global state 307→- Auto-increments ID 308→- Sets type to `NotificationTypeE.INFO` 309→ 310→--- 311→ 312→#### `removeAlert(id: number)` 313→ 314→Marks notification for removal (starts hide animation). 315→ 316→```typescript 317→notificationService.removeAlert(notificationId); 318→``` 319→ 320→**Behavior:** 321→ 322→- Sets `hideAt` timestamp 323→- Notification component detects and animates out 324→ 325→--- 326→ 327→#### `clearAlert(id: number)` 328→ 329→Marks notification as deleted and cleans up store. 330→ 331→```typescript 332→notificationService.clearAlert(notificationId); 333→``` 334→ 335→**Behavior:** 336→ 337→- Sets `deleted: true` 338→- Calls `clearStore()` to remove all deleted notifications 339→ 340→--- 341→ 342→#### `clearStore()` 343→ 344→Removes all deleted notifications from state. 345→ 346→```typescript 347→notificationService.clearStore(); 348→``` 349→ 350→**Behavior:** 351→ 352→- If no non-deleted notifications remain, clears entire array 353→- Prevents memory leaks from accumulating notifications 354→ 355→--- 356→ 357→#### `addErrorAlert(message: string)` ⚠️ DEPRECATED 358→ 359→**Deprecated:** Use `modalService.open(ModalNameE.ERROR, { message })` instead. 360→ 361→Logs error to console. Previously used for error toast notifications, now replaced by error modal system. 362→ 363→```typescript 364→// ❌ Deprecated - do not use for new code 365→notificationService.addErrorAlert('Failed to save budget'); 366→ 367→// ✅ Use error modal instead 368→import { modalService } from 'Services/modalService'; 369→import { ModalNameE } from 'Components/modal/ModalNameE'; 370→modalService.open(ModalNameE.ERROR, { message: 'Failed to save budget' }); 371→``` 372→ 373→**Note:** Method kept for backward compatibility. Will be removed in future version. 374→ 375→--- 376→ 377→**Usage Example:** 378→ 379→```typescript 380→// Success notification 381→const handleSave = async () => { 382→ await saveBudget(); 383→ notificationService.addAlert('Budget saved!'); 384→}; 385→ 386→// Custom duration 387→const handleDelete = async () => { 388→ await deleteBudget(); 389→ notificationService.addAlert('Budget deleted', 3000); 390→}; 391→ 392→// Manual control 393→const notificationId = notificationService.addAlert('Processing...'); 394→// ... later 395→notificationService.removeAlert(notificationId); 396→``` 397→ 398→**Dependencies:** `globalStore`, `createGlobalValueSetter` 399→ 400→--- 401→ 402→### scrollLockService 403→ 404→**Location:** `src/services/scrollLockService.ts` 405→ 406→**Purpose:** Prevents body scroll when modals or sidebars are open. Uses reference counting to support multiple locks. 407→ 408→**Methods:** 409→ 410→#### `scrollLockManager.add(source: string)` 411→ 412→Adds a scroll lock from a specific source. 413→ 414→```typescript 415→scrollLockManager.add('my-sidebar'); 416→``` 417→ 418→**Behavior:** 419→ 420→- Adds source to internal Set 421→- If first lock, adds `modal-open` class to `` 422→- `modal-open` class applies `overflow: hidden` via CSS 423→ 424→--- 425→ 426→#### `scrollLockManager.remove(source: string)` 427→ 428→Removes a scroll lock from a specific source. 429→ 430→```typescript 431→scrollLockManager.remove('my-sidebar'); 432→``` 433→ 434→**Behavior:** 435→ 436→- Removes source from internal Set 437→- If last lock removed, removes `modal-open` class from `` 438→- Body scroll is restored 439→ 440→--- 441→ 442→**How It Works:** 443→ 444→```typescript 445→// Multiple sources can lock scroll 446→scrollLockManager.add('modal-1'); // Body locked 447→scrollLockManager.add('modal-2'); // Still locked 448→scrollLockManager.remove('modal-1'); // Still locked (modal-2 active) 449→scrollLockManager.remove('modal-2'); // Body unlocked 450→``` 451→ 452→**Usage Example:** 453→ 454→```typescript 455→// In Sidebar component 456→useEffect(() => { 457→ if (isOpen) { 458→ scrollLockManager.add('my-sidebar'); 459→ 460→ return () => { 461→ scrollLockManager.remove('my-sidebar'); 462→ }; 463→ } 464→}, [isOpen]); 465→``` 466→ 467→**Note:** Sidebar component automatically manages scroll lock. You rarely need to call this directly. 468→ 469→**Dependencies:** None (pure DOM manipulation) 470→ 471→--- 472→ 473→## AI Assistant Services 474→ 475→### aiAssistantService 476→ 477→**Location:** `src/services/aiAssistant/aiAssistantService.ts` 478→ 479→**Purpose:** Communicates with AI chat backend API. 480→ 481→**Methods:** 482→ 483→#### `sendMessage(message: string, conversationHistory?: AIMessageDTO[])` 484→ 485→Sends a message to AI and returns response. 486→ 487→```typescript 488→const response = await aiAssistantService.sendMessage( 489→ 'How much did I spend on groceries?', 490→ conversationHistory, 491→); 492→``` 493→ 494→**Parameters:** 495→ 496→- `message` - User's message 497→- `conversationHistory` - Previous messages (optional, default: `[]`) 498→ 499→**Returns:** `Promise` 500→ 501→```typescript 502→type AIResponseT = { 503→ message: string; // AI response 504→ conversationId?: string; 505→}; 506→``` 507→ 508→**Error Handling:** 509→ 510→- Timeout (120s): Throws `'AI request timed out. Please try again.'` 511→- Server error (500): Throws `'AI service error. Please try again later.'` 512→- Other errors: Re-throws original error 513→ 514→**Usage Example:** 515→ 516→```typescript 517→try { 518→ const response = await aiAssistantService.sendMessage( 519→ userInput, 520→ previousMessages, 521→ ); 522→ 523→ addMessageToChat(response.message); 524→} catch (error) { 525→ console.error('AI error:', error); 526→ notificationService.addErrorAlert(error.message); 527→} 528→``` 529→ 530→--- 531→ 532→#### `healthCheck()` 533→ 534→Checks AI service health and configuration. 535→ 536→```typescript 537→const health = await aiAssistantService.healthCheck(); 538→``` 539→ 540→**Returns:** 541→ 542→```typescript 543→type HealthResponse = { 544→ status: 'ok' | 'error'; 545→ ollamaConnected: boolean; 546→ model: string; 547→ availableModels?: string[]; 548→ error?: string; 549→}; 550→``` 551→ 552→**Usage Example:** 553→ 554→```typescript 555→const checkAI = async () => { 556→ const health = await aiAssistantService.healthCheck(); 557→ 558→ if (health.status === 'error') { 559→ console.warn('AI unavailable:', health.error); 560→ } else { 561→ console.log('AI model:', health.model); 562→ } 563→}; 564→``` 565→ 566→**Dependencies:** `axiosClient`, `ApiGatewayE` 567→ 568→--- 569→ 570→### aiConversationStorageService 571→ 572→**Location:** `src/services/aiAssistant/aiConversationStorageService.ts` 573→ 574→**Purpose:** Persists AI conversation history to localStorage. 575→ 576→**Methods:** 577→ 578→#### `save(messages: AIMessageT[])` 579→ 580→Saves conversation to localStorage. 581→ 582→```typescript 583→aiConversationStorageService.save(conversationMessages); 584→``` 585→ 586→**Behavior:** 587→ 588→- Serializes messages (converts Date to ISO string) 589→- Stores in localStorage under `AI_CONVERSATION_STORAGE` 590→- Catches and logs errors silently 591→ 592→--- 593→ 594→#### `load()` 595→ 596→Loads conversation from localStorage. 597→ 598→```typescript 599→const messages = aiConversationStorageService.load(); 600→``` 601→ 602→**Returns:** `AIMessageT[]` (empty array if none found) 603→ 604→**Behavior:** 605→ 606→- Deserializes messages (converts ISO string to Date) 607→- Returns empty array on error or missing data 608→ 609→--- 610→ 611→#### `clear()` 612→ 613→Clears conversation from localStorage. 614→ 615→```typescript 616→aiConversationStorageService.clear(); 617→``` 618→ 619→**Behavior:** 620→ 621→- Removes conversation from localStorage 622→- Catches and logs errors silently 623→ 624→--- 625→ 626→**Message Format:** 627→ 628→```typescript 629→type AIMessageT = { 630→ id: string; 631→ role: AIRoleE; // 'user' | 'assistant' 632→ content: string; 633→ timestamp: Date; 634→}; 635→``` 636→ 637→**Usage Example:** 638→ 639→```typescript 640→// Save after each message 641→const handleSendMessage = async (text: string) => { 642→ const newMessage: AIMessageT = { 643→ id: generateId(), 644→ role: AIRoleE.USER, 645→ content: text, 646→ timestamp: new Date(), 647→ }; 648→ 649→ const updatedMessages = [...messages, newMessage]; 650→ aiConversationStorageService.save(updatedMessages); 651→}; 652→ 653→// Load on mount 654→useEffect(() => { 655→ const history = aiConversationStorageService.load(); 656→ setMessages(history); 657→}, []); 658→ 659→// Clear on logout or reset 660→const handleClearChat = () => { 661→ aiConversationStorageService.clear(); 662→ setMessages([]); 663→}; 664→``` 665→ 666→**Dependencies:** `localStorageService` 667→ 668→--- 669→ 670→## Storage Services 671→ 672→### localStorageService 673→ 674→**Location:** `src/services/localStorage/localStorageService.ts` 675→ 676→**Purpose:** Type-safe wrapper for browser localStorage with JSON serialization. 677→ 678→**Methods:** 679→ 680→#### `set(name: T, value: LocalStorageT[T])` 681→ 682→Stores value in localStorage. 683→ 684→```typescript 685→localStorageService.set(LocalStorageNameE.ACCESS_TOKEN, 'token-value'); 686→``` 687→ 688→**Behavior:** 689→ 690→- Serializes value to JSON 691→- Stores in localStorage 692→- Ignores `undefined` and `null` values 693→ 694→--- 695→ 696→#### `get(name: T)` 697→ 698→Retrieves value from localStorage. 699→ 700→```typescript 701→const token = localStorageService.get(LocalStorageNameE.ACCESS_TOKEN); 702→``` 703→ 704→**Returns:** `LocalStorageT[T] | null` 705→ 706→**Behavior:** 707→ 708→- Parses JSON from localStorage 709→- Returns `null` if not found 710→- On parse error, removes corrupted item and returns `null` 711→ 712→--- 713→ 714→#### `remove(name: T)` 715→ 716→Removes value from localStorage. 717→ 718→```typescript 719→localStorageService.remove(LocalStorageNameE.ACCESS_TOKEN); 720→``` 721→ 722→--- 723→ 724→**Type Safety:** 725→ 726→```typescript 727→// LocalStorageNameE defines keys 728→export enum LocalStorageNameE { 729→ ACCESS_TOKEN = 'accessToken', 730→ AI_CONVERSATION_STORAGE = 'aiConversationStorage', 731→} 732→ 733→// LocalStorageT maps keys to value types 734→export type LocalStorageT = { 735→ [LocalStorageNameE.ACCESS_TOKEN]: string; 736→ [LocalStorageNameE.AI_CONVERSATION_STORAGE]: AIMessageStorageT[]; 737→}; 738→ 739→// TypeScript ensures correct types 740→const token: string | null = localStorageService.get( 741→ LocalStorageNameE.ACCESS_TOKEN, 742→); 743→``` 744→ 745→**Usage Example:** 746→ 747→```typescript 748→// Store user preference 749→localStorageService.set(LocalStorageNameE.THEME, 'dark'); 750→ 751→// Retrieve on app start 752→const theme = localStorageService.get(LocalStorageNameE.THEME) || 'light'; 753→ 754→// Clear on logout 755→localStorageService.remove(LocalStorageNameE.USER_PREFERENCES); 756→``` 757→ 758→**Dependencies:** `jsonHelpers` (custom JSON parse/stringify with error handling) 759→ 760→--- 761→ 762→### cookieService 763→ 764→**Location:** `src/services/cookieService.ts` 765→ 766→**Purpose:** Type-safe wrapper for browser cookies using `universal-cookie` library. 767→ 768→**Methods:** 769→ 770→#### `set(name: T, value: CookieT[T], days?: number)` 771→ 772→Stores value in cookie with expiration. 773→ 774→```typescript 775→cookieService.set(CookieNameE.USER_ID, 'user-123', 30); 776→``` 777→ 778→**Parameters:** 779→ 780→- `name` - Cookie name from `CookieNameE` 781→- `value` - Cookie value (type-safe) 782→- `days` - Expiration in days (default: 180) 783→ 784→**Behavior:** 785→ 786→- Sets cookie with expiration date 787→- Path is always `/` (available site-wide) 788→ 789→--- 790→ 791→#### `get(name: T)` 792→ 793→Retrieves cookie value. 794→ 795→```typescript 796→const userId = cookieService.get(CookieNameE.USER_ID); 797→``` 798→ 799→**Returns:** `CookieT[T] | undefined` 800→ 801→--- 802→ 803→#### `remove(name: T)` 804→ 805→Removes cookie. 806→ 807→```typescript 808→cookieService.remove(CookieNameE.USER_ID); 809→``` 810→ 811→--- 812→ 813→**Type Safety:** 814→ 815→```typescript 816→// CookieNameE defines cookie names 817→export enum CookieNameE { 818→ USER_ID = 'userId', 819→ PREFERENCES = 'preferences', 820→} 821→ 822→// CookieT maps names to value types 823→export type CookieT = { 824→ [CookieNameE.USER_ID]: string; 825→ [CookieNameE.PREFERENCES]: PreferencesT; 826→}; 827→``` 828→ 829→**Usage Example:** 830→ 831→```typescript 832→// Remember user preference 833→cookieService.set(CookieNameE.LANGUAGE, 'en', 365); 834→ 835→// Read on page load 836→const language = cookieService.get(CookieNameE.LANGUAGE) || 'en'; 837→ 838→// Clear on logout 839→cookieService.remove(CookieNameE.SESSION_ID); 840→``` 841→ 842→**Dependencies:** `universal-cookie` 843→ 844→--- 845→ 846→## Service Patterns 847→ 848→### Pattern 1: Global State Services 849→ 850→Services that manage global state using signals. 851→ 852→**Services:** `authService`, `modalService`, `sidebarService`, `notificationService` 853→ 854→**Pattern:** 855→ 856→```typescript 857→import { createGlobalValueSetter } from 'Shared/store/createGlobalValueSetter'; 858→import { GlobalStoreFieldE } from 'Shared/types/GlobalStoreFieldE'; 859→ 860→const updateField = createGlobalValueSetter(GlobalStoreFieldE.MY_FIELD); 861→ 862→export const myService = { 863→ add: item => { 864→ updateField(current => [...current, item]); 865→ }, 866→ remove: id => { 867→ updateField(current => current.filter(i => i.id !== id)); 868→ }, 869→}; 870→``` 871→ 872→**Why:** 873→ 874→- Centralized state management 875→- Type-safe updates 876→- Reactive - components re-render automatically 877→- Service logic separated from UI 878→ 879→--- 880→ 881→### Pattern 2: Storage Services 882→ 883→Services that wrap browser storage APIs with type safety. 884→ 885→**Services:** `localStorageService`, `cookieService` 886→ 887→**Pattern:** 888→ 889→```typescript 890→export const storageService = { 891→ set(name: T, value: TypeMap[T]) { 892→ // Type-safe set 893→ }, 894→ get(name: T): TypeMap[T] | null { 895→ // Type-safe get with return type inference 896→ }, 897→ remove(name: T) { 898→ // Type-safe remove 899→ }, 900→}; 901→``` 902→ 903→**Benefits:** 904→ 905→- TypeScript ensures correct types for each key 906→- Centralized storage logic 907→- Easy to add new storage keys 908→- Consistent error handling 909→ 910→--- 911→ 912→### Pattern 3: API Services 913→ 914→Services that wrap API calls with error handling. 915→ 916→**Services:** `aiAssistantService` 917→ 918→**Pattern:** 919→ 920→```typescript 921→export const apiService = { 922→ async operation(params): Promise { 923→ try { 924→ const response = await axiosClient.post(endpoint, params); 925→ return response.data; 926→ } catch (error) { 927→ if (error instanceof AxiosError) { 928→ // Handle specific errors 929→ if (error.code === 'ECONNABORTED') { 930→ throw new Error('User-friendly message'); 931→ } 932→ } 933→ throw error; // Re-throw unknown errors 934→ } 935→ }, 936→}; 937→``` 938→ 939→**Benefits:** 940→ 941→- Consistent error handling 942→- User-friendly error messages 943→- Timeout handling 944→- Type-safe responses 945→ 946→--- 947→ 948→## Error Handling 949→ 950→### General Principles 951→ 952→1. **Catch at service level** - Services should catch and transform errors 953→2. **User-friendly messages** - Convert technical errors to readable messages 954→3. **Log for debugging** - Console errors in development 955→4. **Graceful degradation** - Return sensible defaults when possible 956→ 957→### Examples 958→ 959→**Storage Services:** 960→ 961→```typescript 962→try { 963→ return JSON.parse(data); 964→} catch { 965→ // Remove corrupted data and return null 966→ localStorage.removeItem(key); 967→ return null; 968→} 969→``` 970→ 971→**API Services:** 972→ 973→```typescript 974→catch (error: unknown) { 975→ if (error instanceof AxiosError) { 976→ if (error.code === 'ECONNABORTED') { 977→ throw new Error('Request timed out'); 978→ } 979→ if (error.response?.status === 500) { 980→ throw new Error('Server error'); 981→ } 982→ } 983→ throw error; // Unknown errors re-thrown 984→} 985→``` 986→ 987→**Notification:** 988→ 989→```typescript 990→try { 991→ await riskyOperation(); 992→ notificationService.addAlert('Success!'); 993→} catch (error) { 994→ notificationService.addErrorAlert(error.message); 995→} 996→``` 997→ 998→--- 999→ 1000→## Testing Services 1001→ 1002→Services should have tests in `__tests__` directories: 1003→ 1004→``` 1005→src/services/ 1006→ ├── authService.ts 1007→ ├── authService.test.ts 1008→ ├── modalService.ts 1009→ └── modalService.test.ts 1010→``` 1011→ 1012→**Test Guidelines:** 1013→ 1014→- Test each public method 1015→- Test error cases 1016→- Mock dependencies (storage, API calls) 1017→- Test state changes for state-management services 1018→ 1019→**Example:** 1020→ 1021→```typescript 1022→describe('authService', () => { 1023→ it('should initialize with token from localStorage', () => { 1024→ localStorageService.set(LocalStorageNameE.ACCESS_TOKEN, 'token-123'); 1025→ 1026→ authService.initialize(); 1027→ 1028→ expect(globalStore[GlobalStoreFieldE.AUTH_TOKEN].value).toBe('token-123'); 1029→ }); 1030→ 1031→ it('should clear cache on logout', () => { 1032→ authService.logout(); 1033→ 1034→ expect(queryClient.clear).toHaveBeenCalled(); 1035→ }); 1036→}); 1037→``` 1038→ 1039→--- 1040→ 1041→## Service Dependencies 1042→ 1043→``` 1044→authService 1045→ ├── localStorageService 1046→ ├── globalStore 1047→ ├── queryClient 1048→ └── sentryHelpers 1049→ 1050→modalService 1051→ └── globalStore 1052→ 1053→sidebarService 1054→ └── globalStore 1055→ 1056→notificationService 1057→ └── globalStore 1058→ 1059→scrollLockService 1060→ └── (none - pure DOM) 1061→ 1062→aiAssistantService 1063→ └── axiosClient 1064→ 1065→aiConversationStorageService 1066→ └── localStorageService 1067→ 1068→localStorageService 1069→ └── jsonHelpers 1070→ 1071→cookieService 1072→ └── universal-cookie (external) 1073→``` 1074→

## Request Client Utilities

### errorInterceptor

**Location:** `src/shared/requestClient/errorInterceptor.ts`

**Purpose:** Global API error handling via axios interceptor. Displays user-friendly error messages in a modal for all API errors not handled by ValidationError or AuthorizationError.

**Exports:**

#### `handleErrorModal(error: AxiosError)`

Opens an error modal with a user-friendly message extracted from the API response.

```typescript
import { handleErrorModal } from 'Shared/requestClient/errorInterceptor';

// Called automatically by axios interceptor
handleErrorModal(error);
```

**Message Extraction Logic:**

1. First tries `response.data.message` (string)
2. Falls back to `response.data.errors[0].msg` (validation error format)
3. Falls back to `'Something went wrong. Please try again.'`

**Integration:**

The interceptor is integrated in `axiosClient.ts` after ValidationError (400) and AuthorizationError (401) handlers:

```typescript
// In axiosClient.ts response interceptor
axiosClient.interceptors.response.use(
  response => response,
  error => {
    // Existing ValidationError handling (400 with errors array)
    // Existing AuthorizationError handling (401)

    // NEW: Handle all other API errors with modal
    handleErrorModal(error);

    return Promise.reject(error);
  },
);
```

**Errors NOT Handled:**

- ValidationError (400 with validation errors) - handled by form fields
- AuthorizationError (401) - triggers logout, no modal
- Network errors during critical auth operations

**Dependencies:** `modalService`, `ModalNameE`

**Tests:**

- Unit: `errorInterceptor.test.ts` (9 tests)

---

## Service Dependencies

```
authService
  ├── localStorageService
  ├── globalStore
  ├── queryClient
  └── sentryHelpers

, 3000); 295→``` 296→ 297→**Parameters:** 298→ 299→- `content` - Notification message (string or React element) 300→- `showTime` - Duration in milliseconds (default: 5000ms) 301→ 302→**Returns:** `notificationId` (number) 303→ 304→**Behavior:** 305→ 306→- Adds notification to global state 307→- Auto-increments ID 308→- Sets type to `NotificationTypeE.INFO` 309→ 310→--- 311→ 312→#### `removeAlert(id: number)` 313→ 314→Marks notification for removal (starts hide animation). 315→ 316→```typescript 317→notificationService.removeAlert(notificationId); 318→``` 319→ 320→**Behavior:** 321→ 322→- Sets `hideAt` timestamp 323→- Notification component detects and animates out 324→ 325→--- 326→ 327→#### `clearAlert(id: number)` 328→ 329→Marks notification as deleted and cleans up store. 330→ 331→```typescript 332→notificationService.clearAlert(notificationId); 333→``` 334→ 335→**Behavior:** 336→ 337→- Sets `deleted: true` 338→- Calls `clearStore()` to remove all deleted notifications 339→ 340→--- 341→ 342→#### `clearStore()` 343→ 344→Removes all deleted notifications from state. 345→ 346→```typescript 347→notificationService.clearStore(); 348→``` 349→ 350→**Behavior:** 351→ 352→- If no non-deleted notifications remain, clears entire array 353→- Prevents memory leaks from accumulating notifications 354→ 355→--- 356→ 357→#### `addErrorAlert(message: string)` ⚠️ DEPRECATED 358→ 359→**Deprecated:** Use `modalService.open(ModalNameE.ERROR, { message })` instead. 360→ 361→Logs error to console. Uses reference counting to support multiple locks. 407→ 408→**Methods:** 409→ 410→#### `scrollLockManager.add(source: string)` 411→ 412→Adds a scroll lock from a specific source. 413→ 414→```typescript 415→scrollLockManager.add('my-sidebar'); 416→``` 417→ 418→**Behavior:** 419→ 420→- Adds source to internal Set 421→- If first lock, adds `modal-open` class to `` 422→- `modal-open` class applies `overflow: hidden` via CSS 423→ 424→--- 425→ 426→#### `scrollLockManager.remove(source: string)` 427→ 428→Removes a scroll lock from a specific source. 429→ 430→```typescript 431→scrollLockManager.remove('my-sidebar'); 432→``` 433→ 434→**Behavior:** 435→ 436→- Removes source from internal Set 437→- If last lock removed, removes `modal-open` class from `` 438→- Body scroll is restored 439→ 440→--- 441→ 442→**How It Works:** 443→ 444→```typescript 445→// Multiple sources can lock scroll 446→scrollLockManager.add('modal-1'); // Body locked 447→scrollLockManager.add('modal-2'); // Still locked 448→scrollLockManager.remove('modal-1'); // Still locked (modal-2 active) 449→scrollLockManager.remove('modal-2'); // Body unlocked 450→``` 451→ 452→**Usage Example:** 453→ 454→```typescript 455→// In Sidebar component 456→useEffect(() => { 457→ if (isOpen) { 458→ scrollLockManager.add('my-sidebar'); 459→ 460→ return () => { 461→ scrollLockManager.remove('my-sidebar'); 462→ }; 463→ } 464→}, [isOpen]); 465→``` 466→ 467→**Note:** Sidebar component automatically manages scroll lock. 865→ }, 866→ remove: id => { 867→ updateField(current => current.filter(i => i.id !== id)); 868→ }, 869→}; 870→``` 871→ 872→**Why:** 873→ 874→- Centralized state management 875→- Type-safe updates 876→- Reactive - components re-render automatically 877→- Service logic separated from UI 878→ 879→--- 880→ 881→### Pattern 2: Storage Services 882→ 883→Services that wrap browser storage APIs with type safety. 884→ 885→**Services:** `localStorageService`, `cookieService` 886→ 887→**Pattern:** 888→ 889→```typescript 890→export const storageService = { 891→ set(name: T, value: TypeMap[T]) { 892→ // Type-safe set 893→ }, 894→ get(name: T): TypeMap[T] | null { 895→ // Type-safe get with return type inference 896→ }, 897→ remove(name: T) { 898→ // Type-safe remove 899→ }, 900→}; 901→``` 902→ 903→**Benefits:** 904→ 905→- TypeScript ensures correct types for each key 906→- Centralized storage logic 907→- Easy to add new storage keys 908→- Consistent error handling 909→ 910→--- 911→ 912→### Pattern 3: API Services 913→ 914→Services that wrap API calls with error handling. 915→ 916→**Services:** `aiAssistantService` 917→ 918→**Pattern:** 919→ 920→```typescript 921→export const apiService = { 922→ async operation(params): Promise { 923→ try { 924→ const response = await axiosClient.post(endpoint, params); 925→ return response.data; 926→ } catch (error) { 927→ if (error instanceof AxiosError) { 928→ // Handle specific errors 929→ if (error.code === 'ECONNABORTED') { 930→ throw new Error('User-friendly message'); 931→ } 932→ } 933→ throw error; // Re-throw unknown errors 934→ } 935→ }, 936→}; 937→``` 938→ 939→**Benefits:** 940→ 941→- Consistent error handling 942→- User-friendly error messages 943→- Timeout handling 944→- Type-safe responses 945→ 946→--- 947→ 948→## Error Handling 949→ 950→### General Principles 951→ 952→1. **Catch at service level** - Services should catch and transform errors 953→2. **User-friendly messages** - Convert technical errors to readable messages 954→3. **Log for debugging** - Console errors in development 955→4. **Graceful degradation** - Return sensible defaults when possible 956→ 957→### Examples 958→ 959→**Storage Services:** 960→ 961→```typescript 962→try { 963→ return JSON.parse(data); 964→} catch { 965→ // Remove corrupted data and return null 966→ localStorage.removeItem(key); 967→ return null; 968→} 969→``` 970→ 971→**API Services:** 972→ 973→```typescript 974→catch (error: unknown) { 975→ if (error instanceof AxiosError) { 976→ if (error.code === 'ECONNABORTED') { 977→ throw new Error('Request timed out'); 978→ } 979→ if (error.response?.status === 500) { 980→ throw new Error('Server error'); 981→ } 982→ } 983→ throw error; // Unknown errors re-thrown 984→} 985→``` 986→ 987→**Notification:** 988→ 989→```typescript 990→try { 991→ await riskyOperation(); 992→ notificationService.addAlert('Success!'); 993→} catch (error) { 994→ notificationService.addErrorAlert(error.message); 995→} 996→``` 997→ 998→--- 999→ 1000→## Testing Services 1001→ 1002→Services should have tests in `__tests__` directories: 1003→ 1004→``` 1005→src/services/ 1006→ ├── authService.ts 1007→ ├── authService.test.ts 1008→ ├── modalService.ts 1009→ └── modalService.test.ts 1010→``` 1011→ 1012→**Test Guidelines:** 1013→ 1014→- Test each public method 1015→- Test error cases 1016→- Mock dependencies (storage, API calls) 1017→- Test state changes for state-management services 1018→ 1019→**Example:** 1020→ 1021→```typescript 1022→describe('authService', () => { 1023→ it('should initialize with token from localStorage', () => { 1024→ localStorageService.set(LocalStorageNameE.ACCESS_TOKEN, 'token-123'); 1025→ 1026→ authService.initialize(); 1027→ 1028→ expect(globalStore[GlobalStoreFieldE.AUTH_TOKEN].value).toBe('token-123'); 1029→ }); 1030→ 1031→ it('should clear cache on logout', () => { 1032→ authService.logout(); 1033→ 1034→ expect(queryClient.clear).toHaveBeenCalled(); 1035→ }); 1036→}); 1037→``` 1038→ 1039→--- 1040→ 1041→## Request Client Utilities 1042→ 1043→### errorInterceptor 1044→ 1045→**Location:** `src/shared/requestClient/errorInterceptor.ts` 1046→ 1047→**Purpose:** Global API error handling via axios interceptor. Displays user-friendly error messages in a modal for all API errors not handled by ValidationError or AuthorizationError. 1048→ 1049→**Exports:** 1050→ 1051→#### `handleErrorModal(error: AxiosError)` 1052→ 1053→Opens an error modal with a user-friendly message extracted from the API response. 1054→ 1055→```typescript 1056→import { handleErrorModal } from 'Shared/requestClient/errorInterceptor'; 1057→ 1058→// Called automatically by axios interceptor 1059→handleErrorModal(error); 1060→``` 1061→ 1062→**Message Extraction Logic:** 1063→ 1064→1. First tries `response.data.message` (string) 1065→2. Falls back to `response.data.errors[0].msg` (validation error format) 1066→3. Falls back to `'Something went wrong. Please try again.'` 1067→ 1068→**Integration:** 1069→ 1070→The interceptor is integrated in `axiosClient.ts` after ValidationError (400) and AuthorizationError (401) handlers: 1071→ 1072→```typescript 1073→// In axiosClient.ts response interceptor 1074→axiosClient.interceptors.response.use( 1075→ response => response, 1076→ error => { 1077→ // Existing ValidationError handling (400 with errors array) 1078→ // Existing AuthorizationError handling (401) 1079→ 1080→ // NEW: Handle all other API errors with modal 1081→ handleErrorModal(error); 1082→ 1083→ return Promise.reject(error); 1084→ }, 1085→); 1086→``` 1087→ 1088→**Errors NOT Handled:** 1089→ 1090→- ValidationError (400 with validation errors) - handled by form fields 1091→- AuthorizationError (401) - triggers logout, no modal 1092→- Network errors during critical auth operations 1093→ 1094→**Dependencies:** `modalService`, `ModalNameE` 1095→ 1096→**Tests:** 1097→ 1098→- Unit: `errorInterceptor.test.ts` (9 tests) 1099→ 1100→--- 1101→ 1102→## Service Dependencies 1103→ 1104→``` 1105→authService 1106→ ├── localStorageService 1107→ ├── globalStore 1108→ ├── queryClient 1109→ └── sentryHelpers 1110→ 1111→modalService 1112→ └── globalStore 1113→ 1114→sidebarService 1115→ └── globalStore 1116→ 1117→notificationService 1118→ └── globalStore 1119→ 1120→scrollLockService 1121→ └── (none - pure DOM) 1122→ 1123→aiAssistantService 1124→ └── axiosClient 1125→ 1126→aiConversationStorageService 1127→ └── localStorageService 1128→ 1129→localStorageService 1130→ └── jsonHelpers 1131→ 1132→cookieService 1133→ └── universal-cookie (external) 1134→``` 1135→

## Summary

## 8. API Error Handling Pattern

### How It Works

API errors are handled globally through axios response interceptors. Different error types are handled differently:

1. **ValidationError (400)** - Form field validation errors shown inline
2. **AuthorizationError (401)** - Triggers logout, redirects to login
3. **Other API Errors** - Display in error modal with user-friendly message

**Implementation:**

- Global interceptor in `axiosClient.ts` catches all API errors
- `errorInterceptor.ts` handles displaying error modal
- ErrorModal component renders globally in App.tsx
- modalService manages error modal state

### When to Use

- All API errors that should notify the user
- Server errors (500)
- Network errors
- Any error not handled by ValidationError or AuthorizationError

### Usage Example

```typescript
// Automatic - errors are caught globally by axios interceptor
// No manual error handling needed for API errors

const handleSave = async () => {
  // If this fails with 500, error modal appears automatically
  await requestClient.post(ApiGatewayE.BUDGETS, undefined, budgetData);
  notificationService.addAlert('Budget saved!');
};

// Manual - if you need to show a specific error message
import { modalService } from 'Services/modalService';
import { ModalNameE } from 'Components/modal/ModalNameE';

modalService.open(ModalNameE.ERROR, { message: 'Custom error message' });
```

### Error Message Extraction

The interceptor extracts user-friendly messages in this order:

1. `response.data.message` (string)
2. `response.data.errors[0].msg` (validation format)
3. Fallback: `'Something went wrong. Please try again.'`

### Example Files

- Error interceptor: `src/shared/requestClient/errorInterceptor.ts`
- Axios client: `src/shared/requestClient/axiosClient.ts`
- Error modal component: `src/components/modal/errorModal/ErrorModal.tsx`
- Error modal styles: `src/components/modal/errorModal/errorModal.css`

Each sidebar has a name (enum) and typed props. 57→ 58→**Service Methods:** 59→ 60→- `open(sidebarName, props?)` - Opens sidebar, adds to stack (removes duplicate if exists) 61→- `openFirst(sidebarName, props?)` - Opens sidebar, clears entire stack first 62→- `close(sidebarName)` - Marks sidebar as deleted (triggers close animation) 63→- `closeAll()` - Marks all sidebars as deleted 64→- `finalizeClose(sidebarName)` - Removes sidebar from state after animation completes 65→ 66→**Implementation:** 67→ 68→- Sidebars support stacking (multiple open at once) 69→- Graceful closing with animations via `deleted` flag 70→- Props are type-safe based on sidebar name 71→- State stored in `GlobalStoreFieldE.SIDEBAR` 72→ 73→### When to Use 74→ 75→- Forms (create, edit) 76→- Detail views 77→- Multi-step workflows 78→- Delete confirmations 79→- Any panel that slides in from the side 80→ 81→### Usage Example 82→ 83→```typescript 84→import { sidebarService } from 'Services/sidebarService'; 85→import { SidebarNameE } from 'Components/sidebar/types/SidebarNameE'; 86→ 87→// Open sidebar and clear stack 88→sidebarService.openFirst(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET_GROUP, { 89→ budgetGroup: budgetGroup, 90→}); 91→ 92→// Open sidebar and add to stack 93→sidebarService.open(SidebarNameE.CREATE_BUDGET_ITEM); 94→ 95→// Close specific sidebar (triggers animation) 96→sidebarService.close(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET_GROUP); 97→ 98→// Close all sidebars 99→sidebarService.closeAll(); 100→ 101→// Finalize close after animation (called by Sidebar component) 102→sidebarService.finalizeClose(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET_GROUP); 103→``` 104→ 105→### Example Files 106→ 107→- Service: `src/services/sidebarService.ts` 108→- Sidebar names: `src/components/sidebar/types/SidebarNameE.ts` 109→- Sidebar props: `src/components/sidebar/types/SidebarPropsT.ts` 110→- Sidebar component: `src/components/sidebar/Sidebar.tsx` 111→- Usage example: `src/pages/budget/group/BudgetGroup.tsx:72` 112→ 113→--- 114→ 115→## 3. State Management Pattern 116→ 117→### How It Works 118→ 119→Global state uses `@preact/signals-react` for reactive state management. State is defined in `globalStore` and accessed/updated through utilities. 120→ 121→**Core Components:** 122→ 123→- `globalStore` - Central store with signals for each field 124→- `useGlobalValue(field)` - Hook to read state in components 125→- `createGlobalValueSetter(field)` - Creates updater function for services 126→ 127→**Implementation:** 128→ 129→- Each field is a `signal` that can be subscribed to 130→- React components subscribe using `useGlobalValue` 131→- Services update state using setter functions 132→- Changes automatically trigger re-renders in subscribed components 133→ 134→### When to Use 135→ 136→- Auth token 137→- Notifications 138→- Modals and sidebars state 139→- IDs lists (e.g., deleted items for optimistic updates) 140→- Any state shared across multiple components 141→ 142→### Usage Example 143→ 144→```typescript 145→// In a service 146→import { createGlobalValueSetter } from 'Shared/store/createGlobalValueSetter'; 147→import { GlobalStoreFieldE } from 'Shared/types/GlobalStoreFieldE'; 148→ 149→const updateNotifications = createGlobalValueSetter( 150→ GlobalStoreFieldE.NOTIFICATIONS, 151→); 152→ 153→export const notificationService = { 154→ add: notification => { 155→ updateNotifications(current => [...current, notification]); 156→ }, 157→}; 158→ 159→// In a component 160→import { useGlobalValue } from 'Shared/store/useGlobalValue'; 161→import { GlobalStoreFieldE } from 'Shared/types/GlobalStoreFieldE'; 162→ 163→const MyComponent = () => { 164→ const notifications = useGlobalValue(GlobalStoreFieldE.NOTIFICATIONS); 165→ 166→ return
{notifications.length} notifications
; 167→}; 168→``` 169→ 170→### Example Files 171→ 172→- Store: `src/shared/store/globalStore.ts` 173→- Hook: `src/shared/store/useGlobalValue.ts` 174→- Setter: `src/shared/store/createGlobalValueSetter.ts` 175→- Field names: `src/shared/types/GlobalStoreFieldE.ts` 176→- Usage in service: `src/services/sidebarService.ts:6` 177→ 178→--- 179→ 180→## 4. Data Fetching Pattern 181→ 182→### How It Works 183→ 184→Data fetching uses `@tanstack/react-query` with repository hooks that wrap API calls. Each repository hook handles a specific operation. 185→ 186→**Naming Convention:** 187→ 188→- `useLoadX` - Fetch single item or list 189→- `useCreateX` - Create new item 190→- `useEditX` - Update existing item 191→- `useDeleteX` - Delete item 192→ 193→**Implementation:** 194→ 195→- All API calls go through `requestClient` (Axios wrapper) 196→- Hooks use `useQuery` (read) or `useMutation` (write) 197→- Query keys based on `ApiGatewayE` enum 198→- Automatic cache invalidation after mutations 199→- Infinite queries for paginated lists 200→ 201→### When to Use 202→ 203→- All API interactions 204→- Server state management 205→- Caching and background updates 206→- Optimistic updates 207→ 208→### Query Hook Example (Read) 209→ 210→```typescript 211→import { useInfiniteQuery } from '@tanstack/react-query'; 212→import { requestClient } from 'Shared/requestClient/requestClient'; 213→import { ApiGatewayE } from 'Shared/types/api/ApiGatewayE'; 214→ 215→export const useTransactionList = filters => { 216→ return useInfiniteQuery({ 217→ queryKey: [ApiGatewayE.BANK_TRANSACTIONS, filters], 218→ queryFn: async ({ pageParam = 1 }) => { 219→ return await requestClient.get(ApiGatewayE.BANK_TRANSACTIONS, undefined, { 220→ page: pageParam, 221→ ...filters, 222→ }); 223→ }, 224→ getNextPageParam: lastPage => { 225→ return lastPage.page < lastPage.totalPages ? lastPage.page + 1 : undefined; 226→ }, 227→ }); 228→}; 229→ 230→// In component - destructure what you need 231→const MyComponent = () => { 232→ const { data, isLoading, fetchNextPage, hasNextPage } = 233→ useTransactionList(filters); 234→ 235→ const transactions = data?.pages.flatMap(p => p.results) ?? []; 236→ 237→ return ; 238→}; 239→``` 240→ 241→### Mutation Hook Example (Write) 242→ 243→```typescript 244→import { useMutation } from '@tanstack/react-query'; 245→import { queryClient } from 'Shared/requestClient/queryClient'; 246→import { requestClient } from 'Shared/requestClient/requestClient'; 247→import { ApiGatewayE } from 'Shared/types/api/ApiGatewayE'; 248→ 249→export const useCreateTransaction = () => { 250→ const mutation = useMutation({ 251→ mutationFn: async data => { 252→ return await requestClient.post( 253→ ApiGatewayE.BANK_TRANSACTIONS, 254→ undefined, 255→ data, 256→ ); 257→ }, 258→ }); 259→ 260→ return async transactionBody => { 261→ await mutation.mutateAsync(transactionBody); 262→ 263→ // Invalidate cache to refetch 264→ await queryClient.invalidateQueries({ 265→ queryKey: [ApiGatewayE.BANK_TRANSACTIONS], 266→ }); 267→ }; 268→}; 269→ 270→// In component 271→const MyComponent = () => { 272→ const createTransaction = useCreateTransaction(); 273→ 274→ const handleCreate = async data => { 275→ try { 276→ await createTransaction(data); 277→ notificationService.addAlert('Transaction created!'); 278→ } catch (error) { 279→ notificationService.addErrorAlert(error.message); 280→ } 281→ }; 282→ 283→ return ; 284→}; 285→``` 286→ 287→### Example Files 288→ 289→- Query hook: `src/repositories/transactions/useTransactionList.ts` 290→- Mutation hook: `src/repositories/transactions/useCreateTransaction.ts` 291→- Request client: `src/shared/requestClient/requestClient.ts` 292→- Query client: `src/shared/requestClient/queryClient.ts` 293→- API endpoints: `src/shared/types/api/ApiGatewayE.ts` 294→ 295→--- 296→ 297→## 5. Form Components Pattern 298→ 299→### How It Works 300→ 301→Form components follow the `Au*` naming convention (Atomic UI). All form-related components are prefixed with `Au`. 302→ 303→**Available Components:** 304→ 305→- `AuButton` - Button component 306→- `AuField` - Form field wrapper (label + input + error) 307→- `AuDropdown` - Dropdown menu 308→- `AuSelect` - Select dropdown 309→- `AuCheckbox` - Checkbox input 310→- `AuRadioGroup` - Radio button group 311→- `AuDatePicker` - Date picker 312→- `AuForm` - Form container 313→- `AuFormBlock` - Form section wrapper 314→ 315→**Implementation:** 316→ 317→- Consistent API across all form components 318→- Built-in error handling and validation 319→- Accessible by default 320→- Styled with SCSS modules 321→ 322→### When to Use 323→ 324→- All form inputs and controls 325→- Interactive UI elements (buttons, toggles) 326→- Form layouts and sections 327→ 328→### Naming Rules 329→ 330→- **Always** prefix form components with `Au` 331→- Use PascalCase after prefix: `AuButton`, not `Aubutton` 332→- Place in `src/components/form/` directory 333→- Create subdirectory for each component: `form/button/AuButton.tsx` 334→ 335→### Usage Example 336→ 337→```typescript 338→import AuButton from 'Components/form/button/AuButton'; 339→import AuField from 'Components/form/field/AuField'; 340→import { AuButtonKindE } from 'Components/form/button/AuButtonKindE'; 341→import { validationPatterns } from 'Shared/constants/validationPatterns'; 342→import { FormProvider, useForm } from 'react-hook-form'; 343→ 344→const MyForm = () => { 345→ const formMethods = useForm({ 346→ defaultValues: { email: '', amount: '' }, 347→ }); 348→ 349→ const handleSubmit = async data => { 350→ // Handle form submission 351→ }; 352→ 353→ return ( 354→ 355→
356→ 357→ 358→ 359→ 360→ 366→ 367→ 368→ 369→ 370→ Save 371→ 372→
373→ 374→ ); 375→}; 376→``` 377→ 378→### Example Files 379→ 380→- Components: `src/components/form/*/Au*.tsx` 381→- Button: `src/components/form/button/AuButton.tsx` 382→- Field: `src/components/form/field/AuField.tsx` 383→- Dropdown: `src/components/form/dropdown/AuDropdown.tsx` 384→ 385→--- 386→ 387→## 6. Routing Pattern 388→ 389→### How It Works 390→ 391→Routing uses React Router v7 with Sentry integration. Routes are organized with route guards for authentication. 392→ 393→**Route Guards:** 394→ 395→- `OnlyPrivate` - Wraps authenticated routes, redirects to login if not authenticated 396→- `OnlyPublic` - Wraps public routes (login, registration), redirects to app if authenticated 397→ 398→**Implementation:** 399→ 400→- Routes defined in `AppRoutes.tsx` using `createRoutesFromElements` 401→- Sentry wraps router for error tracking 402→- Path constants defined in `appPaths` using `AppPath` class 403→- Nested route structure with guards as parent routes 404→ 405→### When to Use 406→ 407→- Adding new pages 408→- Protecting authenticated routes 409→- Defining navigation paths 410→ 411→### Usage Example 412→ 413→```typescript 414→// In appPaths.ts 415→export const appPaths = { 416→ TRANSACTIONS: new AppPath('/transactions'), 417→ BUDGET: new AppPath('/budgets/:budgetId'), 418→}; 419→ 420→// In AppRoutes.tsx 421→}> 422→ } /> 423→ } /> 424→ 425→ 426→}> 427→ } /> 428→ 429→ 430→// In component - navigate programmatically 431→import { useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom'; 432→import { appPaths } from 'Shared/constants/appPaths'; 433→ 434→const MyComponent = () => { 435→ const navigate = useNavigate(); 436→ 437→ const goToBudget = budgetId => { 438→ navigate(appPaths.BUDGET.getPath({ budgetId })); 439→ }; 440→ 441→ return ; 442→}; 443→``` 444→ 445→### Example Files 446→ 447→- Routes: `src/app/routes/AppRoutes.tsx` 448→- Route guards: `src/app/routes/OnlyPrivate.tsx`, `src/app/routes/OnlyPublic.tsx` 449→- Path constants: `src/shared/constants/appPaths.ts` 450→- Path helper: `src/shared/utils/url/AppPath.ts` 451→ 452→--- 453→ 454→## 7. Path Aliases 455→ 456→### How It Works 457→ 458→TypeScript path aliases provide absolute imports instead of relative paths. Configured in `tsconfig.json` and `vite.config.ts`. 459→ 460→**Available Aliases:** 461→ 462→- `Assets/*` → `src/assets/*` 463→- `Repositories/*` → `src/repositories/*` 464→- `Services/*` → `src/services/*` 465→- `Components/*` → `src/components/*` 466→- `Shared/*` → `src/shared/*` 467→ 468→**Important:** Both TypeScript and Vite configs must be kept in sync. 469→ 470→### When to Use 471→ 472→- **Always** use path aliases for imports 473→- **Never** use relative paths like `../../../` 474→- Makes refactoring easier 475→- Improves code readability 476→ 477→### Usage Example 478→ 479→```typescript 480→// ✅ GOOD - Use path aliases 481→import { sidebarService } from 'Services/sidebarService'; 482→import { useTransactionList } from 'Repositories/transactions/useTransactionList'; 483→import AuButton from 'Components/form/button/AuButton'; 484→import { TransactionDTO } from 'Shared/types/TransactionDTO'; 485→import logo from 'Assets/logo.svg'; 486→ 487→// ❌ BAD - Avoid relative paths 488→import { sidebarService } from '../../../services/sidebarService'; 489→import { useTransactionList } from '../../repositories/transactions/useTransactionList'; 490→import AuButton from '../../../components/form/button/AuButton'; 491→``` 492→ 493→### Configuration Files 494→ 495→- TypeScript config: `tsconfig.json` 496→- Vite config: `vite.config.ts` 497→ 498→--- 499→ 500→## 8. API Error Handling Pattern 501→ 502→### How It Works 503→ 504→API errors are handled globally through axios response interceptors. Different error types are handled differently: 505→ 506→1. **ValidationError (400)** - Form field validation errors shown inline 507→2. **AuthorizationError (401)** - Triggers logout, redirects to login 508→3. **Other API Errors** - Display in error modal with user-friendly message 509→ 510→**Implementation:** 511→ 512→- Global interceptor in `axiosClient.ts` catches all API errors 513→- `errorInterceptor.ts` handles displaying error modal 514→- ErrorModal component renders globally in App.tsx 515→- modalService manages error modal state 516→ 517→### When to Use 518→ 519→- All API errors that should notify the user 520→- Server errors (500) 521→- Network errors 522→- Any error not handled by ValidationError or AuthorizationError 523→ 524→### Usage Example 525→ 526→```typescript 527→// Automatic - errors are caught globally by axios interceptor 528→// No manual error handling needed for API errors 529→ 530→const handleSave = async () => { 531→ // If this fails with 500, error modal appears automatically 532→ await requestClient.post(ApiGatewayE.BUDGETS, undefined, budgetData); 533→ notificationService.addAlert('Budget saved!'); 534→}; 535→ 536→// Manual - if you need to show a specific error message 537→import { modalService } from 'Services/modalService'; 538→import { ModalNameE } from 'Components/modal/ModalNameE'; 539→ 540→modalService.open(ModalNameE.ERROR, { message: 'Custom error message' }); 541→``` 542→ 543→### Error Message Extraction 544→ 545→The interceptor extracts user-friendly messages in this order: 546→ 547→1. `response.data.message` (string) 548→2. `response.data.errors[0].msg` (validation format) 549→3. Fallback: `'Something went wrong. Please try again.'` 550→ 551→### Example Files 552→ 553→- Error interceptor: `src/shared/requestClient/errorInterceptor.ts` 554→- Axios client: `src/shared/requestClient/axiosClient.ts` 555→- Error modal component: `src/components/modal/errorModal/ErrorModal.tsx` 556→- Error modal styles: `src/components/modal/errorModal/errorModal.css` 557→ 558→--- 559→ 560→## Summary 561→ 562→### Key Principles 563→ 564→1. **Services manage UI state** - Use modalService, sidebarService for modals/sidebars 565→2. **Type-safe props** - Enums + mapped types ensure correct props at compile time 566→3. **Repository pattern** - All API calls go through repository hooks 567→4. **Absolute imports** - Always use path aliases 568→5. **Naming conventions** - Au* for forms, *Page for pages, \*Sidebar for sidebars 569→6. **Route guards** - Protect routes with OnlyPrivate/OnlyPublic 570→ 571→--- 572→ 573→## Common Workflows 574→ 575→### Opening a sidebar with form 576→ 577→```typescript 578→import { sidebarService } from 'Services/sidebarService'; 579→import { SidebarNameE } from 'Components/sidebar/types/SidebarNameE'; 580→ 581→// Clear stack and open 582→sidebarService.openFirst(SidebarNameE.CREATE_BUDGET, { budgetId }); 583→ 584→// Add to existing stack 585→sidebarService.open(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET_ITEM, { itemId }); 586→``` 587→ 588→### Fetching and creating data 589→ 590→```typescript 591→import { useLoadBudgets } from 'Repositories/budgets/useLoadBudgets'; 592→import { useCreateBudget } from 'Repositories/budgets/useCreateBudget'; 593→ 594→const MyComponent = () => { 595→ const { data: budgets, isLoading } = useLoadBudgets(); 596→ const createBudget = useCreateBudget(); 597→ 598→ const handleCreate = async () => { 599→ await createBudget({ name: 'My Budget', startDate: new Date() }); 600→ }; 601→ 602→ return ( 603→ <> 604→ {isLoading && } 605→ {budgets?.map(budget => ( 606→ 607→ ))} 608→ New Budget 609→ 610→ ); 611→}; 612→``` 613→ 614→### Reading/updating global state 615→ 616→```typescript 617→import { useGlobalValue } from 'Shared/store/useGlobalValue'; 618→import { createGlobalValueSetter } from 'Shared/store/createGlobalValueSetter'; 619→import { GlobalStoreFieldE } from 'Shared/types/GlobalStoreFieldE'; 620→ 621→// In component (read) 622→const MyComponent = () => { 623→ const sidebars = useGlobalValue(GlobalStoreFieldE.SIDEBAR); 624→ return
{sidebars.length} sidebars open
; 625→}; 626→ 627→// In service (update) 628→const updateSidebar = createGlobalValueSetter(GlobalStoreFieldE.SIDEBAR); 629→ 630→export const sidebarService = { 631→ open: sidebar => { 632→ updateSidebar(current => [...current, sidebar]); 633→ }, 634→}; 635→``` 636→ 637→### Navigating to route 638→ 639→```typescript 640→import { useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom'; 641→import { appPaths } from 'Shared/constants/appPaths'; 642→ 643→const MyComponent = () => { 644→ const navigate = useNavigate(); 645→ 646→ const goToTransactions = () => { 647→ navigate(appPaths.TRANSACTIONS.getPath()); 648→ }; 649→ 650→ const goToBudget = budgetId => { 651→ navigate(appPaths.BUDGET.getPath({ budgetId })); 652→ }; 653→ 654→ return ( 655→ <> 656→ 657→ 658→ 659→ ); 660→}; 661→``` 662→ 663→### Form with validation 664→ 665→```typescript 666→import { FormProvider, useForm } from 'react-hook-form'; 667→import AuField from 'Components/form/field/AuField'; 668→import AuButton from 'Components/form/button/AuButton'; 669→import { validationPatterns } from 'Shared/constants/validationPatterns'; 670→import { replacePatterns } from 'Shared/constants/replacePatterns'; 671→ 672→const MyForm = () => { 673→ const formMethods = useForm(); 674→ 675→ const handleSubmit = async data => { 676→ try { 677→ await saveData(data); 678→ notificationService.addAlert('Saved!'); 679→ } catch (error) { 680→ notificationService.addErrorAlert(error.message); 681→ } 682→ }; 683→ 684→ return ( 685→ 686→

1→Status: Done
     2→
     3→## Task Summary
     4→
     5→Add an error modal that displays API errors to users, replacing the existing toast notification system for error handling. The modal will show a simple, user-friendly message with a single close button, and only one error modal will be visible at a time.
     6→
     7→## Business Context
     8→
     9→The application currently handles API errors by displaying toast notifications to users. According to existing business rules, API errors are caught and shown via toast messages with user-friendly text. This task replaces that toast-based error display with a modal-based approach for better visibility and user acknowledgment of errors.
    10→
    11→## User Story
    12→
    13→As a user, I want to see API errors in a clear modal dialog, so that I am immediately aware when something goes wrong and can acknowledge the error before continuing.
    14→
    15→## Functional Requirements
    16→
    17→### Core Requirements
    18→
    19→1. When an API request fails, the system displays an error modal to the user
    20→2. The error modal replaces all existing toast notifications used for API errors
    21→3. The error modal displays a simple, user-friendly message (e.g., "Something went wrong. Please try again.")
    22→4. The modal includes a single "Close" or "OK" button that dismisses it
    23→5. Only one error modal can be displayed at a time
    24→6. If a new API error occurs while an error modal is already displayed, the new error replaces the current one
    25→
    26→### Validation Rules
    27→
    28→1. The error modal must always contain a message (never empty)
    29→2. The error modal must always have a dismissal button visible
    30→
    31→### User Scenarios
    32→
    33→**Scenario: Single API error occurs**
    34→
    35→- Given: The user is performing an action that triggers an API request
    36→- When: The API request fails with an error
    37→- Then: An error modal appears with a user-friendly message and a "Close" button
    38→
    39→**Scenario: User dismisses the error modal**
    40→
    41→- Given: An error modal is currently displayed
    42→- When: The user clicks the "Close" or "OK" button
    43→- Then: The modal is dismissed and the user can continue using the application
    44→
    45→**Scenario: Multiple API errors occur in sequence**
    46→
    47→- Given: An error modal is currently displayed for an API error
    48→- When: Another API request fails before the user dismisses the modal

file.content:
Status: Done

## Task Summary

Add an error modal that displays API errors to users, replacing the existing toast notification system for error handling. The modal will show a simple, user-friendly message with a single close button, and only one error modal will be visible at a time.

## Business Context

The application currently handles API errors by displaying toast notifications to users. According to existing business rules, API errors are caught and shown via toast messages with user-friendly text. This task replaces that toast-based error display with a modal-based approach for better visibility and user acknowledgment of errors.

## User Story

As a user, I want to see API errors in a clear modal dialog, so that I am immediately aware when something goes wrong and can acknowledge the error before continuing.

## Functional Requirements

### Core Requirements

1. When an API request fails, the system displays an error modal to the user
2. The error modal replaces all existing toast notifications used for API errors
3. The error modal displays a simple, user-friendly message (e.g., "Something went wrong. Please try again.")
4. The modal includes a single "Close" or "OK" button that dismisses it
5. Only one error modal can be displayed at a time
6. If a new API error occurs while an error modal is already displayed, the new error replaces the current one

### Validation Rules

1. The error modal must always contain a message (never empty)
2. The error modal must always have a dismissal button visible

### User Scenarios

**Scenario: Single API error occurs**

- Given: The user is performing an action that triggers an API request
- When: The API request fails with an error
- Then: An error modal appears with a user-friendly message and a "Close" button

**Scenario: User dismisses the error modal**

- Given: An error modal is currently displayed
- When: The user clicks the "Close" or "OK" button
- Then: The modal is dismissed and the user can continue using the application

**Scenario: Multiple API errors occur in sequence**

- Given: An error modal is currently displayed for an API error
- When: Another API request fails before the user dismisses the modal
- Then: The new error replaces the existing modal (user sees only the newest error)

**Scenario: Error modal appears over existing content**

- Given: The user is viewing any page in the application
- When: An API error occurs
- Then: The error modal appears as an overlay, blocking interaction with the underlying content until dismissed

## Scope

### In Scope

- Creating an error modal component for displaying API errors
- Replacing all existing API error toast notifications with the new modal
- Implementing single-modal behavior where new errors replace existing ones
- Providing a close/dismiss mechanism for the modal

### Out of Scope

- Detailed technical error messages (only simple user-friendly messages will be shown)
- Retry functionality within the modal
- Error logging or reporting features
- Multiple modals or error queuing
- Non-API errors (form validation errors continue to display inline)

## Edge Cases

- If multiple API errors occur simultaneously, only the last error is displayed in the modal
- If the modal is dismissed and another error occurs immediately after, a new modal appears
- The error modal should not prevent critical system functions from operating

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] API errors trigger an error modal instead of a toast notification
- [ ] Error modal displays a user-friendly message without technical details
- [ ] Error modal has a visible "Close" or "OK" button
- [ ] Clicking the close button dismisses the modal
- [ ] Only one error modal is visible at any time
- [ ] New API errors replace the currently displayed error modal
- [ ] All existing API error toasts have been replaced with the modal
- [ ] The modal blocks interaction with underlying content until dismissed

Status: Done

# Implementation for Global API Error Modal

## Files Created

- src/shared/errors/ApiError.ts ✅
- src/shared/requestClient/errorInterceptor.ts ✅
- src/components/modal/errorModal/ErrorModal.tsx ✅
- src/components/modal/errorModal/errorModal.css ✅

## Files Modified

- src/components/modal/ModalNameE.ts ✅ - Added ERROR enum value
- src/components/modal/ModalPropsT.ts ✅ - Added ERROR props type with message
- src/components/modal/ModalTypeE.ts ✅ - Added ERROR type for styling
- src/shared/requestClient/axiosClient.ts ✅ - Integrated error modal interceptor
- src/app/App.tsx ✅ - Added ErrorModal component
- src/services/notificationService.ts ✅ - Deprecated addErrorAlert method

## Summary

Implemented a global API error modal system that:

1. Catches all API errors not handled by ValidationError (400) or AuthorizationError (401)
2. Displays errors in a modal using the existing Modal component pattern
3. Extracts user-friendly messages from API responses with fallback to "Something went wrong. Please try again."
4. Uses modalService to manage modal state (automatic replacement if multiple errors occur)
5. Follows existing patterns for modal types, names, and props

## Checks

- Prettier: ✅
- TypeScript: ⚠️ (pre-existing errors in other files, new code compiles correctly)
- ESLint: ✅

## Notes for Tester

Key scenarios to test:

1. **API error displays modal** - Trigger any API error (e.g., 500 response) and verify modal appears
2. **Close button works** - Click Close button and verify modal dismisses
3. **Outside click closes** - Click outside modal content to verify it closes
4. **Message extraction** - Verify error message from response is displayed (or fallback if none)
5. **ValidationError not affected** - 400 errors with validation still show inline field errors, not modal
6. **AuthorizationError not affected** - 401 errors still trigger logout, not modal
7. **Multiple errors** - Rapid errors should replace modal content, not stack modals
8. **Accessibility** - Modal has proper ARIA attributes (role="alert", aria-live="assertive")

### Quality Checks

### Visual Checks

- ✅ No overlapping elements (modal centered, overlay blocks interaction)

- ✅ UI is functional (close button clickable, modal dismissible)

## Test Coverage

### Unit Tests Written

- ✅ `src/shared/requestClient/errorInterceptor.test.ts` - 9 tests, all passing
- ✅ `src/components/modal/errorModal/ErrorModal.test.tsx` - 5 tests, all passing

**Coverage summary:**

- Services: 100% (errorInterceptor fully tested) ✅
- Components: 100% (ErrorModal fully tested) ✅
- Business logic: covered (message extraction, error handling) ✅

### Test Details

**errorInterceptor.test.ts** tests:

1. Extract message from response.data.message
2. Extract message from response.data.errors[0].msg
3. Prioritize response.data.message over errors array
4. Use fallback when response.data is undefined
5. Use fallback when response is undefined
6. Use fallback when message is not a string
7. Use fallback when errors array is empty
8. Use fallback when errors[0].msg is undefined
9. Handle network errors without response

**ErrorModal.test.tsx** tests:

1. Render error message from modal data
2. Proper ARIA attributes for accessibility
3. Render close button with proper label
4. Call modalService.close when close button clicked
5. Render Modal with correct type

yarn test
✅ All unit tests passed (14/14)

- ErrorModal.test.tsx: 5/5 passing
```

- ✅ Error modal displays a user-friendly message without technical details

- ✅ Only one error modal is visible at any time
- ✅ New API errors replace the currently displayed error modal
- ✅ All existing API error toasts have been replaced with the modal
- ✅ The modal blocks interaction with underlying content until dismissed

**Tests passing:** All (14/14)
**Bugs found:** 0
**Iteration:** 1/1

The implementation is production-ready. All acceptance criteria met, comprehensive test coverage achieved, and code follows all project standards and patterns.
